VCVSsSetup sensitivity setup; assigns here->VCVSsenParmNo = ++(info->SENparms) into a SENstruct. Sensitivity not implemented (analysis-scope.md). PLANNING.md §5 case (b).

Automake source-list fragment (libvsrc_la_SOURCES). C build system: digiTS has no autotools build; components register via src/core/registry.ts. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5. The single hunk only deletes the vsrcdest.c and vsrcmdel.c entries — both themselves NO-COUNTERPART C-heap-free artifacts deleted in v41. No behavioral content.

IFparm VSRCpTable parameter-name table. digiTS keys params by name string (defineModelParams in dc-voltage-source.ts / ac-voltage-source.ts / variable-rail.ts); no integer-id IFparm table, no parser-driven generic ask/set, no IF_REDIRECT flag. Two hunks: h001 swaps the `sine`/`sin` alias rows so `sin` becomes canonical and `sine` becomes IOPR (IF_REDIRECT alias) — a parser-table redirect-flag edit; h002 changes the `external` param type IOP(...,IF_REALVEC) → IOP(...,IF_STRING) under #ifdef SHARED_MODULE and adds the RFSPICE port-parameter rows (portnum/z0/pwr/freq/phase) under #ifdef RFSPICE. Both hunks are parser-table plumbing; RFSPICE port params belong to S-parameter/RF analysis (not in analysis-scope.md). Resolved per hunk (both NO-COUNTERPART).

VSRCaccept — the transient breakpoint-table pass. digiTS implements transient with a breakpoint controller (analysis-scope.md); the counterpart is AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl.acceptStep / nextBreakpoint (ac-voltage-source.ts:831-985). BUT the entire v41 rewrite targets ngspice's VSRCcoeffs[] / VSRCfunctionType / VSRCbreak_time waveform model, which digiTS does not carry (grep across src/ for VSRCcoeffs/functionType/VSRCbreak/newcompat/compatmode returns zero hits — digiTS reimplements waveforms as its own abstraction sine/square/triangle/sawtooth/expression/sweep/am/fm/noise). Ten hunks examined per hunk: the accessor-rename and C-plumbing hunks resolve directly; the PULSE/PWL/TRNOISE/TRRANDOM breakpoint-rewrite hunks target the absent VSRCcoeffs/break_time model and are blocked by reconstruction item vsrc#recon/waveformModel (Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM).

VSRCacLoad — AC small-signal load. AC is implemented engine-wide (analysis-scope.md: ac-analysis.ts, defaultStampAcHandler, optional AnalogElement.stampAc hook). digiTS already has stampAc on both AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl (ac-voltage-source.ts:723-746) and DcVoltageSourceAnalogElement (dc-voltage-source.ts:227-248). The single diff hunk mixes the new RFSPICE S-parameter port-stamp code (VSRCspinit/VSRCspupdate/VSRCgetActivePorts + the #ifdef RFSPICE branch of VSRCacLoad) with the plain #else AC-load path that adds the MODEACNOISE noise-input gate and renames the four matrix pointers ptr→Ptr. The RFSPICE additions belong to S-parameter analysis (not in analysis-scope.md); the #else path is the portable AC stamp. The hunk is split so the RFSPICE block is NO-COUNTERPART and the #else AC-load path is PENDING.

VSRCask — IFparm value getter for query parameters (current, power, port params). digiTS has no integer-id ask API; instance readouts are class members (getPinCurrents returns branch currents; there is no `power` query method on any voltage-source element — grep for VSRC_POWER/power query returns only the draw-label `power` of unrelated components). Two hunks: h001 adds a FREE(errMsg) leak fix + a CKTrhsOld-null guard on VSRC_CURRENT and fixes the VSRC_POWER sign (drops the leading unary minus so power = (Vpos-Vneg)*Ibranch); h002 adds the RFSPICE port-query cases (PORTNUM/Z0/PWR/FREQ/PHASE) and the SHARED_MODULE EXTERNAL no-op case. The C ask-getter has no digiTS counterpart (no integer-id ask path, no power-query method); resolved per hunk (both NO-COUNTERPART).

Instance/model structs + param-id constants. The digiTS counterpart is the instance-field set on the voltage-source element classes (dc-voltage-source.ts / ac-voltage-source.ts / variable-rail.ts) plus their model-param defs. Three hunks: h001 embeds `struct GENinstance gen` with accessor macros, marks node-id fields const, adds the RFSPICE port instance fields/pointers, and adds the portable new `double VSRCbreak_time` field (most-recent-breakpoint time — part of the v41 breakpoint-time waveform model digiTS lacks, blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel); h002 renames the five matrix-pointer fields ptr→Ptr (cosmetic; digiTS holds opaque solver handles); h003 reformats the function-type and device-parameter #defines into enums (byte-identical values), adds the Given-flags bitfields, the RFSPICE port fields, and the `struct GENmodel gen` embedding. Examined per hunk (h001 PENDING/blocked, h002/h003 NO-COUNTERPART).

VSRCdelete. v41 rewrites it from a linked-list unlink (FREE(here)) into a per-instance C-heap free of VSRCcoeffs / trnoise_state / trrandom_state. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS is GC-managed; no allocator, no destructor, no instance linked list, and no VSRCcoeffs/trnoise_state/trrandom_state heap blocks (those are part of the ngspice waveform model digiTS does not carry). No behavioral content.

VSRCdestroy — a C-heap free loop releasing every VSRCmodel and VSRCinstance. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. The whole file is deleted in v41.

C extern-prototype header. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS has no prototype headers — signatures are TypeScript class members. The single hunk changes the VSRCdelete prototype signature, removes the VSRCdestroy/VSRCmDelete prototypes (the deleted C-heap functions), and adds the RFSPICE VSRCspupdate/VSRCgetActivePorts/VSRCspinit prototypes (S-parameter analysis, not in analysis-scope.md). No surviving declaration maps to a digiTS construct.

VSRCfindBr — branch-equation lookup/allocation by instance name. digiTS implements this as findBranchFor on every voltage-source element (dc-voltage-source.ts:197-205, ac-voltage-source.ts:657-665), calling ctx.makeCur when branchIndex is unallocated — the direct counterpart of CKTmkCur. The single hunk is solely the model/instance linked-list-walk accessor rename (model->VSRCnextModel → VSRCnextModel(model), here->VSRCnextInstance → VSRCnextInstance(here)); arithmetic and control flow unchanged.

SPICEdev VSRCinfo registration dispatch struct, converted to C99 designated initialisers. digiTS registration is the DcVoltageSourceDefinition / AcVoltageSourceDefinition / VariableRailDefinition objects; method binding is the element classes. Reviewed per the PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note: the single hunk is the entire-struct reformat. Every surviving function-pointer assignment that was non-NULL in v26 stays non-NULL and points to the same handler; the only non-cosmetic changes are DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete set to NULL (the deleted heap functions). No new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

VSRCload — the transient/DC companion load + waveform evaluation. digiTS implements DC-OP and transient (analysis-scope.md); the matrix-stamp + RHS-write counterpart is the load() method on every voltage-source element (dc-voltage-source.ts:211-225, ac-voltage-source.ts:685-721, variable-rail.ts), already citing vsrcload.c line-for-line. BUT the waveform-evaluation switch (PULSE/SINE/EXP/SFFM/AM/PWL/TRNOISE) is rewritten against ngspice's VSRCcoeffs[]/VSRCfunctionType model that digiTS does not carry (digiTS evaluates its own waveform abstraction in computeWaveformValue, ac-voltage-source.ts:101-256). Fourteen hunks examined per hunk: the matrix-stamp accessor/connectivity hunks resolve directly (PORT); the per-waveform value hunks (PULSE/SINE/SFFM/AM/PWL/TRNOISE rewrites, newcompat.xs phase handling) target the absent VSRCcoeffs model and are blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel (Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM); the RFSPICE PORT case is S-parameter (NO-COUNTERPART).

VSRCmDelete — a C-heap free that unlinks a VSRCmodel and frees its instances. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS is GC-managed; no model destructor, no model linked list. The whole file is deleted in v41.

VSRCparam — instance parameter application. The counterpart is setParam / the constructor on the voltage-source elements (ac-voltage-source.ts:667-683, dc-voltage-source.ts:207-209). Nine hunks: h001/h005/h006 are cosmetic `switch(` → `switch (` whitespace + FALLTHROUGH comments; h002 adds GCC FALLTHROUGH comments to the VSRC_AC numValue switch; h003/h004 add the new pwl `r` repeat-coefficient validation (r<-0.5 disables repeat; buggy-input guard; r>=end_time fix) — part of the VSRCcoeffs PWL model digiTS lacks; h007/h008 add trnoise_state_free / tfree-before-reinit for `alter`-command state re-write (C-heap of the trnoise/trrandom state blocks digiTS does not carry); h009 adds the RFSPICE port-parameter cases. Resolved per hunk: whitespace/FALLTHROUGH hunks NO-COUNTERPART (C parser cosmetics with no digiTS counterpart), pwl-r hunks blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel, trnoise/trrandom-free hunks NO-COUNTERPART (C-heap), RFSPICE NO-COUNTERPART.

VSRCpzLoad — pole-zero load. Case (b) whole non-implemented SPICE analysis per PLANNING.md §5: analysis-scope.md (ratified 2026-05-17) ratifies PZ as not implemented in digiTS (no PZ method on the AnalogEngine interface, no PZ engine file under src/solver/analog/, no PZ MCP tool). The whole file belongs to PZ analysis; the single hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename plus the matrix-pointer ptr→Ptr renames inside VSRCpzLoad.

VSRCpzSetup — pole-zero setup (TSTALLOC of the branch matrix cells for the PZ pass). Case (b) whole non-implemented SPICE analysis per PLANNING.md §5: analysis-scope.md ratifies PZ as not implemented in digiTS. Both hunks (h001 accessor rename; h002 the five TSTALLOC ptr→Ptr renames) live wholly inside VSRCpzSetup, which exists only to allocate matrix cells for the PZ load pass.

VSRCsetup + VSRCunsetup — branch-equation allocation, shorted-source check, and the four/five TSTALLOC matrix-cell allocations. digiTS implements this as setup() on every voltage-source element (dc-voltage-source.ts:180-195 / ac-voltage-source.ts:640-655 / variable-rail.ts), with the idempotent makeCur branch allocation and the four allocElement calls in vsrcset.c TSTALLOC order. Three hunks: h001 adds the new `here->VSRCbreak_time = -1.0` initial-breakpoint seed (part of the v41 breakpoint-time waveform model digiTS lacks — blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel) plus the model/instance accessor rename; h002 adds the RFSPICE port-mode TSTALLOC branch (S-parameter) and renames the four non-RFSPICE TSTALLOC pointers ptr→Ptr (the portable four-cell allocation); h003 rewrites VSRCunsetup branch-equation cleanup (C-side dispatch glue — branch deallocation) with the RFSPICE resNode cleanup. Resolved per hunk.

VSRCtemp — the per-instance AC-magnitude/phase derivation (VSRCacReal/VSRCacImag = VSRCacMag·cos/sin(radians)) plus the DC/transient value-presence warnings. digiTS implements DC-OP/transient/AC (analysis-scope.md); the VSRCacReal/VSRCacImag computation is done inline in stampAc (ac-voltage-source.ts:743-745) from _acMagnitude/_acPhase with the vsrctemp.c:39/42 default-1/default-0 guards (ac-voltage-source.ts:599-602). Two hunks: h001 adds the RFSPICE port-count reset/locals (S-parameter) plus the model/instance accessor rename; h002 rewrites the DC/transient value warnings (downgrades to ERR_INFO, adds the `dc value used for op instead of transient time=0 value` comparison that reads VSRCcoeffs[0]/[1] by function type) and adds the large RFSPICE port-processing block (Z0/freq/power/phase derivation, VSRCportCount bookkeeping, port reordering). The acMag/acPhase derivation is the portable core; the value-presence warnings read the absent VSRCcoeffs model; the RFSPICE block is S-parameter. Resolved per hunk.

Autotools build manifest. digiTS has no C build system; analysis wiring is ES-module imports + Vite. PLANNING.md 5(a). Added sources (cktspnoise/noisesp/optran/com_optran/cktspdum/span/spaskq/spsetp) are themselves noise/optran/RFSPICE NO-COUNTERPART files.

ACan / CKTacLoad map to AcAnalysis.run() frequency-sweep loop + per-element stampAc accumulation. DC-OP relinearization, freq-delta (DECADE/OCTAVE/LINEAR), AC RHS clear/load. AC implemented (analysis-scope.md).
ACsetParm AC_START/AC_STOP validation maps to AcParams validation. v41 relaxes the bound from <=0 to <0. AC implemented (analysis-scope.md).

SPICEanalysis dispatch-table array (analInfo[]). Both hunks only add RFSPICE SPinfo / WITH_HB HBinfo entries (S-parameter and harmonic-balance), neither implemented (analysis-scope.md). digiTS dispatches via the AnalogEngine interface, not a C job table.
CKTdestroy C heap teardown (FREE of rhs/state/matrix, evt_dest, RFSPICE cmat frees). JS is GC-managed; engine drops its object graph on dispose(). PLANNING.md 5(a).
CKTdisto distortion analysis driver (D_SETUP/D_RHSF1/D_RHSF2). Distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
CKTdltMod C heap free of a model+instances (tfree to GENinstanceFree/GENmodelFree). JS GC. PLANNING.md 5(a).
CKTdltNNum deletes a node from the C CKTnodes linked list; hunk adds a controlled_exit guard. digiTS has no CKTnodes list (nodes are compiler-assigned indices). PLANNING.md 5(a).

CKTdoJob copies TSKtask params into the circuit then dispatches. TSK->CKT param transfer maps to ckt-context field population (incl. v41-new indverbosity/xmu/cshunt/epsmin). Include/extern-decl + WANT_SENSE2 sub-blocks have no counterpart; resolved per hunk.
CKTfndMod C hash lookup of a model by name; hunk adds a null-ckt guard. digiTS resolves models at compile time, not via a runtime MODnameHash. PLANNING.md 5(a).

CKTinst2Node front-end query returning a bound CKTnode+name from an instance+terminal, walking the C CKTnodes list and per-instance GENnodeN fields. digiTS has no GENnode array nor CKTnodes list; node binding resolved in the compiler. Not a DC/TRAN/AC numerical path. PLANNING.md 5(a).

CKTic applies .ic/.nodeset initial values into the RHS before the DC-OP solve. digiTS implements nodeset/IC (nr-nodeset-parity.test.ts). v41 hunk also writes CKTrhsOld so the seed survives into the first NR comparison.
CKTload per-iteration device load() driver maps to cktLoad(). The single v41 hunk only adds an XSPICE enh.h include (rshunt); that lone hunk is NO-COUNTERPART, resolved at item level.
CKTmodCrt C heap alloc of a GENmodel + new GENinstanceFree/GENmodelFree heap-free helpers. digiTS creates model objects in the compiler; JS GC frees them. PLANNING.md 5(a).
CKTncDump non-convergence per-node last-vs-previous voltage dump maps to cktncDump() (dc-operating-point.ts:258-269). v41 hunk fixes node-name filter strstr(name,#) to strchr(name,#).
CKTnoise noise-source naming/evaluation driver. Noise not implemented (analysis-scope.md). Hunks add sqrnoise squared_value handling.
CKTnewTask builds a TSKtask copying app/option defaults. v41 hunks add TSKindverbosity/TSKxmu/TSKcshunt/TSKepsmin defaults mapping to ckt-context default fields feeding DC-OP/transient/integration.

CKTop + CKTconvTest + dynamic_gmin/spice3_gmin/new_gmin/gillespie_src/spice3_src are the DC-OP three-level convergence stack mapped explicitly in the dc-operating-point.ts header. DC-OP implemented (analysis-scope.md). v41 adds a new_gmin level and the OPtran tail call (OPtran itself separately NO-COUNTERPART).

CKTpName front-end parser helper: set a device instance parameter by keyword string during netlist parse. digiTS sets element params from the CircuitSpec/property-bag at compile time, not a runtime IFparm keyword scan. PLANNING.md 5(a).
CKTpzSetup pole-zero matrix setup. Pole-zero not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
CKTpzFindZeros / PZeval pole-zero search strategy. Pole-zero not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
sens_sens sensitivity analysis driver. Sensitivity not implemented (analysis-scope.md).

CKTsetBreak registers a transient breakpoint, mapping to the TimestepController breakpoint queue. v41 adds AlmostEqualUlps early-out when the new breakpoint equals current time, plus a null-guard on the CKTbreaks tail compare. Transient implemented (analysis-scope.md).

CKTsetup/CKTunsetup per-device setup()/unsetup() driver + matrix alloc maps to compiler.ts expandCompositeInstance + element setup() walk and NIinit. v41 adds head/DEVices null guards and prev_CKTlastNode incomplete-unsetup detection. XSPICE rshunt include NO-COUNTERPART; resolved per hunk.
sgen_init sensitivity generator iterator. Sensitivity not implemented (analysis-scope.md).

CKTsetOpt + OPTtbl .options dispatch and keyword table. v41-new options indverbosity/xmu/cshunt/epsmin map to ckt-context fields used by DC-OP/transient/integration. XSPICE evt/enh include and description-only edits resolved per hunk.
New file, entirely #ifdef RFSPICE: CKTspDump / CKTspCalcSMatrix / CKTspCalcPowerWave S-parameter raw output. S-parameter (RFSPICE) not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
New file, body gated by #ifdef RFSPICE_ (dead even in ngspice) for S-parameter noise. Neither noise nor S-parameter implemented (analysis-scope.md).

CKTtrouble formats a failure message naming the current DC-sweep (TRCV) source/resistor/temp value; the whole function is bound to the TRCV (.dc sweep) job. .dc transfer-curve sweep is OUT of scope (#54; analysis-scope.md amended 2026-05-29), so dctrcurv.c/ckttroub.c are NO-COUNTERPART. digiTS reports convergence failures via its own diagnostics (cktncDump / convergence log), not the TRCV-specific sweep-point message.
CKTtrunc per-node local-truncation-error timestep bound maps to ckt-terr.ts. Transient implemented (analysis-scope.md). The single hunk only removes a stray token after #endif.

Prototype header for com_optran (the optran command). optran (transient-based OP) is a separate analysis driver digiTS does not implement; see optran.c. PLANNING.md 5(a) + open question on optran scope.

DCop the .op entry: CKTop then SMSIG load. DC-OP implemented (analysis-scope.md). Hunk deletes a commented-out node-dump block and tidies the failure return that calls CKTncDump (cktncDump in digiTS).
DCpss periodic steady-state (PSS) driver (gated WITH_PSS). PSS not in the implemented set (analysis-scope.md lists only DC-OP/transient/AC). PLANNING.md 5(b) + open question.

DCtran transient time-marching driver (TRANop, breakpoint mgmt, state rotation, dt cut/grow, accept) maps to MNAEngine.step/stepToTime + TimestepController + integration.ts. Transient implemented (analysis-scope.md). v41 hunks: bcopy->memcpy, MODEUIC IC print/dump path, breakpoint pop-loop rework, CKTsizeIncr 10->100, ft_norefprint gating.

DCtrCurv the .dc transfer-curve (TRCV) sweep driver: nested V/I/R/temp source stepping with state rotation and per-point CKTop. .dc transfer-curve sweep is OUT of scope (#54; analysis-scope.md amended 2026-05-29 — digiTS does parametric sweeps via parameter-sweep.ts component-property re-runs + external repeated dc_op, not the ngspice TRCV nest-level driver). NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5 (driver of a non-implemented analysis).
DISTOan / DISswap distortion analysis driver. Distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md).

NevalSrc evaluates a single noise-source contribution. Noise not implemented (analysis-scope.md). Large v41 additions are #ifdef RFSPICE S-parameter noise-current correlation; legacy body is the AC-noise gain helper.
NOISEan the .noise analysis driver. Noise not implemented (analysis-scope.md). PLANNING.md 5(b).
New file NOISEsp, body gated by #ifdef RFSPICE_ (dead): S-parameter noise driver. Neither noise nor S-parameter implemented (analysis-scope.md).

New file: com_optran command + OPtran driver generating the OP by a damped transient (own opfinaltime/opstepsize/opramptime + breakpoint table). Not in the implemented set (analysis-scope.md): DC-OP is the direct/gmin/source-step stack in cktop.c which digiTS ports; optran is a distinct driver. PLANNING.md 5(b) + open question. The CKTop tail OPtran call returns the prior converged value when optran is unset, so omitting it is behavior-preserving on the default path.
SENSask sensitivity parameter getter. Sensitivity not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
SENSsetParam + SENSparms sensitivity setter/table. Sensitivity not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
New file, entirely #ifdef RFSPICE: SPan / CKTspnoise / NInspIter / S-Y-Z matrix machinery for S-parameter analysis. S-parameter (RFSPICE) not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
New file, entirely #ifdef RFSPICE: SPaskQuest S-parameter param getter. S-parameter not implemented (analysis-scope.md).
New file, entirely #ifdef RFSPICE: SPsetParm + SPparms + SPinfo registration for S-parameter analysis. Not implemented (analysis-scope.md).

TFanal the .tf small-signal transfer-function / IO-resistance driver. IN scope per #57 (analysis-scope.md amended 2026-05-29): a thin driver over the linearized DC Jacobian (DC transfer ratio output/input + input/output resistance). Absent in src/ today, so both hunks are PENDING blocked by analysis#recon/tf, which builds the .tf driver before the v41 deltas apply.

TRANinit copies .tran params into the circuit and derives CKTmaxStep. Transient implemented (analysis-scope.md). v41 hunk gates the (tstop-tstart)/50 max-step default on the new nostepsizelimit variable.
TRANsetParm .tran TSTOP validation. Transient implemented (analysis-scope.md). Single hunk fixes a TST0P->TSTOP typo in the error string.

Central C device-registration TU. Holds `static_devices[]` (the new-device static list), `spice_init_devices()` / `spice_destroy_devices()` (XSPICE init/teardown), `num_devices()`, `devices()`, `devices_ptr()`, `add_device()`, `add_udn()` (XSPICE runtime registration), `load_opus()` / `load_osdi()` / `load_dev()` / `load_alldevs()` (dynamic-library device loading), Windows dlopen/dlsym/dlerror emulation, and `relink()` (XSPICE/OSDI ft_sim pointer fix-up). The digiTS structural counterpart for the `static_devices[]` table is `src/components/register-all.ts` (the single startup file that imports every `*Definition` and calls `registry.register(*Definition)` — confirmed by reading the file: it begins with ~150 `import` lines and then a sequence of `registry.register(...)` calls). The XSPICE/OSDI dynamic-loader plumbing (heap realloc, dlopen, FormatMessage, MIFiSize/MIFmSize pointer fix-up, GENinstance/GENmodel size assignment) has no digiTS counterpart — digiTS is a static ES-module build with no shared-library plugin model. Per PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note + 2026-05-21 frozen device-class-scope.md: examined hunk by hunk. The 5 v41-new device-registration hunks (h004 hicum2+hisimhv2 includes; h005a vdmos include; h005b ADMS hicum2-relocation + psp103/bsimbulk/bsimcmg/r2_cmc includes; h006 hicum+hsmhv2 static_devices entries; h007 vdmos static_devices entry; h008 ADMS hicum2-removal + psp103/bsimbulk/bsimcmg/r2_cmc static_devices entries) resolve into two buckets per device-class-scope.md: vdmos (IN, reconstruction-bound) → h005a, h007 PENDING; the six foundry compact models (hicum2, hisimhv2, psp103, bsimbulk, bsimcmg, r2_cmc — all OUT per device-class-scope.md "Foundry compact models" section) → h004, h005b, h006, h008 NO-COUNTERPART. Every other hunk in `dev.c` is XSPICE / OSDI / DEVLIB / Windows-dlopen / spice_init_devices / DEVNUM C-plumbing with no digiTS counterpart — PLANNING.md §5 case (a).

C extern-prototype header for the `dev.c` API (spice_init_devices, spice_destroy_devices, num_devices, devices_ptr, devices, load_opus, DEVflag, load_alldevs, load_dev, load_osdi). digiTS has no prototype headers — function signatures are TypeScript class/method declarations on `ComponentRegistry` / `register-all.ts`. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact. The single hunk only adds the `load_osdi(const char *)` extern prototype (an OSDI shared-library entry point) and changes `load_opus(char *)` to `load_opus(const char *)` (a C constness tightening on the dynamic-library name argument).

Shared device-support helpers used by the per-device load() methods: DEVlimvds, DEVpnjlim, DEVfetlim, DEVlimitlog (v41-new), ACM_SourceDrainResistances, ACM_saturationCurrents, ACM_junctionCapacitances, DEVcmeyer, DevCapVDMOS (v41-new), DEVqmeyer, DEVpred, soa_printf. digiTS's voltage-limiting helpers (pnjlim / fetlim / limvds) live in `src/solver/analog/newton-raphson.ts` (lines 100, 185, 243 — each cites `devsup.c` and matches the v26 algorithm). DEVqmeyer is inlined as `devQmeyer` in `src/components/semiconductors/mosfet.ts` (line 586+, called from mos1load at line 1474/1478 with the mode-dependent vgs/vgd swap). Per device-class-scope.md (frozen 2026-05-21) "Engine-side consequences": (1) DEVlimitlog (h001) is PORT — generic NR limiting helper, callable by any IN device; (2) the ACM helper family (h002 / h003 — `ACM_SourceDrainResistances` parameter-name correction) is NO-COUNTERPART by transitive consequence — verified callers are BSIM3 (bsim3/b3temp.c:820, b3set.c:1002, b3ld.c:402/2299) and BSIM3v32 (bsim3v32/b3v32temp.c:890/953, b3v32set.c:1010, b3v32ld.c:397/2582/2612), both in the foundry-compact-model OUT list; (3) DevCapVDMOS (h004) is PENDING — VDMOS is IN per device-class-scope.md and DevCapVDMOS is absorbed into the future `vdmos#recon/wholeClass` reconstruction (declared in the per-device vdmos planning pass, not this overlay).

Automake build-system fragment for the libdense.la static library. C build plumbing with no behavioral content; the library it builds is consumed only by the S-parameter (RFSPICE) analysis (span.c/cktspdum.c/nevalsrc.c), which digiTS does not implement. Case-(a) C-language artifact AND case-(b) whole-analysis.

Dense Mat/CMat matrix library (LU, QR, det, adjoint, inverse, row-echelon, null-space, transpose, concat). New in v41. Sole consumers in the ngspice tree are span.c, cktspdum.c, nevalsrc.c, all under #ifdef RFSPICE - the S-parameter analysis. digiTS's DC-OP/TRAN/AC paths use the sparse solver (src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts), never this dense library. Case-(b): whole SPICE analysis (S-parameter) not implemented per analysis-scope.md.

Public header for the dense matrix library: cplx/Mat/CMat/MatList struct typedefs plus prototypes for every dense.c function, and a large commented-out inline block. Included only inside #ifdef RFSPICE (span.c:27). Same evidence as dense.c: case-(b) whole S-parameter analysis not implemented.

Static-inline complex-scalar helpers (cmultc, caddc, cinv, conju, cdivco, etc.) backing the dense matrix library. Included only inside #ifdef RFSPICE (span.c:28, nevalsrc.c:37). Same evidence as dense.c: case-(b) whole S-parameter analysis not implemented.

ngspice high-level FFT init/free wrapper (Stearns/Green split-radix mixed-cache FFT, public-domain RISC-optimized lib per ref/ngspice/src/maths/fft/Read-Me). Its only two consumers in the ngspice tree are (1) frontend com_fft.c -- the post-processing `fft` spectral-display command operating on already-completed output vectors, NOT a SPICE analysis; and (2) frontend/trannoise/1-f-code.c f_alpha() -- the FFT-based 1/f colored-noise WAVEFORM synthesizer (Kasdin alg) for the HAVE_LIBFFTW3-disabled TRNOISE fallback; vsrcacct.c/isrcacct.c only call fftFree() to release those tables. None lives in digiTS's DC-OP / transient / AC matrix-solve path. digiTS's src/runtime/fft.ts is a separate textbook radix-2 Cooley-Tukey FFT for the oscilloscope frequency-domain UI (self-described, different algorithm) -- not a port of this library. All 3 hunks are pure C-language artifacts (0->NULL pointer comparisons, free->tfree heap wrapper).

ngspice low-level split-radix FFT kernel (same lib as fftext.c; caller manages cos/bit-reversal tables). Same two consumers (com_fft.c frontend command, 1-f-code.c noise synthesizer) -- neither in digiTS's three implemented analyses. digiTS has no port of this kernel; src/runtime/fft.ts is unrelated UI furniture. All 5 hunks are pure C-language artifacts: a <stdlib.h> include, and integer->size_t casts on pointer arithmetic for 64-bit overflow safety -- identical numerical values, no behavioral change.
Automake build-system file. digiTS has no build counterpart (Vite/TS compiler driven). Both hunks only edit the C library source list. NO-COUNTERPART case (a): build system, per PLANNING.md §4 table.

AlmostEqualUlps has a behavioral counterpart: almostEqualUlps (timestep.ts:34-41), consumed by breakpoint gating and source acceptStep. File is PORT. The two v41 hunks, however, are a pure C-macro portability cleanup (local int64_min macro -> stdlib INT64_MIN); both expand to 0x8000000000000000 with zero behavioral change, and digiTS handles opposite-sign operands by an early-return guard rather than the int64 lexicographic reorder, so the touched lines have no digiTS counterpart. See open question Q1.

Fallback erfc()/ierfc() (complementary error function), compiled only when HAVE_ERFC is undefined. v41 DELETES the whole file. digiTS has no erfc/ierfc anywhere (0 matches in src/). Behavioral consumers in ngspice are all OUT-scope or non-analysis: CIDER 2D device simulator (ciderlib/, OUT), LTRA lossy transmission line (ltra, OUT), JUNCAP/PSP foundry models (OUT), and the frontend FFT spectrum command (spec.c). NO-COUNTERPART case (a): dead C fallback file removed; no digiTS counterpart and no IN-scope consumer.

Combined-Tausworthe/LCG RNG (CombLCGTaus) plus Box-Muller polar gaussians (gauss0/gauss1/rgauss), poisson, exprand, and the rndseed/setseed frontend plumbing. digiTS has NO behavioral port of any of these: Monte Carlo uses its own Park-Miller LCG + cos-Box-Muller (SeededRng, monte-carlo.ts), and the vsrc/isrc 'noise' waveform uses Math.random + cos-Box-Muller (boxMuller, ac-voltage-source.ts:52). ngspice consumers are expression gauss()/agauss() (absent from digiTS expression evaluator), Monte Carlo .control loops (not an analysis-scope analysis), CIDER (OUT), and vsrc/isrc TRNOISE RTS via exprand (digiTS does not port exprand). Hunks split: pure-C/frontend plumbing -> NO-COUNTERPART; RNG-algorithm hunks left PENDING pending the user's scope ruling (Q2).

Standalone C test harness with main() that compares ierfc/derfc/glibc erfc; uses fpu_control.h. v41 DELETES it. Not an engine source unit (EXTRA_DIST only, never linked into the library) and has no analysis behavior. NO-COUNTERPART case (a): C-language standalone test program removed.

NIacIter -> AcAnalysis frequency-sweep loop (reorder/LUfac dispatch at ac-analysis.ts:406, complex solve at :419, post-solve rhs[0]/irhs[0] clears). h001 mixes the RFSPICE-only NIspPreload/NIspSolve split-functions (S-parameter port analysis, not in analysis-scope.md) with the NIacIter `double *temp;` declaration removal -> needs a sub-split (see review doc). h002 is the NIacIter post-solve rhs-clear + SWAP-macro refactor, portable.

NIcomCof -> computeNIcomCof (integration.ts:230) + solveGearVandermonde (:149). h001-h003 are the runtime-xmu (CKTxmu) refactor and ag[] zeroing, all with counterparts. h004 (CKTagp bzero->memset) lives inside `#ifdef PREDICTOR` (nicomcof.c:128-206), a feature `#undef`'d in ngspice's default build (config.h:475) and deliberately not implemented in digiTS (analog-engine.ts:384-390).

NIconvTest -> inlined convergence test in newtonRaphson STEP H (newton-raphson.ts:653-748). The substantive v41 change (isnan(new) -> non-convergence return 1) is already cited at newton-raphson.ts:690-696 -> niconv.c:43-46. Remaining hunk content is STEPDEBUG/ft_ngdebug debug-print plumbing and whitespace; the function has a clear counterpart so all hunks are PENDING.

NIdestroy -> C heap free of predictor arrays (CKTpred, CKTsols[]) inside `#ifdef PREDICTOR`. JS is GC'd (PLANNING.md §4 del/dest row) and the predictor is `#undef`'d in ngspice's default build; no behavioral existence in digiTS.

NIdIter is the distortion-analysis iteration solver: its sole callers in ngspice are distoan.c (verified ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/analysis/distoan.c:287-414). Distortion is NOT implemented (analysis-scope.md).

NIinit (CKTniState=NIUNINITIALIZED + SMPnewMatrix) -> CKTCircuitContext constructor matrix/solver init (ckt-context.ts:682-685, `SMPnewMatrix -> spCreate`). h001 adds the new `size` pre-size arg to SMPnewMatrix (passed 0 by niinit), a matrix-allocation init change with a counterpart at the solver-init site.
NIintegrate -> niIntegrate (ni-integrate.ts:19). h001 is whitespace-only (trailing-space removal) in the TRAP order-1/order-2 cases, already mirrored at ni-integrate.ts:31-37.

NIiter -> newtonRaphson (newton-raphson.ts:406). The full v41 NIiter refactor: msgcount static + fteext include (h001), loop-body restructure (h002), MODEINITJCT/TRAN NISHOULDREORDER + singular-matrix message-limiting (h003), SMPluFac dispatch (h004), the big body rewrite incl. memcpy OldCKTstate0 / DOING_TRAN errMsg guard / node-damping rewrite (h005), and the trailing rhs/rhsOld SWAP + NIresetwarnmsg (h006). All map to the single newtonRaphson NIiter port.
NInzIter is the noise-analysis adjoint-system solver (the `nz` = noise): its sole callers in ngspice are noisesp.c:294 and noisean.c:412 (verified). Noise is NOT implemented (analysis-scope.md).

NIpzMuller / NIpzSym2 are the pole-zero (PZ) Muller's-method root-finding routines (PZtrial-typed). Pole-zero is NOT implemented (analysis-scope.md); no PZ method/engine/MCP-tool anywhere in src (verified by grep: no PZ counterpart).

ft_interpolate (re-samples a result vector onto a new scale via piecewise polynomial fit) is a nutmeg/CP-frontend post-processing primitive. Its only ngspice callers are frontend/interp.c (linearize cmd), frontend/fourier.c (fourier cmd), frontend/plotting/plotcurv.c + plotit.c (plot), and maths/cmaths/cmath4.c::cx_interpolate (CP let/plot operator) - none under spicelib/ (no DC-OP/transient/AC caller). No polyfit/ft_interpolate/ft_peval symbol exists anywhere in digiTS src/, and digiTS has no src/frontend / CP shell to host it. Outside analysis-scope.md (DC-OP/transient/AC). Sibling maths-cmaths-review.md classifies the consumer cmath4.c::cx_interpolate NO-COUNTERPART on the same basis.

ft_polyfit (least-squares polynomial fit by Gauss-Jordan elimination) is the primitive under ft_interpolate; called by interpolate.c and (via ft_peval) the same frontend/CP-shell consumers. No caller under spicelib/; no counterpart symbol in digiTS src/; no CP shell. Outside analysis-scope.md.

C preprocessor / memory-API plumbing header. All three hunks are C-language artifacts with no behavioral content: MALLOC/REALLOC #undef plumbing, the SWAP brace-block-to-do/while statement-safety rewrite (digiTS has no SWAP macro - swaps are inline in _swapColumns/_spcRowExchange/_spcColExchange), and the txfree/trealloc const-qualifier signature change. digiTS is GC'd JS with no tmalloc/txfree/trealloc and no C macro layer.

spOrderAndFactor is ported line-for-line as _spOrderAndFactor (sparse-solver.ts:1600). Hunks mix a real behavioral change (the pPivot null-guard in the no-reorder reuse loop, already present at sparse-solver.ts:1646) with HAS_WINGUI INCRESP responsiveness macros (Windows-GUI-only C artifact). Resolved hunk-by-hunk; h002 needs a sub-split (see review.md).

spFileVector is a C debug-dump-to-file routine (fopen-based), reached only via SMPprintRHS/SMPprint (spsmp.c:285,296) - never on a solve path. digiTS has no file-writing matrix dump (browser, no filesystem); the only white-box read surface is sparse-solver-instrumentation.ts, an in-memory CSC reader with no file output and explicitly no ngspice analogue (instrumentation file header lines 19-23). The append->write fopen-mode change has no behavioral existence in digiTS.

SMP-layer wrappers. h001 (SMPnewMatrix size param) and h003 (SMPconstMult/SMPmultiply) have behavioral content only in sensitivity (cktsens.c); h002 (SMPfindElt Col==-1 guard) serves nodeset/IC handling in CKTload, which digiTS implements via a different mechanism. Resolved hunk-by-hunk.
Automake build-system file. No behavioral content; digiTS builds with Vite/tsc, not automake. PLANNING.md SS4 table: Makefile.am -> NO-COUNTERPART.

B-Source (ASRC B-element) behavioral-expression engine (inpptree.c header: Tree generator for B-Source parser). ASRC is asrc#recon/wholeClass (device-class-scope.md) - never ported. digiTS analog expression engine (expression.ts / expression-evaluate.ts / expression-differentiate.ts) is digiTS-own (consumed by VCCS/VCVS/CCCS/CCVS/AC sources), not a port of inpptree/ptfuncs. Architectural divergence -> REVIEW-CLASS, hunks PENDING, open question (PLANNING.md SS5).

Deck-reader machinery. digiTS has no ngspice-style runtime SPICE-deck reader (the 4-pass INPpas* / INP2X card dispatch); its runtime input is the compiled CircuitSpec graph (src/compile/compile.ts, src/solver/analog/compiler.ts), and the only SPICE-text path is the import feature (model-parser.ts -> spice-model-builder.ts), a digiTS reimplementation rather than a port. Whole-file architectural divergence -> REVIEW-CLASS, hunks PENDING, raised as an open question (PLANNING.md SS5: architectural divergence is the user-s call, not NO-COUNTERPART).

C include-guard-only header (ifndef ngspice_INPPAS4_H ... endif, one include). No declarations, no data, no behavior. PLANNING.md SS5 case (a) C-language artifact.

New Bison lexer/constructor prototype header: declares PTlex and PT_mk*node externs plus struct PTltype. Prototype-only C plumbing, no behavioral content; PLANNING.md SS4 (prototype header) + SS5 case (a).

Machine-generated GNU Bison parser implementation (header comment: A Bison parser, made by GNU Bison 2.5). Whole-file delete (-1972): v41 regenerates the parser from the .y grammar at build time. Generated C artifact with no hand-authored behavior; PLANNING.md SS5 case (a).
Machine-generated Bison parser header (token enums, YYSTYPE union). Whole-file delete (-94). Generated C artifact; PLANNING.md SS5 case (a).

Architectural divergence -> REVIEW-CLASS, hunks PENDING, open question (PLANNING.md SS5).

Deck-reader machinery. digiTS has no ngspice-style runtime SPICE-deck reader (the 4-pass INPpas* / INP2X card dispatch); its runtime input is the compiled CircuitSpec graph (src/compile/compile.ts, src/solver/analog/compiler.ts), and the only SPICE-text path is the import feature (model-parser.ts -> spice-model-builder.ts), a digiTS reimplementation rather than a port. Whole-file architectural divergence -> REVIEW-CLASS, hunks PENDING, raised as an open question (PLANNING.md SS5: architectural divergence is the user-s call, not NO-COUNTERPART).

items.rationale: Removal of the singh@ / routine-description comment block at the top of ASRCask. Part of the integer-id IFparm value-getter dispatch (ASRCask), which has no digiTS counterpart: digiTS has no integer-id ask API; output quantities (current/voltage) come from getPinCurrents()/instance fields and params are read by name from the property bag. Getter dispatch glue, PLANNING.md §5 case (a). Mirrors the dio/dioask.c REVIEW-CLASS→NO-COUNTERPART precedent.

ASRCask switch-body restyle + new ASRC_M getter case. Integer-id IFparm value-getter dispatch with no digiTS counterpart (no ask API). The load-bearing readback quantities (output current/voltage, the m value) are produced by the recon's load()/getPinCurrents() and stored as instance state, not by this dispatch shell. Getter glue, PLANNING.md §5 case (a).

items.rationale: ASRCdelete — C heap free of the instance (INPfreeTree + FREE of acValues/posPtr/vars). JavaScript is garbage-collected; digiTS has no per-instance teardown method. PLANNING.md §4 (<dev>del.c) / §5 case (a).

ASRCdestroy — C heap free of the module-wide asrc_vals/asrc_derivs scratch buffers (and their nvals reset). JavaScript is garbage-collected; the scratch arrays become element-local typed arrays with no explicit destroy step. PLANNING.md §4 (<dev>dest.c) / §5 case (a).

items.rationale: Automake source-list fragment — a C build-system artifact (PLANNING.md §5 case (a)). digiTS has no autotools build; components register via src/core/registry.ts. The hunk only deletes the capdel.c and capdest.c entries from libcap_la_SOURCES; both files are themselves NO-COUNTERPART C-heap-free artifacts. No behavioral content.

Automake source-list fragment — a C build-system artifact (PLANNING.md §5 case (a)). The hunk only deletes the capmdel.c entry from libcap_la_SOURCES; capmdel.c is itself a NO-COUNTERPART C-heap-free artifact. No behavioral content.

CAPpTable IFparm rows for the `cap`/`c` aliases change from IOPAP to IOPAPR — the macro that adds the IF_REDIRECT flag so ngspice's generic ask/set machinery redirects the alias name onto the canonical CAP_CAP id. digiTS keys parameters by name string (defineModelParams primary key `capacitance` in capacitor.ts); it has no integer-id IFparm table, no parser-driven generic ask/set, and no IF_REDIRECT flag. The hunk is C parser-table plumbing with no behavioral content — PLANNING.md §5 case (a). No parameter is added or removed and no default changes.

CAPmPTable IFparm model-param alias rows (`cox`/`capsw`/`w`/`l`) change from IOPR to IOPAR/IOPXR — the redirect-flag macro variants. digiTS keys parameters by name string with no integer-id IFparm table, no parser-driven generic ask/set, and no IF_REDIRECT flag. The hunk is C parser-table plumbing with no behavioral content — PLANNING.md §5 case (a): no parameter is added or removed and no default changes. NOTE: Q1 is resolved — the canonical parameters these rows alias (cj/cjsw/defw/defl — geometric/process capacitance) ARE now ported into the digiTS capacitor model by reconstruction item cap#recon/computeTemperature. Even so, this hunk stays NO-COUNTERPART: the v41 change is purely the IF_REDIRECT alias-flag edit, and digiTS's name-keyed param system has no IFparm redirect mechanism to apply it onto. The new params do not give this redirect-flag-only hunk a counterpart; it depends on no reconstruction item.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/capacitor.ts — AnalogCapacitorElement.stampAc. verifierNotes: REOPENED — Tier-2 bijective construct correspondence depended on the stampAc shape verified by cap#recon/stampAc, which has been retracted. Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/cap/capacld.c:21-40 (cap.md:93-118). Zero-delta rule satisfied: every +/- line in the hunk slice is a VERIFICATION.md sec4 allowed cosmetic rename — three model/instance linked-list accessor renames (model->CAPnextModel -> CAPnextModel(model); model->CAPinstances -> CAPinstances(model); here->CAPnextInstance -> CAPnextInstance(here)) on the for-loop headers (cap.md:97-99 -> :100-102), plus four 'ptr'->'Ptr' field-capitalisation renames on the matrix-pointer field names (CAPposPosptr/CAPnegNegptr/CAPposNegptr/CAPnegPosptr -> ...Ptr at cap.md:108-111 -> :112-115). No new statements, no new branches, no arithmetic change, no operand reorder, no nesting change. Tier-2 piggybacks on cap#recon/stampAc (now APPLIED): post-edit stampAc at capacitor.ts:462-478 is bijective with v41 CAPacLoad — m=CAPm, val=CKTomega*CAPcapac, four ±m*val stamps to the imaginary half of (posPos, negNeg, posNeg, negPos) cells reached through _handles[0..3] (allocated once in setup() via allocNortonStamp, matching capsetup.c:114-117 TSTALLOC). items.rationale: sCAPinstance struct: replaces the hand-rolled CAPmodPtr/CAPnextInstance/CAPname/CAPstate members with an embedded `struct GENinstance gen` plus accessor macros, and marks the node-id fields `const`. This is C generic-struct-embedding plumbing — PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no GENinstance, no model-instance linked list, and no integer node-id instance fields (pinNodes is a ReadonlyMap). The renamed CAPposPos/negNeg/posNeg/negPos `ptr` to `Ptr` matrix-pointer fields likewise have no digiTS counterpart — digiTS holds opaque Norton-stamp handles, not raw double* matrix pointers. No state slot, parameter, or default changes.

C struct-field rename — PLANNING.md §5 case (a). digiTS holds opaque Norton-stamp handles (the _handles tuple from allocNortonStamp), not raw double* matrix pointers; there is no field to rename. No behavioral content.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/capacitor.ts — CAPACITOR_SCHEMA (the defineStateSchema slot list Q, CCAP) and AnalogCapacitorElement.stateSize. The new `#define CAPnumStates 2` is the named per-instance state-slot count.

Zero-delta rule satisfied: the +line is a C preprocessor named-constant introduction with no executable behaviour of its own -- the literal value 2 is consumed downstream at capsetup.c:103 (*states += CAPnumStates), which is itself a separate sub-item (capsetup.c#h003a). digiTS encodes the slot count symbolically by construction: CAPACITOR_SCHEMA = defineStateSchema(...) lists exactly 2 slots Q, CCAP at capacitor.ts:200-203 and stateSize = CAPACITOR_SCHEMA.size at :219. VERIFICATION.md sec4 allowed C->TS surface form: a C `#define <name> 2` consumed at one allocation site maps to a TS schema whose `size` field is the named slot count. MATCH (verifier, round 2). Tier1: ccvs.md docLineRange[548-562] hunk2 +/- lines are the pure ptr->Ptr capitalisation rename on the five TSTALLOC calls — identifier rename, allowed §4. items.rationale: CSWtrunc is DORMANT in v41: cswload.c:132 leaves the controlling-current store behind a commented-out FIXME, so lastChange is always 0 and the truncation never fires. h001 is the header-comment removal. digiTS CurrentControlledSwitch has no getLteTimestep and CSW_SCHEMA has no ctrlvalue slot; the v41 truncation is bit-identical to no-truncation, so building it would be dead code.

CSWtrunc function body. Dormant in v41 (cswload.c:132 FIXME disables the ctrlvalue store -> lastChange==0 always -> no timestep limit emitted). No digiTS counterpart (no truncation method, CSW_SCHEMA lacks a ctrlvalue slot). Omission is bit-identical to v41 behavior; NO-COUNTERPART per user ruling 2026-06-03.

The hunk only deletes the diodel.c/diodest.c entries from libdio_la_SOURCES; both are themselves NO-COUNTERPART C-heap-free files. No behavioral content.

items.rationale: Adds an OPUR("qd", DIO_CHARGE, ...) output-redirect alias for the existing 'charge' output. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS keys params/outputs by name string with no integer-id IFparm table and no IF_REDIRECT alias mechanism. The aliased quantity (DIO_CHARGE) is unchanged; the hunk is purely a parser-table alias-flag row. No behavioral content.

Changes the 'tref' DIOmPTable row from IOPR to IOPUR — adds the unit + redirect flags on the tref→DIO_MOD_TNOM alias. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS keys params by name string (TNOM in DIODE_PARAM_DEFS) with no integer-id IFparm table and no IF_REDIRECT alias mechanism. The aliased canonical param (TNOM) is present and unchanged; the hunk is purely a parser-table flag edit. No parameter added/removed, no default changed.

Changes the 'cj0'/'cj' DIOmPTable rows from IOPR to IOPAR — adds the area + redirect flags on the cj0/cj→DIO_MOD_CJO aliases. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS keys params by name string (CJO in DIODE_PARAM_DEFS) with no integer-id IFparm table and no IF_REDIRECT alias mechanism. The aliased canonical param (CJO) is present and unchanged; the hunk is purely a parser-table flag edit. No parameter added/removed, no default changed.

Removes a duplicate IOP("ctp", DIO_MOD_CTP, ...) row that was accidentally listed twice in the v26 DIOmPTable. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): a C parser-table de-duplication; digiTS has no IFparm table and never had a duplicate. The single surviving ctp row (the canonical CTP perimeter-cap temp coeff) is unaffected. No behavioral content.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DIODE_PARAM_DEFS (the new model params: isr/nr recombination; rth0/cth0 self-heating; id_max/te_max/pd_max SOA; lm/lp/wm/wp/xom/xoi/xm/xp level-3 geometry) plus the new 'Tj' thermal terminal in the diode pin layout (buildDiodePinDeclarations / pinNodes). The DIOmPTable rows + DIOnames Tj entry map to model-param entries and the pin declaration.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the MODEAC capacitance/conductance companion-stamp block (capGate path, diode.ts:804-877). digiTS folds the AC small-signal stamp into load() under MODEAC rather than a separate stampAc method; the ngspice DIOacLoad gspr/geq/xceq imaginary-half stamps are that path. h001 is the accessor rename plus the `gspr = DIOtConductance` area-relocation (area folded into DIOtConductance in diotemp now).

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, MODEAC path, new self-heating thermal-node AC stamp block (DIOtempPos/PosPrime/Neg/Temp + posTemp/posPrimeTemp/negTemp ptrs, xgcTt). Requires the thermal node and the dIth_*/gcTt instance state introduced by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — the DIOthermal flag accessor (a getThermal-equivalent on DiodeAnalogElement, read of the THERMAL param). Introduced together with the self-heating subsystem; blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

items.rationale: Changes the DIO_POWER ask output to add the series-resistance dissipation term (Id·Vd + Id²/gspr). PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no integer-id ask API and no DIO_POWER output — power is not an exposed diode output in DiodeAnalogElement (getPinCurrents returns terminal currents only). The hunk modifies an output-getter that has no behavioral existence in digiTS. The dissipated-power quantity is recomputed internally by the self-heating Ith term (dioload.c h-block), which is the porting target for power; this ask-getter is not.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.checkConvergence (diode.ts:880-901). h001 is the local-var declaration (delTemp/deldelTemp) + accessor rename; in digiTS this maps to the function-local additions in checkConvergence.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.checkConvergence, the cdhat prediction. h002 adds the self-heating delTemp/deldelTemp term to cdhat (cdhat += dIdio_dT·deldelTemp). Requires the thermal node + DIOdeltemp/DIOdIdio_dT state from dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.checkConvergence, the CKTtroubleElt store site. h003 is the whitespace normalization of the troubleElt assignment (tab→spaces). The CKTtroubleElt/CKTnoncon bump maps to checkConvergence returning false; no behavioral change in this hunk.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement instance fields / pinNodes. The GENinstance struct embedding, const node-id qualifiers, and ptr→Ptr matrix-pointer renames have no digiTS counterpart (pinNodes is a ReadonlyMap; stamps use opaque handles), but the hunk as a whole introduces the thermal node, so it is PENDING blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement instance fields + DIODE_PARAM_DEFS. Adds the level-3 instance Given-flags (DIOlengthMetalGiven etc.) and instance fields (DIOlengthMetal/Poly, DIOwidthMetal/Poly) plus the `int DIOthermal` self-heating flag. The level-3 geometry fields are blocked by dio#recon/level3GeoCap; the DIOthermal flag by dio#recon/selfHeating. functionGroup-split tracked in §5 of the review (the hunk mixes two reconstructions).

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — the temperature-derivative working fields mirroring DioTempParams (DIOtConductance_dT, DIOtSatCur_dT, DIOtSatSWCur_dT, DIOtTunSatCur_dT, DIOtTunSatSWCur_dT). These _dT derivatives feed the self-heating Jacobian; they are introduced by the diotemp.c rewrite. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate (which rebuilds the v26 temp body the derivatives extend) and consumed by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement instance fields. Adds DIOtRecSatCur/DIOtRecSatCur_dT (recombination, blocked by dio#recon/recombination), the dIth_dVrs/dIth_dVdio/dIth_dT/gcTt/dIrs_dT/dIdio_dT self-heating Jacobian-cache fields (blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating), and DIOcmetal/DIOcpoly level-3 parasitic caps (blocked by dio#recon/level3GeoCap). Mixed; whole hunk PENDING (the dominant introduction is the self-heating Jacobian cache).

items.rationale: Removes the old `#define DIORSNOIZ 0 .. #define DIONSRCS 4` macro block (replaced by the enum in h001) and leaves the DIOnVar[NSTATVARS][DIONSRCS] noise-density array declaration. items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DIODE_SCHEMA (defineStateSchema) + SLOT_* constants + stateSize. The DIOdphidp/DIOsensxp/DIOnumSenStates macros it also touches are sensitivity (NO-COUNTERPART per analysis-scope.md), but the new self-heating state slots dominate and require the schema expansion; whole hunk PENDING blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

items.rationale: Whole-file deletion of DIOdelete — a C-heap free (FREE(here)) unlinking one instance from a linked list. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS is GC-managed; no allocator, no destructor, no model-instance linked list. The file is deleted in v41.
Whole-file deletion of DIOdestroy — a C-heap free loop releasing every model and instance. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. The file is deleted in v41.

Distortion setup (DIOdSetup). PLANNING.md §5 case (b): distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md). The hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename. Whole file belongs to distortion.

Distortion setup (DIOdSetup), breakdown-region branch. PLANNING.md §5 case (b): distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md). The hunk reorders the breakdown gd computation (gd = csat*evrev/vt computed once, removing the dead vte-based assignment) inside the distortion-only DIOdSetup. Whole file belongs to distortion.

C extern-prototype header. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no prototype headers — signatures are TS class members. The single hunk only removes the extern declarations of DIOdelete/DIOdestroy/DIOmDelete (the three deleted C-heap-free functions). No surviving declaration is changed — no new parameter slot, no changed default, no new capability.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the MODEUIC initial-condition branch (vd = params.IC, diode.ts:618-619). ngspice DIOgetic seeds DIOinitCond from the external node voltages when IC is not given; digiTS reads params.IC under the MODEINITJCT+MODETRANOP+MODEUIC gate. The single hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename inside the IC-retrieval loop.

items.rationale: SPICEdev DIOinfo registration dispatch struct, converted to C99 designated initialisers. PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note + §5 case (a): digiTS has no SPICEdev table — registration is the DiodeDefinition object and method binding is the DiodeAnalogElement class. Reviewed hunk-by-hunk per the glue note: every surviving function-pointer assignment (DEVload/DEVsetup/DEVtemperature/DEVtrunc/DEVacLoad/DEVconvTest/DEVsetic/DEVask/DEVmodAsk + the disto/noise/soaCheck/sen* pointers) is unchanged v26→v41; the only non-cosmetic change is DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete/DEVdelete set to NULL (the deleted heap functions). No new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, function-local declarations. Adds cdb_dT/cdsw_dT temperature derivatives of the bottom/sidewall currents; in digiTS these are load()-local values feeding the self-heating Jacobian. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate (the derivatives) and consumed by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, function-local declarations for the recombination current path (gen_fac, gen_fac_vd, t1, evd_rec, cdb_rec, gdb_rec, cdb_rec_dT). Blocked by dio#recon/recombination.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, function-local declarations for self-heating (deldelTemp, delTemp, Temp, ceqqth, Ith, gcTt, vrs, dIdio_dT, dIth_dVdio, dIrs_dT, dIth_dVrs, dIth_dT, csat_dT, csatsw_dT, argsw_dT) and the Check_dio/Check_th split. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the per-instance init block. Sets Check_th from the selfheat predicate, reorders the cd/cdb/gd/gdb/cdsw init, and relocates `gspr = DIOtConductance` (area now folded into DIOtConductance by diotemp). The gspr area-relocation is blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate; the Check_th/selfheat predicate by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the sensitivity-seed branch (CKTsenInfo). digiTS has no sensitivity (analysis-scope.md), so the CKTsenInfo wrapper has no counterpart; the behavioral content this hunk adds is the delTemp seeding from CKTstate0/1 inside that path, which lands in the MODEINIT* vd/delTemp dispatch in load(). Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the MODEINITSMSIG/MODEINITTRAN/MODEINITJCT/MODEINITFIX vd-selection chain (diode.ts:610-645). Adds delTemp seeding alongside each vd seed (delTemp from CKTstate0/1, or 0.0 in the off/JCT cases). Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the MODEINITPRED branch (diode.ts:628-639). Adds the state1→state0 copies for DIOdeltemp/DIOdIdio_dT/DIOqth, the DEVpred(delTemp) prediction, the qth init (cth0·delTemp), and the MODEINITTRAN qth history copy. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load / checkConvergence bypass path. Adds the deldelTemp tolerance test to the bypass condition (skip recompute only if both current and temperature predictions are within tol), storing delTemp/dIdio_dT on bypass. digiTS has no NOBYPASS path in load() today; the thermal-prediction term is part of the self-heating subsystem. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the pnjlim block (diode.ts:649-674). Renames Check→Check_dio and adds the DEVlimitlog(delTemp, ...) thermal-step limiter call (digiTS limitlog already exists in newton-raphson.ts:295). The thermal limiting is part of self-heating. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the `next1:` block opening. Adds the selfheat Temp = DIOtemp + delTemp re-evaluation via DIOtempUpdate(Temp) and the csat_dT/csatsw_dT assignment from the temperature-derivative fields, plus gspr. The DIOtempUpdate re-eval at perturbed Temp requires the parameterized temp function (dio#recon/diotempUpdate); the selfheat gating by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the sidewall current block (diode.ts:697-726). Adds cdsw_dT temperature-derivative terms to each sidewall region (forward/reverse/breakdown) and the merged csat_dT += csatsw_dT. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate (the _dT derivatives extend the temp body).

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load / computeDiodeIV, the bottom-current forward region. Adds cdb_dT and the entire recombination-current sub-block (vterec, evd_rec, cdb_rec, gen_fac via tJctPot/tGradingCoeff, gdb_rec, cdb_rec_dT) gated on DIOrecSatCurGiven. Blocked by dio#recon/recombination (the recombination block) and dio#recon/diotempUpdate (cdb_dT).

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, sidewall tunneling current. Adds cdsw_dT tunneling-derivative term. Tunneling (DIOtTunSatSWCur) is a v26 feature digiTS never ported, and the _dT derivative extends it. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, bottom tunneling current. Adds cdb_dT tunneling-derivative term. Tunneling (DIOtTunSatCur) is a v26 feature digiTS never ported, and the _dT derivative extends it. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the cd/gd assembly + IKF/IKR knee limiting (diode.ts:737-769). Adds dIdio_dT = cdb_dT + cdsw_dT (self-heating Jacobian) and changes the knee-current gates from `model->DIOforwardKneeCurrent > 0.0` to `model->DIOforwardKneeCurrentGiven` (same for reverse). The Given-flag gate change is blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate (where the *KneeCurrentGiven epsmin-disable defaulting lives, diosetup.c h-block); dIdio_dT by dio#recon/selfHeating.

items.rationale: Adds `*(CKTstate0 + DIOdIdio_dT) = dIdio_dT;` inside the `if(SenCond){}` block (dio.md 1411-1418) — a per-device SENSITIVITY store. digiTS implements no per-device sensitivity (no CKTsenInfo/SenCond), consistent with the frozen DIO sensitivity family (diosload/diosacl/diosset/diosupd/diosprt/diomask). User ruling 2026-06-03: device sensitivities NO-COUNTERPART. (Prior planningNote misidentified this as the MODEDCTRANCURVE capGate widening — that is a different hunk, already PORTed.)

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the charge-storage block (diode.ts:811-821) + computeJunctionCharge. Two changes: (1) `diffcharge = TT*cd` (the v41 merge of bottom + sidewall diffusion charge into the total current cd, replacing separate diffcharge/diffchargeSW; diffcap = TT*gd likewise) — a genuine arithmetic relocation, PORT; (2) `capd += DIOcmetal + DIOcpoly` adds the level-3 parasitic caps, blocked by dio#recon/level3GeoCap.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the small-signal/transient charge store-back. Adds the SenCond store of dIdio_dT (sensitivity wrapper, no digiTS counterpart) and the selfheat NIintegrate(cth0, qth) → gcTt/ceqqth thermal-capacitor companion with its MODEINITTRAN cqth history copy. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load / checkConvergence, the non-convergence bump. Changes `if (Check == 1)` to `if ((Check_th == 1) || (Check_dio == 1))` so a thermal-limiting event also flags non-convergence. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.load, the `next2:` state store-back + stamp block (diode.ts:775-800). Adds the DIOdeltemp/DIOdIdio_dT state writes, the entire self-heating power/Jacobian computation (vrs, Ith = vd·cd + vrs²·gspr, dIrs_dT, dIth_dVrs/dVdio/dT, gcTt cache), the thermal RHS contributions to posNode/posPrimeNode/negNode/tempNode, and the 7 thermal-node matrix stamps (tempPos/tempPosPrime/tempNeg/tempTemp/posTemp/posPrimeTemp/negTemp). Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

items.rationale: Model-param ask getter (DIOmAsk), DIO_MOD_IS case. Adds a CKTepsmin floor to the returned IS value. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no integer-id model-ask API — model params are read by name from the property bag. This is an output-getter clamp with no behavioral existence in digiTS. The IS epsmin floor that affects the simulation lives in diosetup.c (model->DIOsatCur clamp) which is a separate PORT site under dio#recon/diotempUpdate; this ask-getter clamp is not that site.

Model-param ask getter (DIOmAsk). Adds getter cases for the new model params (id_max/pd_max/te_max, isr/nr, rth0/cth0, lm/lp/wm/wp/xom/xoi/xm/xp). PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no integer-id model-ask API — params are read by name from the property bag, not via a which-id switch returning IFvalue. The underlying params are introduced by their reconstruction items (recombination/selfHeating/level3GeoCap), but the integer-id getter mechanism this hunk extends has no digiTS counterpart. No behavioral content.

Whole-file deletion of DIOmDelete — a C-heap free unlinking a model from the model list and freeing its instances. PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS is GC-managed; no model destructor, no model linked list. The file is deleted in v41.

Whole hunk PENDING.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — model-param defaulting in createDiodeElement. Changes the IKF/IKR (forward/reverse knee) defaulting: instead of `if(!given) set 0`, v41 keeps the Given-flag and, if given but < CKTepsmin, clears the Given-flag with a warning. Pairs with the dioload.c h015 `*KneeCurrentGiven` gate change. The IKF/IKR knee path exists in digiTS (diode.ts:742-768) but the epsmin-disable defaulting is part of the v26-baseline temp/setup defaulting digiTS never fully ported. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — model-param defaulting in createDiodeElement / DiodeAnalogElement.setup. Adds the model defaults: id_max/pd_max/te_max=1e99 (SOA), recEmissionCoeff=2 / recSatCur=1e-14 (recombination), the DIOsatCur CKTepsmin floor, DIOnomTemp=CKTnomTemp, DIOconductance from DIOresist, rth0=0/cth0=1e-5 (self-heating), and the eight level-3 geometry defaults (lengthMetal/Poly, widthMetal/Poly =0; metalOxideThick/polyOxideThick =1e-6; metalMaskOffset/polyMaskOffset =0). Spans recombination/selfHeating/level3GeoCap/diotempUpdate; whole hunk PENDING. The dominant introduction (satCur floor, nomTemp, conductance) is the v26 setup-defaulting under dio#recon/diotempUpdate; the rest are reconstruction-specific defaults.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.setup, the instance-loop area/perimeter + level-3 geometry derivation. Adds the model/instance-walk rename, the DIOcmetal/DIOcpoly init, and the level=3 block: area/pj scaled by scale², the wm/lm/wp/lp instance-or-model selection, and the CONSTepsSiO2-based parasitic metal/poly overlap-capacitance computation. Blocked by dio#recon/level3GeoCap (requires CONSTepsSiO2, the geometry params, and the level=3 model branch).

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.setup + DIODE_SCHEMA. Changes `*states += 5` to `*states += DIOnumStates` (the 5→9 state-slot expansion for the self-heating thermal-cap and delTemp/dIdio_dT slots) and the sensitivity `2 * SENparms` to `DIOnumSenStates * SENparms` (sensitivity, NO-COUNTERPART per analysis-scope.md). The state-count expansion is the self-heating subsystem. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.setup, the selfheat predicate computation (int selfheat = DIOtempNode>0 && DIOthermal && DIOrth0Given) inserted before the TSTALLOC macro. This gates the thermal-node matrix allocations in h007. Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

Blocked by dio#recon/selfHeating.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — DiodeAnalogElement.setup, the DIOunsetup posPrime-node teardown. Changes the unsetup guard to `DIOposPrimeNode > 0` and always resets DIOposPrimeNode = 0. digiTS allocates the prime node in setup() via ctx.makeVolt (diode.ts:573-578); unsetup/node-release has a partial counterpart (the internalLabels / engine node lifecycle). The guard-and-reset change is a structural refinement of node teardown — PORT.

items.rationale: DIOunsetup device teardown (dio.md 2071-2094): CKTdltNNum(posPrimeNode) + DIOposPrimeNode=0 reset behind a >0 guard. digiTS has no device-teardown/unsetup surface (no unsetup() vtable; engine rebuilt fresh per compile, the prime node re-derived each setup). User ruling 2026-06-03: device deletes/teardown NO-COUNTERPART. Sibling of the frozen diodel/diomdel family.

Sensitivity load (DIOsLoad). PLANNING.md §5 case (b): analysis-scope.md ratifies sensitivity as not implemented (no SENstruct/SENparms/senParmNo, no sensitivity engine pass/tool). The hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename inside the sensitivity loop. Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

Sensitivity setup (DIOsSetup). PLANNING.md §5 case (b): sensitivity not implemented (analysis-scope.md) — the function assigns DIOsenParmNo into a SENstruct (info->SENparms) that has no digiTS analogue. The hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/diode.ts — dioTemp() and DiodeAnalogElement.computeTemperature. The whole DIOtemp→DIOtempUpdate(model, here, Temp, ckt) rewrite: the temperature body is extracted into a function taking an explicit Temp (so self-heating can re-evaluate at Temp+delTemp), the DIOgradingCoeffMax cp_getvar grading-coeff limit, ALL _dT temperature derivatives (tSatCur_dT, tSatSWCur_dT, tTunSatCur_dT, tTunSatSWCur_dT, tRecSatCur_dT, tConductance_dT), the recombination tRecSatCur scaling, the tlev/tlevc branch selection, TM1/TM2 grading-temp, the breakdown-matching iteration, area-scaled DIOtConductance, and the sidewall tF2SW/tF3SW/tDepSWCap. digiTS's dioTemp implements only a subset of the v26 body (no tlev/tlevc, TM1/TM2, TTT1/TTT2, TRS/TRS2, tunneling, brkdEmissionCoeff, full sidewall cap), so the v26 baseline must be reconstructed before this rewrite applies. Blocked by dio#recon/diotempUpdate; the _dT derivatives are further consumed by dio#recon/selfHeating and tRecSatCur by dio#recon/recombination.

The hunk only deletes the inddel.c and inddest.c entries from libind_la_SOURCES; both files are themselves NO-COUNTERPART C-heap-free artifacts. No behavioral content.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — INDUCTOR_PARAM_DEFS (the defineModelParams secondary group). The v41 `IOPA("dia", IND_MOD_DIA, IF_REAL, ...)` row introduces a new model-level parameter `dia` (cylindrical-inductor diameter). digiTS's INDUCTOR_PARAM_DEFS has no model-level params at all (only instance-level inductance/IC/TC1/TC2/TNOM/SCALE/M); the dia parameter belongs to the broader missing geometry/turns model (Q-IND-MODEL).
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/ind.c:44-51 (INDmPTable). Slice ind.md:80-87 adds one model-param row: IOPA("dia", IND_MOD_DIA, IF_REAL, "Inductor diameter"). digiTS counterpart: INDUCTOR_PARAM_DEFS.secondary.dia (inductor.ts:128) { default:0.0, unit:'m', spiceName:'dia' } sits between csect (ind.c:46) and length (ind.c:48) in the same row order. The MUTpTable IOPR->IOPAPR change (ind.md:96-97) is IF_REDIRECT parser-flag plumbing with no digiTS counterpart (planning ind.c#h003 NO-COUNTERPART). re-derived independently: IND_MOD_DIA enum value confirmed via inddefs.h:421 (IND_MOD_DIA follows IND_MOD_CSECT=104, so =105). Blocked-by ind#recon/modelParams (APPLIED). Source-only per VERIFICATION.md sec1.

items.rationale: Removes the `#ifdef MUTUAL` preprocessor guard at the file-mid boundary between the INDmPTable size declarations and the MUTpTable definition. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS has no preprocessor and no MUTUAL feature-flag — mutual inductance is unconditionally available via the MutualInductorElement class. No behavioral content.

MUTpTable IFparm row for the `coefficient` alias changes from IOPR to IOPAPR — the macro that adds the IF_REDIRECT flag so ngspice's generic ask/set machinery redirects the alias name onto the canonical MUT_COEFF id. Also removes the `#endif /*MUTUAL*/` closing guard at the file tail. digiTS keys parameters by name string (mutual-inductor.ts paramDefs primary key `K` with alias logic handled in setParam at mutual-inductor.ts:168-173); it has no integer-id IFparm table, no parser-driven generic ask/set, and no IF_REDIRECT flag. The hunk is C parser-table plumbing with no behavioral content — PLANNING.md §5 case (a). No parameter is added or removed and no default changes.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — AnalogInductorElement.stampAc (inductor.ts:491-505). The five matrix-pointer dereferences map to solver.stampElement / solver.stampElementImag calls on the five _hPIbr/_hNIbr/_hIbrN/_hIbrP/_hIbrIbr handles allocated in setup() (inductor.ts:274-278). The hunk is the IND-side linked-list-walk accessor rename plus the five matrix-pointer ptr→Ptr capitalisation renames; arithmetic and control flow are unchanged.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/indacld.c:16-40 (INDacLoad). Hunk ind.md:111-138 is the model/instance accessor renames (model->INDnextModel -> INDnextModel(model); model->INDinstances -> INDinstances(model); here->INDnextInstance -> INDnextInstance(here)) plus the five 'ptr'->'Ptr' field-capitalisation renames (INDposIbrptr/INDnegIbrptr/INDibrPosptr/INDibrNegptr/INDibrIbrptr -> ...Ptr). Zero-delta rule satisfied: every +/- line is a VERIFICATION.md sec4 allowed identifier rename; no arithmetic change. Tier2 bijective vs indacld.c:27-35: m=INDm <-> inductor.ts:923 const m via this._M; val=CKTomega*INDinduct/m <-> stampAc:923 const val=omega*this._effectiveL/this._M (operand order omega*L/m, C left-to-right ((omega*L)/m), NOT factored omega*(L/m) -- Directive 2 compliant); the 4 real +-1 connectivity stamps + the imaginary `*(INDibrIbrPtr+1) -= val` (indacld.c:35) <-> stampElementImag(this._hIbrIbr, -val) (inductor.ts:934). Alloc lives in setup() (5 allocElement, indsetup.c:112-116 order), matching INDsetup/INDacLoad function boundary. HARNESS GATE part: rlc-oscillator + sine-transformer AC-path inductors run bit-exact in tran (see device-completion gate note on indsetup.c#h004).

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — class AnalogInductorElement (instance-field declarations at inductor.ts:206-220). The portable body of this hunk is the new `double INDinductinst` field — the raw inductance value as supplied on the L-card, before any TC/SCALE/M derating. digiTS already stores this in `_nominalL` (inductor.ts:206, 234, 312). The `#define MUTUAL` removal, GENinstance generic-struct embedding accessor macros, `const` qualifier on INDposNode/INDnegNode, and ptr→Ptr matrix-pointer renames are C struct/preprocessor plumbing — see Q-IND-INDUCTINST in the review on whether the existing _nominalL is the structural counterpart of INDinductinst.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/inddefs.h:25-77 (struct sINDinstance). Slice ind.md:143-192 is GENinstance struct-embedding + accessor-macro defines (INDmodPtr/INDnextInstance/INDname/INDstate as gen.* macros), const node-id qualifiers, the 5 'ptr'->'Ptr' matrix-pointer renames, the new `double INDinductinst` field (inddefs.h:39), and the new system/system_next_ind/system_idx fields, plus #define MUTUAL removal. digiTS has no C struct; the portable content maps: INDinductinst -> _nominalL (inductor.ts:282, Directive 1 field pair, no new field), INDinduct -> _effectiveL (inductor.ts:287), system/system_next_ind/system_idx -> _system/_systemNextInd/_systemIdx (inductor.ts:359-361). The struct-mechanics +/- lines (GENinstance embed, const, ptr->Ptr macro spellings) are VERIFICATION.md sec4 C->TS surface form with no TS struct to diff against. Re-derived independently. Q-IND-INDUCTINST resolved per ind-review sec6e Directive 1 (confirmed structural-match: _nominalL is the INDinductinst counterpart).

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — class AnalogInductorElement (instance-field declarations at inductor.ts:216-220). The portable content of this hunk is the five matrix-pointer field-name capitalisation renames `INDposIbrptr/INDnegIbrptr/INDibrNegptr/INDibrPosptr/INDibrIbrptr` → `INDposIbrPtr/...`. digiTS holds opaque Norton-stamp handles (the five _h* fields from solver.allocElement), not raw double* matrix pointers. The hunk also includes a whitespace-only `INDinitCond` comment alignment. See Q-IND-INDUCTINST/Q-IND-MODEL on the broader field set.

items.rationale: Whole-file deletion of INDdelete — a C-heap free (FREE(here)) that unlinks one INDinstance from a linked list. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; element instances drop by reference. No allocator, no destructor, no linked list. The file is deleted in v41.

Whole-file deletion of INDdestroy — a C-heap free loop releasing every INDmodel and INDinstance. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. The file is deleted in v41.

C extern-prototype header — PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact. The hunk only removes the extern declarations of INDdelete/INDdestroy/INDmDelete, the three deleted IND-side C-heap-free functions. digiTS has no prototype headers; method signatures are TypeScript class members. No surviving declaration is changed — no new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

C extern-prototype header — PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact. The hunk only removes the extern declarations of MUTdelete/MUTdestroy/MUTmDelete, the three deleted MUT-side C-heap-free functions. digiTS has no prototype headers. No surviving declaration is changed.

SPICEdev INDinfo + MUTinfo registration dispatch structs, converted to C99 designated initialisers. PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note + §5 case (a): digiTS has no SPICEdev table — registration is the InductorDefinition / MutualInductorDefinition objects and method binding is the AnalogInductorElement / MutualInductorElement classes. Reviewed hunk-by-hunk per the glue note: every surviving function-pointer assignment that was non-NULL in v26 stays non-NULL and points to the same handler (INDparam, INDmParam, INDload, INDsetup, INDunsetup, INDtemp, INDtrunc, INDacLoad, INDpzLoad, INDask, INDmAsk, INDsSetup, INDsLoad, INDsUpdate, INDsAcLoad, INDsPrint, MUTparam, MUTsetup, MUTtemp, MUTacLoad, MUTask, MUTpzLoad, MUTsSetup, MUTsPrint). The non-cosmetic changes are DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete/DEVdelete set to NULL on both INDinfo and MUTinfo (the deleted heap functions). No new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — model-parameter getter path. digiTS has no SPICE model-parameter ask API and no model-level parameters on the inductor; the hunk is dominated by (a) cosmetic whitespace/brace reformatting and (b) a new case IND_MOD_DIA returning here->INDdia. items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — file-scope `Math.PI` reference (PI is digiTS's built-in `Math.PI`; no `const PI` declaration needed) and the module-private `Lundin(l, csec)` helper function declared at file-scope before AnalogInductorElement. The Lundin helper is the digiTS counterpart of ngspice's file-static `Lundin(double l, double csec)`; its forward-declaration role in C disappears in TypeScript (hoisted functions are visible to earlier callers within the module). Blocked by ind#recon/modelParams — the Lundin helper does not exist on the v26 baseline digiTS lacks, so the hunk applies as the (effectively) no-op preamble of the model-layer reconstruction once that lands.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/indsetup.c:13-15. Slice ind.md:1208-1218 adds `#define PI 3.141592654` (indsetup.c:13) + `static double Lundin(double l, double csec);` forward declaration. digiTS counterpart: const PI = 3.141592654 (inductor.ts:71) and function Lundin(l, csec) (inductor.ts:78). PI-LITERAL CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING: digiTS uses the truncated literal 3.141592654, NOT Math.PI (3.141592653589793); a Math.PI substitution in csect=PI*dia^2/4 produces a 4.1e-14 absolute csect divergence (verified numerically) -- a Math.PI substitution would be a MISMATCH; it is correctly avoided. Blocked-by ind#recon/modelParams (APPLIED). Re-derived independently from fresh indsetup.c read.
