items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — AnalogInductorElement.setup, the model-level geometry-and-turns derivation. The v41 hunk replaces the v26 specInd computation: (1) changes `INDmuGiven` default 0.0 → 1.0; (2) adds `if (model->INDdiaGiven) { model->INDcsect = PI * INDdia * INDdia / 4.; }` (cylindrical-inductor csect derivation); (3) drops the now-redundant `if (INDmuGiven) ... else ...` branch in specInd formula in favour of unconditional `INDmu·CONSTmuZero·INDcsect/INDlength` (the v41 formula uses `INDcsect` not `INDcsect·INDcsect` — also a behavioral change); (4) adds the `Lundin's geometry correction factor` multiplication; (5) drops the commented-out fprintf debug; (6) adds `if (!INDmIndGiven) INDmInd = INDmodNt² · INDspecInd` outside the `INDlengthGiven` branch (it was inside the else in v26). All five sub-changes touch the model-side geometry/turns derivation that digiTS lacks entirely (Q-IND-MODEL).
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/indsetup.c:56-85 (the v41 specInd reformulation). This is the load-bearing geometry hunk. Slice ind.md:1249-1317 replaces the v26 two-branch csect^2 specInd with: (1) INDmu default 1.0 not 0.0 (indsetup.c:57, ind.md:1253-1254 '-INDmu=0.0' / '+INDmu=1.0'); (2) `if(INDdiaGiven) INDcsect = PI*INDdia*INDdia/4.;` (indsetup.c:61-63); (3) single-csect specInd `INDspecInd = (INDmu*CONSTmuZero*INDcsect)/INDlength` (indsetup.c:67-68) -- NOT v26 csect*csect, NOT a muGiven/!muGiven branch (single unconditional branch, mu defaults 1.0); (4) `if(INDlengthGiven && (INDdiaGiven||INDcsectGiven)) INDspecInd *= Lundin(INDlength,INDcsect)` (indsetup.c:74-75); (5) `if(!INDmIndGiven) INDmInd = INDmodNt*INDmodNt*INDspecInd` (indsetup.c:84-85). digiTS _deriveSpecIndAndMInd (inductor.ts:562-584) is construct-bijective LINE-FOR-LINE: !_muGiven->_mu=1.0 (inductor.ts:514-516); _diaGiven->_csect=PI*this._dia*this._dia/4.0 (inductor.ts:564-566, operand order PI*dia*dia/4 preserved); _lengthGiven&&_length>0 -> _specInd=(this._mu*CONST_MU_ZERO*this._csect)/this._length (inductor.ts:570, SINGLE csect, no ^2, no mu/!mu branch); _lengthGiven&&(_diaGiven||_csectGiven) -> _specInd*=Lundin(this._length,this._csect) (inductor.ts:576-577); !_mIndGiven -> _mInd=this._modNt*this._modNt*this._specInd (inductor.ts:581-582). INDEPENDENT NUMERICAL VALIDATION (since the harness cannot drive geometry params -- see device-completion note): from-scratch v41 formula recomputation matches digiTS .inductance output to all printed digits: csect=2e-4,length=0.05,mu=100,modNt=200 -> L=0.017635543505673 (digiTS actual 0.017635543505673); mu-default-1.0 case csect=1e-4,length=0.02,modNt=50 -> 0.000012549024744083752 (digiTS actual matches); instance-nt csect=2e-4,length=0.05,mu=100,instNt=10 -> 0.0000440888587641825 (digiTS actual matches); csect-doubling ratio 1.9266 (v41 single-csect+Lundin, NOT v26's 4.0) confirmed produced by digiTS. items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — AnalogInductorElement.setup, the instance-loop iteration site (inductor.ts:265, 274-278). items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — file-scope (the Lundin function would be a module-private helper) and AnalogInductorElement (no destroy/unsetup method exists on AnalogElement). The hunk covers two unrelated structural changes interleaved by the @@ window: (1) INDunsetup body — accessor rename, brace-cleanup converting `if (here->INDbrEq) { ... }` to `if (here->INDbrEq > 0) ... ; here->INDbrEq = 0;` — INDunsetup is C-side dispatch glue for the branch-equation deallocation (digiTS handles it implicitly via element lifecycle, no counterpart method); (2) the new `static double Lundin(double l, double csec)` definition implementing Nagaoka's coefficient via Lundin's handbook formula — model-level math contingent on Q-IND-MODEL (the missing geometry/turns model). Either resolves to PORT-into-digiTS or accept-as-architectural per the open question.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/indsetup.c:144-173 (the Lundin function body). Slice ind.md:1344-1399 adds `static double Lundin(double l, double csec)` -- the Nagaoka/Lundin geometry correction. digiTS counterpart: function Lundin(l, csec) (inductor.ts:78-108) is operand-for-operand v41: the 1um floor guard `if (csec < 1e-12 || l < 1e-6) return 1` (inductor.ts:83-87 <-> indsetup.c:151-155); x = sqrt(csec/PI)*2./l (inductor.ts:90 <-> indsetup.c:157, using the truncated PI literal); xx=x*x, xxxx=xx*xx (inductor.ts:93-94 <-> indsetup.c:159-160); slender branch x<1: num=1+0.383901*xx+0.017108*xxxx, den=1+0.258952*xx, return num/den-4*x/(3*PI) (inductor.ts:98-100 <-> indsetup.c:163-165); short branch else: num=(log(4*x)-0.5)*(1+0.383901/xx+0.017108/xxxx), den=1+0.258952/xx, kk=0.093842/xx+0.002029/xxxx-0.000801/(xx*xxxx), return 2*(num/den+kk)/(PI*x) (inductor.ts:103-106 <-> indsetup.c:168-172). Every coefficient, operand, operator, and grouping identical; both branches present; PI = the truncated literal not Math.PI. log->Math.log, sqrt->Math.sqrt, fprintf(stderr)->console.warn (VERIFICATION.md sec4 surface form). INDEPENDENT NUMERICAL VALIDATION: Lundin(0.05,2e-4)=... folded into the specInd recomputation above (test1/3 match to all digits), Lundin(0.1,3.141592654e-4)=0.9200911773358132 for the dia case. items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — AnalogInductorElement.computeTemperature (inductor.ts:301-308), the base-inductance-selection block. The v41 `else here->INDinduct = here->INDinductinst;` adds the new behavioral branch resetting the base inductance to the raw instance-line value (INDinductinst, ngspice counterpart of digiTS _nominalL) before the TC/SCALE fold. digiTS's computeTemperature ALREADY achieves the same effect via `_effectiveL = _nominalL * factor * SCALE / M` (it always reads from _nominalL and recomputes _effectiveL fresh, never compounds); the v26 indtemp.c by contrast COMPOUNDS the TC/SCALE/M fold on successive INDtemp calls. Whether digiTS's separate _nominalL/_effectiveL fields are the structural counterpart of v41's INDinductinst-reset is Q-IND-INDUCTINST. The hunk also adds an `else if (here->INDntGiven) here->INDinduct = model->INDspecInd · INDnt²` model-level branch — the latter is contingent on Q-IND-MODEL.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/indtemp.c:49-56 (base-inductance selection). Slice ind.md:1595-1604 adds the v41 `else here->INDinduct = here->INDinductinst;` reset branch (indtemp.c:55-56) to the existing !INDindGiven if/else-if (INDntGiven->specInd*nt^2 / else->INDmInd). digiTS counterpart: computeTemperature base selection (inductor.ts:638-650) -- if(!this._indGiven){ if(this._instanceNtGiven) base=this._specInd*this._instanceNt*this._instanceNt (inductor.ts:642 <-> indtemp.c:50-51) else base=this._mInd (inductor.ts:645 <-> indtemp.c:52-53) } else base=this._nominalL (inductor.ts:649 <-> indtemp.c:56 INDinduct=INDinductinst). Directive 3 confirmed: the else=INDinductinst reset maps to the RHS read of _nominalL into `base` (rebuild-from-base, not in-place mutate); _effectiveL is recomputed at inductor.ts:666 base*factor*SCALE, never *=. Blocked-by ind#recon/modelParams (APPLIED). Q-IND-INDUCTINST resolved per Directive 3. Re-derived independently.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/inductor.ts — AnalogInductorElement.computeTemperature (inductor.ts:301-308), the tail of the temperature pass after the TC/SCALE fold. The v41 hunk REMOVES the v26 trailing `here->INDinduct = here->INDinduct / here->INDm;` line; in v41 the `/m` is now done at the load() and stampAc() stamp sites (indload.c:43-49 `INDinduct/m`, indacld.c:29 `INDinduct/m`, indpzld.c:30 `INDinduct/m`). digiTS currently folds `/M` inside `_effectiveL` (inductor.ts:245, 307, 313, 329, 332) — which matches the v26 indtemp.c behavior. The v41 change moves the /M fold from indtemp into the per-stamp sites; whether digiTS should follow (Q-IND-LDIVM) or keep the construction-time /M fold is an open question.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/indtemp.c:72-74 (TC/SCALE fold, /M removal). Slice ind.md:1605-1612 REMOVES the v26 trailing `here->INDinduct = here->INDinduct / here->INDm;` (ind.md:1609 '-' line), leaving only `here->INDinduct = here->INDinduct * factor * here->INDscale;` (indtemp.c:74). This is a REAL arithmetic relocation, NOT a cosmetic zero-delta: /M moves from computeTemperature to the per-stamp sites. digiTS counterpart: computeTemperature ends at _effectiveL = base * factor * this._SCALE (inductor.ts:666) with NO /M (Directive 2); /M present at every stamp in v41 operand order: loadFluxInit L/m (inductor.ts:783,786), load newmind=L/m (inductor.ts:856), stampAc omega*_effectiveL/_M (inductor.ts:923). Directive 2 compliant: the v26 /M removal is applied as a non-empty arithmetic relocation, NOT waved through as zero-delta. factor=1.0+tc1*difference+tc2*difference*difference (inductor.ts:661 <-> indtemp.c:72). Re-derived independently. HARNESS GATE: RL-step/RLC/sine-transformer all bit-exact prove the /M-at-stamp relocation is numerically correct vs ngspice (which has /M at indload.c:43/112, indacld.c:29).

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/mutual-inductor.ts — MutualInductorElement.computeTemperature (mutual-inductor.ts:140-144) and module-private helpers. Port content of this hunk has two parts. (1) Always-on MUTfactor change: add `Math.abs(ind1*ind2)` inside the sqrt in MutualInductorElement.computeTemperature, MutualInductorElement.recomputeMutFactor (mutual-inductor.ts:146-150), and MutualInductorElement.setup (mutual-inductor.ts:112-114) — mirrors ngspice's `fabs(ind1*ind2)` guard against negative inductances. (2) CKTindverbosity-gated diagnostic subsystem (INDsystem linked-list bookkeeping, cholesky() helper, positive-definite verification with stderr warnings on duplicate K, incomplete coupling, negative L, |K|>1) — this whole subsystem is absent on the v26 baseline and is blocked by mut#recon/inductiveSystemVerify. Once the reconstruction lands the v41 hunk applies as the per-stamp deltas onto the rebuilt v26 baseline.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/muttemp.c:13-218 (cholesky + MUTtemp + INDsystem verify subsystem). Slice ind.md:2049-2277. The leaf fabs change: v26 `MUTfactor = MUTcoupling * sqrt(ind1 * ind2)` -> v41 `MUTfactor = MUTcoupling * sqrt(fabs(ind1 * ind2))` (muttemp.c:56). digiTS counterpart: MUTfactor = this._coupling * Math.sqrt(Math.abs(this._partner1.inductance * this._partner2.inductance)) at every site (mutual-inductor.ts:208-210 setup, 249-251 computeTemperature, 256-258 recomputeMutFactor) -- fabs->Math.abs, operand order ind1*ind2 preserved, sqrt->Math.sqrt. The CKTindverbosity-gated INDsystem Cholesky positive-definite verification subsystem (muttemp.c:13-32 cholesky + muttemp.c:58-205 the verify driver) is the mut#recon/inductiveSystemVerify reconstruction (APPLIED), whose verifier confirmed cholesky (mutual-inductor.ts:66-83) + the 5-way merge chain + matrix assembly + waiver + emit gate are construct-bijective with muttemp.c and ran the harness gate on transformer/tapped-transformer. Blocked-by mut#recon/inductiveSystemVerify (APPLIED). Re-derived independently.

items.rationale: Automake source-list fragment. C build system; digiTS registers via src/core/registry.ts and has no autotools build. PLANNING.md §5 case (a). The hunk only removes the isrcdest.c and isrcmdel.c entries - both themselves NO-COUNTERPART (deleted C-heap files).

Author-line file-header comment context only; the @@ hunk header carries the `Author: 1987 Thomas L. Quarles` comment. The actual changed lines are the IFparm ISRCpTable rows in h002/h003. This stanza is the parser's header-attributed slice and carries no code; PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C source comment with no behavioral content.

IFparm ISRCpTable parameter-name/id table rows. digiTS has no IFparm registration table - params are keyed by name string via defineModelParams. This hunk adds the `c` current alias (IOPPR), reorders the `sine`/`sin` aliases (IOPR redirect), and changes `external` from IF_REALVEC to IF_STRING. The parser-flag/redirect plumbing (IOPPR/IOPR/IF_STRING) has no digiTS counterpart; the `current` readout it advertises is realized structurally by getPinCurrents, not a table row. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C dispatch-table plumbing.

Continuation of the IFparm ISRCpTable rewrite: relocates the `c` (ISRC_DC) and `current` (ISRC_CURRENT) readout rows out of the XSPICE #ifdef into the unconditional table. Same as h002 - IFparm table plumbing with no digiTS registration-table counterpart; the `current`/`v`/`p` readouts are computed by element accessors, not advertised through an IFparm row. PLANNING.md §5 case (a).
Adds `#include "ngspice/compatmode.h"`. C include-directive plumbing supporting the newcompat.xs branch in h002-h005; digiTS has no compatmode header. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact.

items.rationale: Frozen sub-item of the isrcacct.c#h004 split: the newcompat.xs compatibility-mode PULSE-phase arm — the removed XSPICE phase-normalization block and the added `if (newcompat.xs) { phase=PHASE/360; phase=fmod(phase,1); deltat=phase*PER; while(deltat>0) deltat-=PER; time+=deltat; tshift=TD-deltat; } else if (PHASE>0.0) { tmax=PHASE*PER; }` plus the `if (!newcompat.xs && time > tmax) {/*nothing*/} else {` gate. newcompat is ngspice's emulation of foreign SPICE dialects (PSPICE/LTspice/HSPICE/Spice2-3, ngspice/compatmode.h), gated by `set ngbehavior`/`.option`; it only changes the PULSE phase convention when a compat flag is set. digiTS has no compat-mode system (no ngbehavior/.option parser, no newcompat global; grep across src/ = zero hits) and foreign-dialect emulation is out of scope for a browser logic-sim. NO-COUNTERPART matching the ratified VSRC precedent (vsrcload.c#h004 carved out NO-COUNTERPART for the same newcompat.xs construct). The non-compat baseline PULSE breakpoint logic is the portable path and lands via the coeff engine in the #h004a/#h004c portable sub-items.

In digiTS the non-noise path is the only counterpart: acReal/acImag are computed inline from _acMagnitude/_acPhase (ac-current-source.ts:626-628), and the four stamps read this._M*acReal / this._M*acImag (ac:630-633). items.hunkHeader: @@ -92,13 +92,14 @@ ISRCask(CKTcircuit *ckt, GENinstance *inst, int which, IFvalue *value, IFvalue *
items.enclosingContext: ISRCask(CKTcircuit *ckt, GENinstance *inst, int which, IFvalue *value, IFvalue *
items.rationale: ISRCask is the SPICE IFparm value getter (integer-keyed `which` switch returning ISRCcurrent for ISRC_CURRENT, no-op for ISRC_EXTERNAL). digiTS has no IFparm ask API - parameter readout is via element accessors / getPinCurrents, not an ask switch. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C dispatch artifact. The hunk only reshapes the XSPICE/SHARED_MODULE preprocessor guards around the two cases.

sISRCinstance head rewrite: replaces the hand-rolled ISRCmodPtr/ISRCnextInstance/ISRCname/ISRCstate members with `struct GENinstance gen` embedding + GEN-macro accessor #defines, and adds `const` qualifiers to the node ids. C struct-layout + generic-struct-embedding mechanics; digiTS instances are TypeScript classes with no GENinstance base, no linked-list pointers, no IFuid name field. PLANNING.md §5 case (a). The one behavioral member nearby (ISRCcurrent) is covered by h002.

Unguards the ISRCcurrent member (removes its XSPICE #ifdef) and rewrites the sISRCmodel head to embed `struct GENmodel gen` with GEN-macro accessors. The ISRCcurrent readout is realized in digiTS by AcCurrentSourceAnalogImpl.getPinCurrents (computed I, not a stored struct member); the rest is C generic-struct-embedding mechanics. No StatePool slot exists for isrc (RHS-only stamp, no state). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) struct-layout artifact.

Converts the source-function-type macros (PULSE..EXTERNAL) and the device-parameter id macros (ISRC_DC..ISRC_EXTERNAL) from #define to C enum, with byte-identical numeric values. Pure C-preprocessor reformat of integer ids that key the IFparm dispatch table; digiTS has no IFparm id namespace (params are name-keyed). PLANNING.md §5 case (a). The source-type ids drive the coeff-array waveform engine, which is reconstructed by isrc#recon/coeffWaveforms - but the enum-vs-#define id constants themselves have no digiTS representation.

ISRCdelete rewritten from a linked-list-unlink-and-FREE into a per-instance FREE of ISRCcoeffs / trnoise_state / trrandom_state. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS is GC-managed; no heap free, no destructor, no instance linked list. Every line of the new and old body is C heap bookkeeping.
Whole-file deletion of ISRCdestroy, a C-heap free loop releasing every ISRCmodel and ISRCinstance. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor.

C extern-prototype header. Case (a) C-language artifact per PLANNING.md §5: digiTS has no prototype headers - signatures are TypeScript class members. The hunk rewrites the ISRCdelete prototype to the GENinstance* signature and removes the ISRCdestroy/ISRCmDelete prototypes (deleted heap functions); no surviving declaration maps to digiTS.

SPICEdev ISRCinfo dispatch struct converted to C99 designated initialisers. digiTS registration is the DcCurrentSourceDefinition / AcCurrentSourceDefinition objects; method binding is the element classes. Reviewed per PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note: every non-NULL function-pointer slot in v26 stays non-NULL in v41 (DEVparam/DEVload/DEVtemperature/DEVacLoad/DEVaccept/DEVask), the only changes are DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete set to NULL (deleted heap functions) and the cosmetic .DEVinstSize/.DEVmodSize designators. No new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) dispatch glue.

Adds `#include "ngspice/compatmode.h"`. C include-directive plumbing supporting the newcompat.xs PULSE branch in h003-h005; digiTS has no compatmode header. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact.

Sibling of the already-NC isrcload.c#h014 / isrcpar.c#h003/#h004.

TRNOISE source-waveform value arm of ISRCload: the timezero reset state machine + n1=floor(time/TS), V1=trnoise_state_get(state,ckt,n1), V2=...(n1+1), value=V1+(V2-V1)*(time/TS-n1)+RTS+DC 1/f-and-white interpolation. This is the trnoise_state noise subsystem, out of scope for a browser digital-logic simulator (user ruling 2026-06-02, ruled 3x). The trnoise_state lifecycle (trnoise_state_init/_get/_free, f_alpha 1/f synthesizer, timezero/top state, RTS trap) is tracked separately as the noise subsystem task (ESC-24-noise); maths-misc#recon/randnumb delivered only SeededRng primitives, not trnoise_state. NO-COUNTERPART: the TRNOISE/TRRANDOM source-waveform arms have no digiTS counterpart and are not reconstructed under isrc#recon/coeffWaveforms.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/njfet.ts::NJFETElement (JFET AC small-signal load path)
verifierNotes: MISMATCH (Tier 2). ngspice ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/jfet/jfetacld.c:16-73 (JFETacLoad), v41 hunk jfet.md docLineRange 80-93 (hunkHash 4ad72ce944da6249). Our file: src/components/semiconductors/njfet.ts. TIER1 of the assigned hunk h001 PASSES in isolation: the hunk's only change is the loop-accessor macro rename (model->JFETnextModel to JFETnextModel(model); model->JFETinstances/here->JFETnextInstance to JFETinstances(model)/JFETnextInstance(here)), which is iteration plumbing so a zero-line TS delta that is line-isomorphic per VERIFICATION.md sec4. BUT TIER 2 FAILS: the functionGroup is jfetacld.c::JFETacLoad (JFET AC small-signal load path), and the whole JFETacLoad function HAS NO digiTS COUNTERPART. NJFETElement implements no stampAc method (search stampAc in njfet.ts = 0 matches), whereas the AC analysis driver calls element.stampAc(solver,omega,loadCtx,rhsRe,rhsIm) per element (ac-analysis.ts:376 runByDeviceFamily('stampAc',...)) and elements without stampAc are SILENTLY SKIPPED (default-loaders.ts:60-61). ngspice's JFET DEVacLoad is JFETacLoad (NOT NULL), so the 'NULL implies skip' equivalence does not apply: a JFET in an AC sweep currently contributes ZERO to the complex matrix. JFETacLoad stamps 22 admittance terms with both real and imaginary parts: real conductances gdpr/gspr and state0 gm/gds/ggs/ggd (jfetacld.c:36-42,47-68), AND the omega-scaled junction-charge susceptances xgs = state0[JFETqgs]*CKTomega and xgd = state0[JFETqgd]*CKTomega stamped into the +1 (imaginary) cells (jfetacld.c:41,43,49,52,54,57,59,63,66). None of these are produced by digiTS. The sibling ported devices DO implement this: bjt.ts, mosfet.ts, vdmos.ts all have stampAc methods (njfet.ts absent). The jfet planning review (spec/v41-port/planning/jfet-review.md:60-62) FROZE the scope as 'DC operating point, transient, AND AC small-signal' and asserted (line 92) 'AC is implemented' for jfetacld.c; that assertion is FALSE for JFET (bad baseline / Tier-2 trap): no AC small-signal path exists in njfet.ts. REMEDY (applier, within functionGroup so not an escalation per sec6): add a stampAc(solver,omega,loadCtx,rhsRe,rhsIm) method to NJFETElement in njfet.ts that mirrors JFETacLoad jfetacld.c:36-68 line-by-line; read gdpr=drainConduct*area, gspr=sourceConduct*area, gm/gds/ggs/ggd from state0 slots GM/GDS/GGS/GGD, xgs=state0[QGS]*omega, xgd=state0[QGD]*omega, and stamp all 22 real/imag terms via the cached TSTALLOC handles (reuse the bjt/mosfet stampAc pattern). The handles already exist in setup() (_hGG,_hGDP,_hGSP,_hDPG,_hDPDP,_hDPSP,_hSPG,_hSPDP,_hSPSP,_hDDP,_hDPD,_hDD,_hSSP,_hSPS,_hSS). After porting, re-verify h001 Tier 2 against jfetacld.c.

APPLIER ROUND 2 (attempt 1): Fixed the named Tier-2 divergence. Added NJFETElement.stampAc(solver,omega,_ctx,_rhsRe,_rhsIm) to src/components/semiconductors/njfet.ts mirroring jfetacld.c:36-68 line-by-line: gdpr=(RD>0?1/RD:0)*AREA, gspr=(RS>0?1/RS:0)*AREA, gm/gds/ggs/ggd from s0[base+SLOT_GM/GDS/GGS/GGD], xgs=s0[base+SLOT_QGS]*omega, xgd=s0[base+SLOT_QGD]*omega, m=params.M. All 22 terms stamped in source order via cached handles _hDD/_hGG/_hSS/_hDPDP/_hSPSP/_hDDP/_hGDP/_hGSP/_hSSP/_hDPD/_hDPG/_hDPSP/_hSPG/_hSPS/_hSPDP; += real via stampElement, +=(+1 imag) via stampElementImag, each ngspice -= rendered as stamp of -(m*(expr)) since the solver primitive is additive-only (negation is exact for IEEE doubles, preserving operand order). Also added: import type SparseSolverStamp. The hunk h001 macro-rename remains a zero-line delta (sec4 plumbing); the body is the new method. tsc: only 3 pre-existing TS6133 unused-field warnings (_egGiven/_icVDSGiven/_icVGSGiven), present in HEAD, not introduced here and out of this functionGroup scope. Ready for re-verification.

VERIFIER ROUND 2: MATCH. v41 ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/jfet/jfetacld.c:16-73 (JFETacLoad) vs src/components/semiconductors/njfet.ts:930-976 (NJFETElement.stampAc). TIER 1 (hunk h001, jfet.md docLineRange 80-93): the only ngspice change is the loop-accessor macro rename (model->JFETnextModel->JFETnextModel(model); model->JFETinstances/here->JFETnextInstance->JFETinstances(model)/JFETnextInstance(here)) — sec4-allowed iteration plumbing replaced by the AC-driver per-element dispatch; zero-line TS delta is line-isomorphic per VERIFICATION.md sec4. PASS. TIER 2 (bijective): independently re-derived the identifier correspondence from source (not consumed from rename-map). gdpr=JFETdrainConduct*JFETarea -> (RD>0?1/RD:0)*AREA, gspr likewise; gm/gds/ggs/ggd from state0 JFETgm/gds/ggs/ggd -> s0[base+SLOT_GM/GDS/GGS/GGD]; xgs=state0[JFETqgs]*CKTomega -> s0[base+SLOT_QGS]*omega, xgd likewise (SLOT_GM=5,GDS=6,GGS=7,GGD=8,QGS=9,QGD=11 confirmed against load() which sets s0[GM..GGD]=gm..ggd at njfet.ts:872-875 and s0[QGS/QGD] at 763-791); m=JFETm->params.M. All 22 admittance stamps present in jfetacld.c source order with identical operand order/grouping; += -> stampElement (real half), +1 += -> stampElementImag (imag +1 half per SparseSolverStamp:64-73, _elValImag), each -= rendered as stamp of -(m*(expr)) (additive-only primitive; unary negate exact for IEEE doubles, operand order inside parens preserved). Handle->cell mapping verified independently against setup() allocElement calls (njfet.ts:442-456): _hDD=(d,d),_hGG=(g,g),_hSS=(s,s),_hDPDP=(dp,dp),_hSPSP=(sp,sp),_hDDP=(d,dp),_hGDP=(g,dp),_hGSP=(g,sp),_hSSP=(s,sp),_hDPD=(dp,d),_hDPG=(dp,g),_hDPSP=(dp,sp),_hSPG=(sp,g),_hSPS=(sp,s),_hSPDP=(sp,dp) — matches each JFET*Ptr. stampAc(solver,omega,_ctx,_rhsRe,_rhsIm) signature mirrors mosfet.ts:1719; JFETacLoad writes no RHS so unused _rhsRe/_rhsIm is correct. Bijection complete: every v41 construct maps to a stampAc construct and vice versa; no v41 construct without counterpart, no extra digiTS construct.

items.rationale: Whole-file deletion of JFETdelete - a C-heap FREE(here) that unlinks one JFETinstance from a linked list and frees it. digiTS is GC-managed; there is no allocator, no destructor, and no instance linked list. File deleted in v41. PLANNING.md §5 case (a).
Whole-file deletion of JFETdestroy - a C-heap FREE() loop releasing every JFETmodel and JFETinstance. digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. File deleted in v41. PLANNING.md §5 case (a).

JFETdisto - the distortion-analysis stamp (includes distodef.h, dereferences DISTOAN* job from CKTcurJob, reads CKTomega, computes Volterra harmonic responses). Distortion analysis is NOT implemented in digiTS (analysis-scope.md: no distortion engine file under src/solver/analog/, no distortion AnalogEngine method, no distortion MCP tool). The whole function belongs to distortion. PLANNING.md §5 case (b).

JFETdSetup - distortion setup (includes distodef.h; precomputes the gm2/gm3/cross-derivative Volterra-kernel coefficients consumed by JFETdisto). Distortion analysis is NOT implemented (analysis-scope.md). This hunk removes the unused `temp` local. PLANNING.md §5 case (b).
JFETdSetup - distortion setup. Accessor-macro rename (JFETnextModel/JFETinstances) inside the distortion-only JFETdSetup. Distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md). PLANNING.md §5 case (b).

JFETdSetup - distortion setup. The SWAP(double, vgs, vgd) macro substitution inside the distortion-only JFETdSetup vds<0 inverse-mode branch. Distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md). PLANNING.md §5 case (b).

C extern-prototype header. The hunk only removes the extern decls of JFETdelete/JFETdestroy/JFETmDelete (the 3 deleted C-heap functions). digiTS has no prototype headers - method signatures are TS class members. No surviving declaration changes. PLANNING.md §5 case (a).

verifierNotes: MATCH. Tier1+Tier2 vs fresh ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/jfet/jfetmpar.c:72-80 (JFETmParam), v41 hunk jfet.md docLineRange 775-789. KF/AF givenness (ngspice JFETfNcoefGiven/JFETfNexpGiven) is consumed ONLY by jfetset.c defaulting (fNcoef=0,fNexp=1), realized in digiTS by param defaults KF=0/AF=1 (njfet.ts:91-92), so no *Given tracking is required for KF/AF. TIER2: the case removal/relocation is position-only; net JFETmParam handles the same set of params, all mapped in setParam. FILE-SCOPE: setParam committed at recon c8dbef88; no stray file. Not recon/device-completion so sec1a N/A.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/njfet.ts::computeJfetTempParams (tThreshold/tBeta; njfet.ts:260-261)
verifierNotes: MATCH. Tier1+Tier2 vs fresh ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/jfet/jfettemp.c:115-124 (tThreshold/tBeta temperature scaling), v41 hunk jfet.md docLineRange 1081-1100. Independent re-derivation: v26 'tThreshold = threshold - tcv*(temp-tnom); tBeta = beta*pow(temp/tnom,bex)' to v41 'if (JFETvtotcGiven) tThreshold = threshold + vtotc*(temp-tnom); else tThreshold = threshold - tcv*(temp-tnom); if (JFETbetatceGiven) tBeta = beta*pow(1.01,betatce*(temp-tnom)); else tBeta = beta*pow(temp/tnom,bex)'. digiTS njfet.ts:283-294: if (given.vtotcGiven) tThreshold = p.VTO + p.VTOTC*(temp-p.TNOM); else tThreshold = p.VTO - p.TCV*(temp-p.TNOM); if (given.betatceGiven) tBeta = p.BETA*Math.pow(1.01,p.BETATCE*(temp-p.TNOM)); else tBeta = p.BETA*Math.pow(temp/p.TNOM,p.BEX). TIER1: the 10 added lines (two if/else branches) and 3 removed lines (single-form tThreshold/tBeta) map one-to-one onto the working-tree delta. line-isomorphic. TIER2 (fresh read): vtotc leg + operator with +vtotc*(temp-tnom); tcv leg - operator with -tcv*(temp-tnom); betatce leg pow(1.01, betatce*(temp-tnom)); bex leg pow(temp/tnom, bex); operands/operators/order/grouping identical. FILE-SCOPE: njfet.ts only; no stray file. Not recon/device-completion so sec1a N/A.

items.rationale: MOS3ask integer-id IFvalue getter dispatcher, MOS3_CAPGS case (gate-source cap + overlap). digiTS has no integer-id ask API - params/outputs are read by name from the property bag / getPinCurrents. The hunk is MOS3modPtr(here)-> accessor-macro renames inside this getter case; the underlying quantity exists in the reconstructed device, but the which-id getter mechanism has no digiTS counterpart (same disposition as dio/jfet2 ask/mask).

MOS3ask integer-id IFvalue getter dispatcher, MOS3_CAPGD case (gate-drain cap + overlap). digiTS has no integer-id ask API - params/outputs are read by name from the property bag. The hunk is MOS3modPtr(here)-> accessor-macro renames inside this getter case; the which-id getter mechanism has no digiTS counterpart.

MOS3ask integer-id IFvalue getter dispatcher, MOS3_CAPGB case (gate-bulk cap + overlap). digiTS has no integer-id ask API - params/outputs are read by name from the property bag. The hunk is MOS3modPtr(here)-> accessor-macro renames inside this getter case; the which-id getter mechanism has no digiTS counterpart.

items.tsFunction: src/components/semiconductors/mosfet3.ts :: Mosfet3AnalogElement.checkConvergence (folded inline into load() at the Check-flag site per the unified-interface model)

items.rationale: Mixed hunk, both halves NO-COUNTERPART: (1) adds the MOS3RDNOIZ/MOS3RSNOIZ/MOS3IDNOIZ/MOS3FLNOIZ/MOS3TOTNOIZ/MOS3NSRCS noise-source-index enum - indices into the MOS3nVar noise-density array consumed solely by MOS3noise; noise not implemented (analysis-scope.md). (2) embeds struct GENinstance gen into sMOS3instance and macro-lifts MOS3modPtr/MOS3nextInstance/MOS3name/MOS3states onto gen.GEN*, plus const-qualifies the d/g/s/b node-id fields - pure C struct-layout/dispatch plumbing; digiTS holds instance fields as TS members and state in the reconstructed MOSFET3_SCHEMA StatePool, with no GENinstance base, no IFuid, no instance list.

Removes the old MOS3RDNOIZ..MOS3NSRCS #define block (relocated to h001's enum) and surrounds the NONOISE-guarded MOS3nVar[NSTATVARS][MOS3NSRCS] noise-density array declaration. Both the removed defines and the MOS3nVar array are noise-only constructs; noise not implemented (analysis-scope.md). No consumer in any implemented analysis.

Embeds struct GENmodel gen into sMOS3model and macro-lifts MOS3modType/MOS3nextModel/MOS3instances/MOS3modName onto gen.GEN*, dropping the hand-rolled linked-list fields. Pure C struct-layout/dispatch plumbing; digiTS has no GENmodel base, no model list, no IFuid - the reconstructed device holds model params as TS members.

items.hunkHeader: @@ -421,138 +426,141 @@ typedef struct sMOS3model {       /* model structure for a resistor */
items.enclosingContext: typedef struct sMOS3model {       /* model structure for a resistor */
items.functionGroup: mos3/mos3defs.h::typedef struct sMOS3model {       /* model structure for a resistor */
items.rationale: Device-question and model-parameter id #define->enum reformat (MOS3_W..MOS3_DTEMP and MOS3_MOD_VTO..MOS3_MOD_DELVTO renumbered into C enums, byte-identical values). This is the IFparm dispatch-id table; digiTS has no IFparm ask/param-id integer dispatch - params resolve by name in setParam/paramDefs. No new param, no changed default, no value change (same disposition as jfet2 defs.h#h004).

MOS3delete file-header comment-block removal (drops the empty /* */ block above the includes). Part of the device-teardown file; digiTS has no device teardown (engine rebuilt fresh per compile). No behavioral content.

Rewrites MOS3delete from the v26 linked-list-unlink-and-FREE(here) form to the v41 GENinstance teardown form (MOS3delete(GENinstance *gen_inst) { FREE(inst->MOS3sens); }). digiTS is GC-managed; no allocator, no destructor, no instance list, no MOS3sens sensitivity struct. Device teardown has no digiTS counterpart.
Whole-file deletion of MOS3destroy - a C-heap FREE() loop releasing every MOS3model and MOS3instance. digiTS is GC-managed; no model/instance lists. File deleted in v41.

MOS3disto distortion stamp, entered only under D_TWOF1/D_THRF1/D_F1MF2/D_F1PF2/D_2F1MF2 Volterra modes (CKTcurJob == DISTOAN). Distortion not implemented: no distortion engine file, no distortion AnalogEngine method, no distortion MCP tool (analysis-scope.md). The single hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename inside the distortion-mode-gated loop.
MOS3dSetup precomputes the cross-derivative Volterra kernels consumed by MOS3disto. Distortion not implemented (analysis-scope.md). The single hunk is the linked-list-walk accessor rename.

C extern-prototype header. The hunk removes the extern decls of MOS3destroy and MOS3mDelete (deleted C-heap-free functions) and re-signatures MOS3delete from (GENmodel*,IFuid,GENinstance**) to (GENinstance*) - the device-teardown form. digiTS has no prototype headers; signatures are TS class members. No surviving declaration change with a counterpart.

MOS3getic .ic/UIC initial-condition capture (registered as DEVsetic; seeds MOS3icVBS/icVDS/icVGS from external node voltages when not given). digiTS has no per-element setic/getic engine hook - engine-phase-deferred (non-blocking), same disposition as jfet2 getic (OQ-JFET2-GETIC) and mes getic. The single hunk is the accessor-macro rename over the model/instance walk.

SPICEdev MOS3info dispatch struct converted to C99 designated initialisers. digiTS has no SPICEdev table - registration is the reconstructed Mosfet3N/Mosfet3P component definitions in register-all.ts, and which DEV* slots are non-NULL is captured by which methods the element class implements. Examined hunk-by-hunk: every surviving function-pointer assignment is unchanged v26->v41; the only non-cosmetic change is DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete set NULL (the deleted heap fns). No new slot, default, or capability.

items.rationale: MOS3pzLoad(..., SPcomplex *s) stamps the complex admittance for the pole-zero sweep. Pole-zero not implemented: no PZ method on the AnalogEngine interface, no PZ engine file, no PZ MCP tool (analysis-scope.md). The single hunk is the accessor-macro rename.

MOS3sAcLoad sensitivity AC-load - operates on ckt->CKTsenInfo with SENstatus = PERTURBATION over the state vector. digiTS implements no per-device sensitivity (no CKTsenInfo/SenCond/SENstruct). The hunk is the accessor-macro rename inside the perturbation loop. Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

MOS3sAcLoad sensitivity AC-load, the SENparms iparmno loop. Adds an (int) cast on here->MOS3sens_l in the (MOS3senParmNo + sens_l) sensitivity-parameter index. digiTS has no per-device sensitivity (no SENparms/senParmNo). Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

src/components/semiconductors/mosfet3.ts :: Mosfet3AnalogElement.setup() (instance walk / geometry-IC defaults / prime-node alloc / 22-cell TSTALLOC)

items.rationale: MOS3unsetup node teardown - CKTdltNNum on the drain/source prime nodes with the v41 (>0) delete-order rewrite. digiTS has no device teardown; the engine is rebuilt fresh per compile (no CKTdltNNum analogue, no persistent prime-node pool to release). Same disposition as the delete/destroy/unsetup family.

MOS3sLoad sensitivity load - computes charge sensitivities into the sensitivity tables keyed on info->SENparms/MOS3senParmNo. digiTS implements no per-device sensitivity (no CKTsenInfo/SenCond). The hunk is the accessor-macro rename over the model/instance walk. Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

MOS3sLoad sensitivity load, the sens_w branch (Osxp*_dw stores). Adds an (int) cast on here->MOS3sens_l in the (MOS3senParmNo + sens_l) sensitivity-parameter index. digiTS has no per-device sensitivity. Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

MOS3sPrint sensitivity debug printout (printf of LEVEL 3 MOSFET sensitivity model/instance values). digiTS has no sensitivity analysis and no per-device stdout. The single hunk is the accessor-macro rename.

MOS3sSetup(SENstruct *info, ...) sensitivity setup - assigns MOS3senParmNo into the SENstruct sensitivity-parameter table. digiTS has no SENstruct and no sensitivity analysis (analysis-scope.md). The single hunk is the accessor-macro rename.

MOS3sUpdate sensitivity update - recomputes charge sensitivities (MOS3dphi*_dl) into the sensitivity tables. digiTS has no per-device sensitivity. The hunk is the accessor-macro rename over the model/instance walk. Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

MOS3sUpdate sensitivity update, the sens_w branch (sxp*_dw accumulation). Adds an (int) cast on here->MOS3sens_l in the (MOS3senParmNo + sens_l) sensitivity-parameter index. digiTS has no per-device sensitivity. Whole file belongs to sensitivity.

Automake libres_la_SOURCES fragment — a C build-system artifact (PLANNING.md §5 case (a)). digiTS has no autotools build; components register via src/core/registry.ts. The hunk only deletes the resdel.c/resdest.c/resmdel.c entries; all three files are themselves NO-COUNTERPART C-heap-free artifacts deleted in v41. No behavioral content.

RESpTable IFparm row addition `IOPPR("r", RES_RESIST, IF_REAL, ...)` — a redirect-flag (IF_REDIRECT) alias row so ngspice's generic ask/set machinery accepts `r` as a name for the canonical RES_RESIST id. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-table artifact: digiTS keys params by name string (RESISTOR_PARAM_DEFS primary `resistance`); no integer-id IFparm table, no parser-driven generic ask/set, no IF_REDIRECT flag. No parameter is added or removed and no default changes — the canonical `resistance` already exists; this is purely a parser-table alias.

RESpTable IFparm instance rows: changes `tc1` to IOPUR (redirect flag) and adds an IOPU `tce` row + an IOPR `noise` redirect-alias for RES_NOISY. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-table artifact: digiTS has no integer-id IFparm table or IF_REDIRECT flag. The `tce` parameter itself is realised by res#recon/computeConductance's defineModelParams (and its setter resparam.c#h002 / model setter resmpar.c#h001), not by this parser-table row; `noise` is a noise-model param (not implemented). The IFparm table is C parser plumbing with no digiTS counterpart. See Q1.

RESmPTable IFparm MODEL rows: changes dw/dlr/tc1r/tc2r/w to redirect-flag macro variants (IOPZR/IOPQR/IOPQOR/IOPXR) and adds new model rows tce (IOPQ) and lf/wf/ef (IOP). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-table artifact: digiTS has no integer-id IFparm table or IF_REDIRECT flag. The model params these rows declare (tce → reconstructed conductance model; lf/wf/ef → noise model, not implemented) are realised by defineModelParams in the reconstruction / are noise-only, not by this parser-table; the table itself has no digiTS counterpart. See Q1.

RESask — the SPICE IFparm instance-parameter value getter (integer-id switch dispatch). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-API artifact: digiTS has no integer-id ask API; instance values are read as TypeScript class members. Same disposition as ind/mutask.c. The hunk drops the `value->rValue *= RESm` / `/= RESm` rescaling from the RES_CONDUCT/RES_RESIST/RES_ACCONDUCT/RES_ACRESIST getters — a consequence of v41 folding RESm into RESconduct — but it lives wholly inside the no-counterpart ask dispatch.

RESask getter — adds the RES_TCE case (`value->rValue = fast->REStce`). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-API artifact: digiTS has no integer-id ask API. The tce instance value is read as a class member in digiTS; this parser-facing getter case has no digiTS counterpart.

RESask getter — the RES_CURRENT case: replaces TMALLOC+strcpy error-message construction with tprintf, and changes the current computation to `(V+ - V-)` then `*= RESconduct` (dropping the `*= RESm` factor, an m-fold consequence). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-API artifact: digiTS has no integer-id ask API (the load-time current path is ResistorAnalogElement.getPinCurrents, not this parser getter). Wholly inside the no-counterpart ask dispatch.

RESask getter — the RES_POWER case: replaces TMALLOC+strcpy with tprintf and reorders the power computation to multiply by RESconduct at the end (dropping the `*= RESm` factor, an m-fold consequence). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C parser-API artifact: digiTS has no integer-id ask API. Wholly inside the no-counterpart ask dispatch.

Adds the RESNSRCS noise-source enum (RESTHNOIZ/RESFLNOIZ/RESTOTNOIZ — noise analysis, not implemented per analysis-scope.md) and rewrites the sRESinstance head to embed `struct GENinstance gen` with accessor macros (RESmodPtr/RESnextInstance/RESname/RESstate) and marks RESposNode/RESnegNode `const`. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C generic-struct-embedding mechanics + case (b) noise enum: digiTS has no GENinstance, no model-instance linked list, no integer node-id instance fields (pinNodes is a ReadonlyMap), and does not implement noise. No behavioral content with a digiTS counterpart.

sRESinstance body: adds the REStce instance field, the RESeffNoiseArea field (noise-only), and renames the four sparse-matrix-pointer fields RES{posPos,negNeg,posNeg,negPos}ptr → ...Ptr (capitalisation only). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C struct mechanics: digiTS holds opaque Norton-stamp handles (_hPP/_hNN/_hPN/_hNP from solver.allocElement), not raw double* matrix pointers; RESeffNoiseArea serves the non-implemented noise analysis. The REStce data field is ported by its writer (resparam.c#h002 / resmpar.c#h001) and reader (restemp.c#h001), all PENDING and blocked by res#recon/computeConductance; the struct declaration itself has no counterpart (same as cap/capdefs.h).

sRESinstance Given-flags section: adds REStceGiven, relocates the RESTHNOIZ/RESFLNOIZ/RESTOTNOIZ/RESNSRCS noise-source `#define`s out of the struct (now the enum in h001), and reorders the noisy/bv_max Given flags. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C struct-bitfield mechanics + case (b) noise macros: digiTS has no `*Given` bitfields (it uses the param-bag/_xxxGiven boolean pattern realised inside the reconstruction) and does not implement noise. No behavioral content with a digiTS counterpart at the struct-declaration level.

sRESmodel head: embeds `struct GENmodel gen` with accessor macros (RESmodType/RESnextModel/RESinstances/RESmodName) and adds the REStempCoeffe model field. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C generic-struct-embedding mechanics: digiTS has no GENmodel and no model linked list. The REStempCoeffe data field is ported by its writer (resmpar.c#h001) and reader (restemp.c#h001), PENDING and blocked by res#recon/computeConductance; the struct declaration itself has no counterpart.

items.rationale: sRESmodel body: adds the model noise fields RESlf/RESwf/RESef + their REStceGiven/RESlfGiven/RESwfGiven/RESefGiven flags. PLANNING.md §5 case (b) noise analysis (lf/wf/ef are flicker-noise area/frequency exponents consumed only by RESnoise, not implemented per analysis-scope.md) + case (a) C struct-bitfield mechanics (no *Given bitfields in digiTS). The REStceGiven flag's portable content is folded into the reconstruction's param bag; the noise fields have no implemented-analysis counterpart. No behavioral content with a digiTS counterpart at the struct-declaration level.

items.hunkHeader: @@ -122,53 +131,71 @@ typedef struct sRESmodel {       /* model structure for a resistor */
items.enclosingContext: typedef struct sRESmodel {       /* model structure for a resistor */
items.rationale: Reformats the RES_*/RES_MOD_*/RES_QUEST_SENS_* integer-id `#define` constants into C `enum` blocks (byte-identical numeric values: RES_RESIST=1.., RES_ACRESIST=10.., RES_MOD_TC1=101.., RES_QUEST_SENS_REAL=201..) and adds the `extern void RESupdate_conduct(...)` prototype. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS keys params by name string (no integer ids), has no extern prototypes (signatures are class members), and the RES_QUEST_SENS_* ids belong to sensitivity (not implemented). No value, parameter, or default changes.

Whole-file deletion of RESdelete — a C-heap free (FREE(here)) unlinking one RESinstance from a linked list. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; instances drop by reference — no allocator, no destructor, no linked list. File deleted in v41.

Whole-file deletion of RESdestroy — a C-heap free loop releasing every RESmodel and RESinstance. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. File deleted in v41.

C extern-prototype header. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no prototype headers — signatures are TypeScript class members. The hunk only removes the extern decls of RESdelete/RESdestroy/RESmDelete (the three deleted C-heap-free functions). No surviving declaration is changed — no new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

SPICEdev RESinfo registration dispatch struct, converted to C99 designated initialisers. PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note + §5 case (a): digiTS has no SPICEdev table — registration is ResistorDefinition and method binding is the ResistorAnalogElement class. Reviewed hunk-by-hunk per the glue note: every surviving function-pointer assignment that was non-NULL in v26 stays non-NULL and points to the same handler (RESparam/RESmParam/RESload/RESsetup/REStemp/RESacLoad/RESask/RESmodAsk/RESpzLoad/RESsSetup/RESsLoad/RESsAcLoad/RESsPrint/RESnoise/RESsoaCheck). The only non-cosmetic changes are DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete/DEVdelete set to NULL (the deleted heap functions). No new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/resistor.ts — ResistorAnalogElement.load (resistor.ts:194-201), the four conductance stamps through the setup()-allocated handles. The v41 hunk is the model/instance linked-list-walk accessor rename plus removal of the `m *` factor from the four stamps (v41 folds RESm into RESconduct in RESupdate_conduct, so RESload stamps RESconduct directly). digiTS's load() already stamps _G with no separate m factor, matching the v41 shape; the m fold into _G is supplied by res#recon/computeConductance. Blocked by res#recon/computeConductance.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Tier1+Tier2 vs fresh ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/res/resload.c:15-38 (RESload), v41 hunk res.md docLineRange 652-684. Identifier correspondence re-derived independently (rename-maps NOT consulted): RESconduct<->_G, RESposPosPtr/RESnegNegPtr/RESposNegPtr/RESnegPosPtr<->_hPP/_hNN/_hPN/_hNP, REScurrent<->getPinCurrents return. Tier1: the v41 hunk drops `double m;`, drops `m = (here->RESm);`, renames the model/instance walk accessors (C iteration plumbing, no TS counterpart -> allowed sec4), and removes the `m *` factor from the four stamps so they become bare `here->RESconduct`. Our delta (resistor.ts:598-606) removes `const g = this._M * this._G` and stamps `this._G` directly into _hPP/_hNN (+) and _hPN/_hNP (-); getPinCurrents (resistor.ts:629-631) drops `this._M *` so `I = this._G*(vA-vB)`, matching the unchanged REScurrent=(V+ - V-)*RESconduct line. Line-isomorphic. Tier2 bijective: REScurrent (resload.c:28-29) <-> getPinCurrents _G*(vA-vB); four ±RESconduct stamps (resload.c:31-34) <-> four ±_G stampElement calls in posPos/negNeg/posNeg/negPos order. M-fold into _G supplied by res#recon/computeConductance (APPLIED), so the bare-stamp shape is correct. FILE-SCOPE: git diff --name-only source change is solely src/components/passives/resistor.ts.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/resistor.ts — ResistorAnalogElement.stampAc (resistor.ts:203-212), the AC small-signal conductance stamp. The v41 hunk (RESacload) is the accessor rename + the new `g = RESacresGiven ? RESacConduct : RESconduct` selection of the separate AC conductance + removal of the `m *` factor from the four stamps. digiTS's stampAc currently stamps _G unconditionally with no RESacConduct/acres path; the reconstruction supplies RESacConduct and the M fold. Blocked by res#recon/computeConductance.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Tier1+Tier2 vs fresh ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/res/resload.c:43-71 (RESacload), v41 hunk res.md docLineRange 685-728. Correspondence re-derived independently: RESacConduct<->_Gac, RESconduct<->_G, RESacresGiven<->_acresGiven, four ...Ptr<->_hPP/_hNN/_hPN/_hNP. Tier1: v41 adds `double g;`, drops `double m;` and `m=(here->RESm);`, renames walk accessors, and replaces the v26 `if(RESacresGiven){4 stamps m*RESacConduct}else{4 stamps m*RESconduct}` with `if(RESacresGiven) g=RESacConduct; else g=RESconduct;` then four bare `±g` stamps. Our delta (resistor.ts:608-622) replaces `const g=this._M*this._Gac` with `let g; if(this._acresGiven) g=this._Gac; else g=this._G;` then four `±g` stampElement calls. Line-isomorphic (the acres branch + four ±g stamps map 1:1). Tier2 bijective: acres-gated g selection (resload.c:59-62) <-> resistor.ts:613-616; four ±g stamps (resload.c:64-67) <-> resistor.ts:618-621 in posPos/negNeg/posNeg/negPos order. M-fold into _G/_Gac supplied by res#recon/computeConductance. FILE-SCOPE: source change solely resistor.ts.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/resistor.ts — model-parameter read path for `tce`. digiTS has no integer-id model-ask API; the read counterpart is the reconstructed model parameter `tce` (REStempCoeffe) exposed as a class accessor / param-bag read. The v41 hunk adds case RES_MOD_TCE returning model->REStempCoeffe. The model param `tce` is introduced by res#recon/computeConductance (it is the exponential temperature coefficient feeding the conductance fold). Blocked by res#recon/computeConductance.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Tier1+Tier2 vs fresh ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/res/resmask.c:36-38 (RESmodAsk RES_MOD_TCE case), v41 hunk res.md docLineRange 733-742. Correspondence re-derived independently: model->REStempCoeffe<->modTce (param-bag read path). RESmodAsk is the C integer-id model-ask getter; digiTS has no integer-id ask API (REVIEW-CLASS), so the read counterpart is the reconstructed model param `modTce` declared in RESISTOR_PARAM_DEFS.secondary (resistor.ts:57, spiceName 'tce'), readable via the param bag. This is the sanctioned C-parser-getter -> TS-param-declaration surface form, identical disposition to the APPLIED ind/indmask and cap precedents. The v41 case returns model->REStempCoeffe; our modTce (REStempCoeffe) is now declared + readable + ctor-loaded (resistor.ts:332-333). Read path present, bijective at the per-param level. FILE-SCOPE: source change solely resistor.ts.

items.rationale: RESmodAsk getter — adds cases RES_MOD_LF/WF/EF returning model->RESlf/RESwf/RESef. PLANNING.md §5 case (b) whole non-implemented SPICE analysis: lf/wf/ef are flicker-noise area/frequency exponents consumed only by RESnoise (noise not implemented per analysis-scope.md). Also case (a): digiTS has no integer-id model-ask API. No implemented-analysis counterpart.

Whole-file deletion of RESmDelete — a C-heap free that unlinks a RESmodel and frees its instances. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; no model destructor, no model linked list. File deleted in v41.

items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/resistor.ts — reconstructed model defineModelParams + setParam for `tce`. The v41 hunk adds case RES_MOD_TCE storing model->REStempCoeffe + REStceGiven. The model param `tce` (exponential temperature coefficient) is introduced by res#recon/computeConductance and feeds the conductance temperature fold (factor = pow(1.01, tce·ΔT)). Blocked by res#recon/computeConductance.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Tier1+Tier2 vs fresh ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/res/resmpar.c:33-36 (RESmParam RES_MOD_TCE case), v41 hunk res.md docLineRange 807-817. Correspondence re-derived independently: REStempCoeffe<->_modTce, REStceGiven<->_modTceGiven. Tier1: v41 adds `case RES_MOD_TCE: model->REStempCoeffe = value->rValue; model->REStceGiven = TRUE; break;`. Our delta (resistor.ts:574-577) adds `case "modTce": this._modTce = value; this._modTceGiven = true; break;` plus the modTce secondary param def (resistor.ts:57), the _modTce/_modTceGiven fields (resistor.ts:239,251), and the ctor read (resistor.ts:332-333). Line-isomorphic (field assign + Given flag). Tier2 bijective: RES_MOD_TCE case <-> setParam 'modTce' case. Trailing updateConduct(false) (resistor.ts:595) is the unified-setParam recompute established by res#recon/computeConductance and required for hot-loadable params; the hunk's own content (field+Given) is bijective. FILE-SCOPE: source change solely resistor.ts.

items.rationale: RESmParam setter — adds cases RES_MOD_LF/WF/EF storing model->RESlf/RESwf/RESef + their Given flags. PLANNING.md §5 case (b) whole non-implemented SPICE analysis: lf/wf/ef are flicker-noise area/frequency exponents consumed only by RESnoise (noise not implemented per analysis-scope.md). No implemented-analysis counterpart.

items.rationale: Whole-file deletion of SWdelete — a C-heap free (FREE(here)) that unlinks one SWinstance from a linked list. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; element instances drop by reference. No allocator, no destructor, no linked list. The file is deleted in v41.

Whole-file deletion of SWdestroy — a C-heap free loop releasing every SWmodel and SWinstance. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. The file is deleted in v41.

C extern-prototype header for the SW dispatch functions. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no prototype headers — signatures are TypeScript class members. The hunk removes the extern decls of the deleted SWdelete/SWdestroy/SWmDelete and reformats surviving prototype whitespace; no surviving declaration changes behavior.

SPICEdev SWinfo dispatch struct converted to C99 designated initialisers. PLANNING.md §4 registration-glue note + §5 case (a): digiTS has no SPICEdev table — registration is SwitchSPSTDefinition/SwitchSPDTDefinition (register-all.ts:482-483); method binding is the element classes. Examined per the glue note: every non-NULL function-pointer assignment is preserved v26→v41; the only behavioral-direction change is DEVdestroy/DEVmodDelete/DEVdelete → NULL (the deleted heap functions). No new parameter slot, no changed default, no new device capability.

C extern-declaration header for SWpTable/SWmPTable/SWnames + size globals. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no extern headers. The hunk only strips `[ ]`→`[]` whitespace in the array extern decls; no behavioral content.

The stamp arithmetic (+=1.0/-=1.0/+=1.0/-=1.0/-=VCVScoeff/+=VCVScoeff) is byte-identical across v26->v41; no +/- line changes operands, sign pattern or control flow -> empty TS delta line-isomorphic. IFparm VSRCpTable alias-row edit: the `sine` and `sin` rows are swapped so `sin` is now the canonical IOP row for VSRC_SINE and `sine` becomes IOPR (the IF_REDIRECT alias macro) redirecting onto the same id. digiTS keys parameters by name string (waveform property + defineModelParams), has no integer-id IFparm table, no parser-driven generic ask/set, and no IF_REDIRECT flag. Parser-table plumbing only per PLANNING.md §5 case (a) — no parameter added/removed, no default changed.

IFparm VSRCpTable edit: changes the SHARED_MODULE `external` row type IF_REALVEC → IF_STRING (a parser-table type-flag edit on a SHARED_MODULE-only param), and adds the #ifdef RFSPICE port-parameter rows (portnum/z0/pwr/freq/phase). digiTS has no IFparm table and no IF_STRING flag; the RFSPICE port params belong to S-parameter / RF analysis, which is not in analysis-scope.md (only DC-OP, transient, AC are implemented). Parser-table plumbing + non-implemented-analysis params per PLANNING.md §5 cases (a)/(b). No behavioral content for an implemented analysis.

Adds `#include "ngspice/compatmode.h"` so VSRCaccept can read the global `newcompat` compatibility-mode struct. C include directive per PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no preprocessor and no ngspice global newcompat struct (grep for compatmode/newcompat across src/ returns zero hits). The newcompat.xs phase-handling it enables is part of the VSRCcoeffs waveform model handled under vsrc#recon/waveformModel; the include itself has no digiTS counterpart.

Removes the file-local `#define SAMETIME(a,b)` and `#define TIMETOL 1e-7` macros from VSRCaccept (the v41 breakpoint rewrite no longer uses the SAMETIME ULP-equality test). C-preprocessor macro definitions per PLANNING.md §5 case (a). digiTS's acceptStep does carry its own SAMETIME/TIMETOL constants (ac-voltage-source.ts:858-859), but those belong to digiTS's own breakpoint mechanism; the v41 macro removal is C-preprocessor plumbing for the rewritten ngspice break model handled under vsrc#recon/waveformModel. No standalone behavioral content.

The v41 PULSE arm replaced the v26 SAMETIME edge-detection with a break_time-gated wait-to-next-boundary scheme. digiTS _acceptNgspice PULSE (ac-voltage-source.ts:1409-1441) is bijective with the v41 NON-compat path: `time = simTime - TD` (1410) <-> vsrcacct.c time=CKTtime-TD; PHASE>0 tmax cap with break (1413-1416) <-> the `else if(PHASE>0.0){tmax=PHASE*PER; if(time>tmax) break;}` v41 branch; `if(simTime >= _breakTime)` gate (1419) <-> `if(CKTtime >= VSRCbreak_time)`; repeating basetime (1421-1424) <-> the in-gate `if(time>=PER){basetime=PER*floor(time/PER); time-=basetime;}`; the wait-ladder (1427-1432): time<0->-time, <TR->TR-time, <TR+PW->TR+PW-time, <TR+PW+TF->TR+PW+TF-time, else PER-time -- operand-for-operand identical to v41 lines 230-249; `_breakTime=simTime+wait; addBreakpoint(_breakTime); _breakTime-=_minBreak` (1435-1439) <-> VSRCbreak_time=CKTtime+wait; CKTsetBreak; VSRCbreak_time-=CKTminBreak. The newcompat.xs phase-normalization sub-branch (v41: `if(newcompat.xs){phase=PHASE/360; fmod; deltat...; time+=deltat;}`) has NO digiTS counterpart (newcompat is an ngspice process-global, cross-subsystem); it is the compat-mode-only path, distinct from the non-compat PULSE schedule which is fully ported. The PULSE break-schedule is bijective over the non-compat path; the newcompat.xs gap is the same frozen compat-mode global escalated for vsrcload.c#h004 (NO-COUNTERPART) and does not affect the default (non-compat) breakpoint schedule. addBreakpoint dedup at CKTminBreak per FIX-003 (cktsetbk.c). The v41 PWL arm replaced the v26 AlmostEqualUlps knot-detection + goto bkptset with a break_time-gated next-knot schedule. digiTS _acceptNgspice PWL (ac-voltage-source.ts:1451-1479) is bijective: `if(simTime >= _breakTime)` gate (1453) <-> `if(CKTtime >= VSRCbreak_time)`; `time = simTime - _rdelay` (1454) <-> time=CKTtime-VSRCrdelay; `end = coeffs[order-2]` (1455) <-> end=VSRCcoeffs[VSRCfunctionOrder-2]; `if(time > end)` with repeat fold `if(_rGiven){ period=end-coeffs[_rBreakpt]; time-=coeffs[_rBreakpt]; time-=period*floor(time/period); time+=coeffs[_rBreakpt]; } else { _breakTime=_cktFinalTime; break; }` (1456-1467) <-> v41 lines 282-297 operand-for-operand; the next-knot loop `for(i=0;i<order;i+=2){ if(coeffs[i]>time){ _breakTime=simTime+coeffs[i]-time; addBreakpoint(_breakTime); _breakTime-=_minBreak; break; } }` (1470-1477) <-> v41 lines 305-318. items.rationale: Fixes a spelling typo in a C source comment (`tansient` → `transient`) above the TRNOISE case and adds one blank line inside the TRNOISE case. Pure source-comment / whitespace change per PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact. No code, no preprocessor directive, no behavioral content.

TRNOISE source-waveform breakpoint arm of VSRCaccept: the VSRCbreak_time-gated `break_time += TS` sample scheduling and the RTS (random-telegraph-signal) shot-noise capture/emission scheduling reading state->RTScapTime/RTSemTime/RTSCAPT/RTSEMT. This is the trnoise_state noise subsystem, which is out of scope for a browser digital-logic simulator (user ruling 2026-06-02, ruled 3x). The trnoise_state lifecycle (trnoise_state_init/_get/_free, f_alpha 1/f synthesizer, RTS trap state machine) is tracked separately as the noise subsystem task (ESC-24-noise); maths-misc#recon/randnumb delivered only SeededRng primitives, not trnoise_state. NO-COUNTERPART: the TRNOISE/TRRANDOM source-waveform arms have no digiTS counterpart and are not reconstructed under vsrc#recon/waveformModel.

TRRANDOM source-waveform breakpoint arm of VSRCaccept: the VSRCbreak_time-gated scheduling with TD initial-delay, reading state->TS/TD and calling trrandom_state_get(state). This is the trrandom_state noise subsystem, out of scope for a browser digital-logic simulator (user ruling 2026-06-02, ruled 3x). The trrandom_state lifecycle (trrandom_state_init/_get) is tracked separately as the noise subsystem task (ESC-24-noise); maths-misc#recon/randnumb delivered only SeededRng primitives. NO-COUNTERPART: the TRNOISE/TRRANDOM source-waveform arms have no digiTS counterpart and are not reconstructed under vsrc#recon/waveformModel.

Removes the `bkptset: ;` goto-label at the bottom of the VSRCaccept instance loop (the v41 PWL rewrite no longer `goto bkptset`). C goto-label control-flow plumbing per PLANNING.md §5 case (a): digiTS has no goto and no label; acceptStep returns directly. No behavioral content.

items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/ac-voltage-source.ts — AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl.stampAc (ac-voltage-source.ts:723-746) and src/components/sources/dc-voltage-source.ts — DcVoltageSourceAnalogElement.stampAc (dc-voltage-source.ts:227-248). The portable content (the #else, non-RFSPICE path) is the MODEACNOISE noise-input gate (acReal/acImag = 1/0 when this instance is ckt->noise_input, else the VSRCacReal/VSRCacImag values) plus the four ±1 incidence stamps (renamed ptr→Ptr) and the two RHS writes (CKTrhs += acReal, CKTirhs += acImag). digiTS already stamps the four ±1 cells and writes acMag·cos / acMag·sin into rhsRe/rhsIm in stampAc; the MODEACNOISE noise-input gate is the new behavioral content to port. items.rationale: RFSPICE VSRCspinit/VSRCspupdate/VSRCgetActivePorts + #ifdef RFSPICE acLoad branch. S-parameter analysis not implemented (vsrc-review.md §1 / §5 split 1).

The only digiTS-absent content of this #else AC-load hunk is the MODEACNOISE noise-input gate (acReal/acImag = 1/0 when this instance is ckt->noise_input). digiTS has no small-signal .noise analysis — zero MODEACNOISE bit in ckt-mode.ts (bits stop at MODEUIC 0x10000), no ckt->noise_input designation, no noise driver — so the gate has no counterpart (user ruling 2026-06-02: NO-COUNTERPART; .noise out of analysis-scope). The hunk's other content (the four ±1 incidence stamps + the CKTrhs/CKTirhs writes of acMag·cos/acMag·sin) is ALREADY present and isomorphic in AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl.stampAc / DcVoltageSourceAnalogElement.stampAc (verified by the parallel-run verifier); the noise gate is the sole delta and it is out of scope.

items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/ac-voltage-source.ts — AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl instance fields (ac-voltage-source.ts:562-585). items.rationale: Renames the five sparse-matrix-pointer fields VSRC{posIbr,negIbr,ibrPos,ibrNeg,ibrIbr}ptr → ...Ptr (capitalisation only). digiTS holds opaque solver handles (the _hPosBr/_hNegBr/_hBrNeg/_hBrPos fields from ctx.solver.allocElement, dc-voltage-source.ts:170-173), not raw double* matrix pointers. C struct field-name cosmetics per PLANNING.md §5 case (a). No behavioral content.

Reformats the function-type #defines (PULSE..EXTERNAL) and the device-parameter #defines (VSRC_DC..VSRC_EXTERNAL) into C enum blocks with byte-identical values, adds the Given-flags bitfield section trailer, the #ifdef RFSPICE port instance fields, and the `struct GENmodel gen` embedding with accessor macros. digiTS keys parameters by name string and has no integer-id enums, no GENmodel/model linked list, and no RFSPICE port fields (S-parameter analysis, not in analysis-scope.md). C struct + id-enum + RFSPICE mechanics per PLANNING.md §5 cases (a)/(b). No value, parameter, or default change for an implemented digiTS construct.

VSRCdelete rewrite — v41 changes it from a linked-list unlink (FREE(here)) into a per-instance C-heap free of VSRCcoeffs / trnoise_state / trrandom_state. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; element instances drop by reference. No allocator, no destructor, no linked list, and no VSRCcoeffs/trnoise_state/trrandom_state heap blocks (these belong to the ngspice waveform model digiTS does not carry). No behavioral content.

Whole-file deletion of VSRCdestroy — a C-heap free loop releasing every VSRCmodel and VSRCinstance. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS is GC-managed; no explicit destructor. The file is deleted in v41.

C extern-prototype header — PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact. The hunk changes the VSRCdelete prototype signature (GENmodel*,IFuid,GENinstance** → GENinstance*), removes the VSRCdestroy/VSRCmDelete prototypes (the deleted C-heap functions), and adds the #ifdef RFSPICE VSRCspupdate/VSRCgetActivePorts/VSRCspinit prototypes (S-parameter analysis, not in analysis-scope.md). digiTS has no prototype headers; method signatures are TypeScript class members. No surviving declaration maps to a digiTS construct — no new parameter slot, no changed default, no new capability for an implemented analysis.

items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/dc-voltage-source.ts — DcVoltageSourceAnalogElement.findBranchFor (dc-voltage-source.ts:197-205) and the same method on AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl (ac-voltage-source.ts:657-665). VSRCfindBr looks up an instance by name and allocates its branch equation via CKTmkCur if unallocated; digiTS's findBranchFor calls ctx.findDevice(name) then ctx.makeCur. The hunk is solely the model/instance linked-list-walk accessor rename (VSRCnextModel/VSRCnextInstance macro form); arithmetic and control flow unchanged.

ZERO-DELTA RULE (sec2.4): the only +/- lines in h001 are the model/instance walk accessor renames (`model->VSRCnextModel`->`VSRCnextModel(model)`, `model->VSRCinstances`/`here->VSRCnextInstance`->`VSRCinstances(model)`/`VSRCnextInstance(here)`) = C linked-list plumbing with no digiTS counterpart (the engine device registry replaces the per-model instance list). Arithmetic and control flow unchanged. Bijective.

The newcompat.xs phase handling it enables is part of the VSRCcoeffs waveform model handled under vsrc#recon/waveformModel; the include itself has no digiTS counterpart.

items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/ac-voltage-source.ts — AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl.load PULSE-path local declarations (the V1/V2/TD/TR/TF/PW/PER + phase locals). The v41 hunk replaces the XSPICE #ifdef phase block with unconditional `double PHASE/phase/deltat` plus the new `tmax` local. digiTS reads V1/V2/period/riseTime/fallTime from its own params (computeWaveformValue square case, ac-voltage-source.ts:119-162) rather than VSRCcoeffs[0..7]. Blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel — the VSRCcoeffs-indexed PULSE locals do not exist on the v26 baseline digiTS lacks (Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM).
verifierNotes: MATCH (Tier1+Tier2) vs fresh vsrcload.c PULSE value arm (diff vsrc.md docLineRange 1293-1306). functionGroup vsrc/vsrcload.c::VSRCload. PULSE coeff reads (V1=coeffs[0], V2=coeffs[1], TD/TR/TF/PW/PER order-guarded defaults from CKTstep/CKTfinalTime) <-> evaluateNgspiceWaveform PULSE arm (ac-voltage-source.ts coeff engine). The v41 reshape removes the #ifdef XSPICE wrapper on PHASE/phase/deltat. Non-compat value computation (the V1/V2/ramp ladder) is present and bijective. The newcompat.xs branch (load h004) is the frozen NO-COUNTERPART, not in this hunk. PASS.

items.rationale: The hunk's sole v41-new content is the `newcompat.xs` compatibility-mode branch — ngspice's emulation of foreign SPICE dialects (PSPICE/LTspice/HSPICE/Spice2-3, ngspice/compatmode.h), gated by `set ngbehavior`/`.option`; it only changes the PULSE phase convention WHEN a compat flag is set. digiTS has no compat-mode system (no ngbehavior/.option parser, no newcompat global; grep across src/ = zero hits) and foreign-dialect emulation is out of scope for a browser logic-sim (user ruling 2026-06-02: NO-COUNTERPART). The non-compat baseline PULSE phase path is the portable one and lands via the coeff engine; the newcompat.xs branch has no counterpart.

items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/ac-voltage-source.ts — computeWaveformValue sine case (ac-voltage-source.ts:112-117) SINE-path local declarations. The v41 hunk de-#ifdefs the XSPICE PHASE/phase locals so the sine phase is always available (phase = PHASE·π/180). digiTS's sine case already takes an unconditional phase argument. Blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel — the SINE locals are read from VSRCcoeffs[] by VSRCfunctionOrder, which is part of the ngspice waveform model digiTS does not carry (Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM).
verifierNotes: MATCH (Tier1+Tier2) vs fresh vsrcload.c SINE value arm (diff vsrc.md docLineRange 1377-1385). functionGroup vsrc/vsrcload.c::VSRCload. The v41 SINE arm unwraps #ifdef XSPICE making the PHASE-aware form unconditional: VO=coeffs[0], VA=coeffs[1], FREQ order-guarded (default 1/CKTfinalTime), TD, THETA, PHASE; time-=TD; if(time<=0) value=VO+VA*sin(phase) else value=VO+VA*sin(FREQ*time*2*PI+phase)*exp(-time*THETA). evaluateNgspiceWaveform SINE arm matches operand-for-operand (phase in radians, the *2*M_PI, the exp(-time*THETA) damping). Bijective. PASS.

verifierNotes: MATCH (Tier1+Tier2) vs fresh vsrcload.c EXP value arm (diff vsrc.md docLineRange 1386-1394). functionGroup vsrc/vsrcload.c::VSRCload. EXP: V1=coeffs[0], V2=coeffs[1], TD1/TAU1/TD2/TAU2 order-guarded (CKTstep defaults; TD2 default TD1+CKTstep), the three-region value (time<=TD1->V1; time<=TD2->V1+(V2-V1)*(1-exp(-(time-TD1)/TAU1)); else +(V1-V2)*(1-exp(-(time-TD2)/TAU2))). evaluateNgspiceWaveform EXP arm matches operand-for-operand. Bijective. PASS.

items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/ac-voltage-source.ts — computeWaveformValue sine case (ac-voltage-source.ts:112-117), the value formula. The v41 hunk collapses the XSPICE/non-XSPICE #ifdef so the SINE value is always VO + VA·sin(FREQ·time·2π + phase)·exp(−time·THETA) including the phase term (the non-XSPICE v26 path omitted +phase). digiTS already computes VO + VA·sin(FREQ·t·2π + phase) (the THETA damping is digiTS's `expression`/EXP-equivalent, absent for plain sine). Blocked by vsrc#recon/waveformModel — the THETA-damped SINE and the VSRCcoeffs model are absent from the v26 baseline digiTS lacks (Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM).
verifierNotes: MATCH (Tier1+Tier2) vs fresh vsrcload.c PULSE value-tail (diff vsrc.md docLineRange 1395-1413). functionGroup vsrc/vsrcload.c::VSRCload. The post-newcompat PULSE value ladder `if(time<=0 || time>=TR+PW+TF) value=V1; else if(time>=TR && time<=TR+PW) value=V2; else if(time>0 && time<TR) value=V1+(V2-V1)*time/TR; else value=V2+(V1-V2)*(time-(TR+PW))/TF` <-> evaluateNgspiceWaveform PULSE value ladder, operand-for-operand. Note this slice (diff lines 1395-1413, fresh vsrcload.c:188+ SINE head + PULSE tail) is AFTER the newcompat.xs block (frozen h004); the non-compat ladder is fully ported. Bijective. PASS.

ZERO-DELTA RULE (VERIFICATION.md sec1.4): the docLineRange 2053-2055 slice is context-only -- `radians = VSRCacPhase * M_PI / 180.0; VSRCacReal = VSRCacMag*cos(radians); VSRCacImag = VSRCacMag*sin(radians)` is unchanged v26->v41; no +/- line in this slice, so the empty worktree delta is line-isomorphic. Tier-2 construct correspondence re-derived independently: v41 vsrctemp.c:68 `radians = VSRCacPhase * M_PI / 180.0` <-> ac-voltage-source.ts:1310 `const radians = this._acPhase * Math.PI / 180.0` (same operands/operator/order/grouping); v41:69 `VSRCacReal = VSRCacMag * cos(radians)` <-> ac:1311 `rhsRe[branchIndex] += this._acMagnitude * Math.cos(radians)`; v41:70 `VSRCacImag = VSRCacMag * sin(radians)` <-> ac:1312 `rhsIm[branchIndex] += this._acMagnitude * Math.sin(radians)`. The VSRCacReal/VSRCacImag intermediates are inlined directly into the stampAc RHS accumulation -- this is the vsrctemp(compute)/vsrcacld(consume to RHS) boundary; digiTS computes per-instance at stampAc time. The arithmetic expressions VSRCacMag*cos/sin(radians) are operand/operator/order-identical, NOT an algebraic simplification. blockedBy vsrc#recon/waveformModel = APPLIED in this worktree. Bijective over the derivation.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/ckt-context.ts::CKTCircuitContext
verifierNotes: ESCALATED 2026-06-02 (final analysis ledger disposition). CKTdoJob task->ckt copy block (cktdojob.c:40-63). This hunk's new behavioral assignments are `ckt->CKTindverbosity = task->TSKindverbosity` (present: indVerbosity), `ckt->CKTxmu = task->TSKxmu` (present: xmu), AND `ckt->CKTcshunt = task->TSKcshunt` (cktdojob.c, the shunt-capacitor-to-ground param). cshunt is a GENUINELY UNPORTED behavioral param with no ResolvedSimulationParams field and no load-path (Grep cshunt/cShunt/CShunt over src/ = 0 matches). It must NOT be buried under the struct-copy NO-COUNTERPART that covers the present params; escalated as its own gap per ESC-016/ESC-024. Set aside (not workable-PENDING for the scout) pending the cshunt per-param user disposition.
items.escalation: ESC-024: cktdojob.c#h003 carries `ckt->CKTcshunt = task->TSKcshunt` (shunt capacitor to ground). cshunt is unported in digiTS (no SimulationParams field, no load-path). The hunk also copies indverbosity/xmu (both present/APPLIED), but the cshunt assignment forbids a clean NO-COUNTERPART of the whole hunk (would bury a real feature gap, PLANNING.md §5 / CLAUDE.md no-pragmatic-patches). Blocked pending the cshunt feature decision (new ResolvedSimulationParams.cshunt field + a load-path adding the per-node shunt conductance). See ESCALATIONS.md ESC-024 and ESC-016.

items.rationale: CKTdoJob TSKtask->CKTcircuit struct-copy block (cktdojob.c:104-110). The single new behavioral assignment is `ckt->CKTepsmin = task->TSKepsmin` (cktdojob.c:110); digiTS has no CKTdoJob method and no TSKtask struct — options arrive as typed fields on ResolvedSimulationParams (src/core/analog-engine-interface.ts:186 `epsmin?: number`, default 1e-28 at :226) read into CKTCircuitContext.cktEpsmin via configure() (ckt-context.ts:840-841, hot-loadable per CLAUDE.md). The behavioral param is APPLIED; this hunk is the C struct-copy MECHANISM (the TSKtask->ckt field-by-field transfer + CKTtroubleNode/troubleElt reset + inp_evaluate_temper + WANT_SENSE2/XSPICE plumbing) with no data content of its own and no line-isomorphic TS counterpart — the classic struct-layout/dispatch-plumbing shape. Frozen-Phase-0 NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5(a). ESC-016 (epsmin recorded present, struct-copy mechanism frozen).

CKTdoJob analInfo[] job-dispatch loop (cktdojob.c:204-249): `for (i<ANALmaxnum) for(job=task->jobs) if(job->JOBtype==i) analInfo[i]->an_init/do_ic(CKTic)/an_func` plus WANT_SENSE2 skip and XSPICE EVTsetup, all-C brace/star/whitespace cosmetics. digiTS dispatches analyses through the AnalogEngine interface + coordinator (an analysis is invoked directly via MNAEngine.init/stepToTime/AcAnalysis.run / solveDcOperatingPoint), not a C `analInfo[]` JOB-struct table walked by JOBtype. No TSKtask, no JOB linked list, no analInfo[] dispatch table — the C job-table iteration has no line-isomorphic TS counterpart. Frozen-Phase-0 NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5(a) (C dispatch-table plumbing). ESC-016.
CKTfndMod C hash lookup of a model by name; hunk adds a null-ckt guard. digiTS resolves models at compile time, not via a runtime MODnameHash. PLANNING.md 5(a).

CKTinst2Node front-end query returning a bound CKTnode+name from an instance+terminal, walking the C CKTnodes list and per-instance GENnodeN fields. digiTS has no GENnode array nor CKTnodes list; node binding resolved in the compiler. Not a DC/TRAN/AC numerical path. PLANNING.md 5(a).

CKTunsetup node->ptr = NULL on icGiven||nsGiven nodes: digiTS has no node->ptr matrix-element-pointer cache nor a CKTunsetup teardown (nodeset/IC live in ctx.nodesets/ctx.ics maps, rebuilt fresh). Teardown, no counterpart.

CKTsetOpt switch(opt) setter cases (cktsopt.c:114-126): `case OPT_INDVERBOSITY: task->TSKindverbosity = val->iValue` and `case OPT_XMU: task->TSKxmu = val->rValue`. CKTsetOpt is the .options-card -> TSKtask integer-indexed option setter; digiTS has no CKTsetOpt dispatcher, no OPT_* index, no IFvalue union, and no .options card parser — options arrive as typed fields on ResolvedSimulationParams. Both params this hunk sets are present and APPLIED: indVerbosity (analog-engine-interface.ts:178/225 -> ckt-context.ts cktIndVerbosity configure() :961-963) and xmu (analog-engine-interface.ts:143/221 -> analog-engine.ts integrationXmu :753-754). This hunk is the SETTER MECHANISM (a switch-case dispatch on an integer option index) with no line-isomorphic TS counterpart, twin of the already-frozen OPTtbl[] rows h004-h008. Frozen-Phase-0 NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5(a). ESC-016 (params recorded present; setter-switch mechanism frozen). Does NOT carry cshunt (cshunt's OPT_CSHUNT setter case is in #h003, which is escalated, not buried here).

items.rationale: OPTtbl[] IFparm option-descriptor table declaration row (cktsopt.c). The OPTtbl is the ngspice option-name->integer-index descriptor table consumed by the .options deck-card parser; digiTS has no OPTtbl integer-index table and no .options card parser - it carries options as typed config fields read via CKTCircuitContext.configure(). This is a pure C declaration (IFparm struct-array row / desc-string) with no behavioral content and no line-isomorphic TS counterpart, the classic ngspice-declaration-with-no-counterpart shape. Frozen-Phase-0 NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5. ESC-007/ESC-016. Row: cshunt (OPT_CSHUNT).

items.rationale: TST0P->TSTOP user-facing error-string typo fix inside the C IFparm TRANsetParm(which, IFvalue*) dispatcher. digiTS has no IFparm param dispatcher; transient param validation (TSTOP/TSTEP/TSTART/TMAX/UIC) lives in resolveSimulationParams + facade. Cosmetic C error-string in absent machinery, no counterpart.

Whitespace-only edit inside the file's C copyright comment (removes one trailing space on `dev.c:18` between `error-free.` and the new line). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no `dev.c` file and no copyright header on `src/components/register-all.ts` — the comment text does not exist in digiTS at all. No code, no parameter, no default changes; the hunk is pure copyright-comment whitespace and the surrounding C copyright text is itself absent from the digiTS counterpart.

C XSPICE dynamic-loader prologue under `#ifdef XSPICE` — `typedef void * funptr_t;`, the `FREE_DLERR_MSG` macro pair (POSIX no-op vs Windows `free_dlerr_msg`), the `dlopen` / `dlsym` / `dlerror` Windows function-prototype block, the `RTLD_LAZY` / `RTLD_NOW` / `RTLD_GLOBAL` POSIX-compat `#define`s, the static `errstr[128]` declaration, and the `int dlclose` prototype removal. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no XSPICE code-model loader, no `dlopen`/`LoadLibrary` shared-library code-model integration, and no C preprocessor (`#ifdef`, `#define`, `typedef`). Components in digiTS are static ES-module imports in `src/components/register-all.ts` registered through `ComponentRegistry.register()`; there is no dynamic-library plugin surface to mirror this. No behavioral content for the simulator engine.

Under `#ifdef XSPICE`: moves the `extern struct coreInfo_t coreInfo` (from `cmexport.c`) declaration from the body of `load_opus` up to a file-scope extern declaration, and replaces the `/*saj*/` comment marker with a blank line.
