Pure C-comment fix on the `DEVicesfl` global flag-array comment: `/*Flag to indicate that device type it is,` → `/*Flag to indicate what device type it is,` (one typo correction inside an `#ifdef XSPICE`-gated global). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no `DEVicesfl` global, no XSPICE flag-array, and the comment text does not exist in digiTS. No code, no parameter, no default changes.

Adds `DEVNUM = 0;` to `spice_destroy_devices()` after the `tfree(DEVices)`. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: `DEVNUM` is the `static int` device count at file scope of `dev.c`, paired with the C heap-allocated `DEVices` array. digiTS has no `DEVNUM` integer global, no `tfree`/`free` heap teardown (the component registry is held by the `ComponentRegistry` instance and freed by ES garbage collection), and no `spice_destroy_devices` function — the registry is reset by dropping the instance reference. No behavioral counterpart.

Inside `spice_init_devices`: (1) adds `DEVNUM = NUMELEMS(static_devices);` with the comment `/* Safeguard against double initialization */`; (2) corrects the typo `Initilise` → `Initialize` in an `#ifdef XSPICE`-gated comment; (3) drops a trailing-whitespace line. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no `spice_init_devices` function, no `DEVNUM` integer, no `static_devices[]` C array, and no XSPICE g_evt_udn_info table. The double-init protection in digiTS is `ComponentRegistry.register()` (`registry.ts:538-540`) which throws `"<name>" is already registered` when called twice for the same component — a different protection mechanism for a different (single) global, not the C-static-count reset. No new behavior to mirror.

The `#ifdef DEVLIB` `DEVICES_USED` `#define` (used by `load_alldevs` to dlopen each named device library). In v41 both the ADMS and non-ADMS branches gain `hisimhv2`, plus the ADMS branch swaps `hicum2` out and adds `psp103`/`bsimbulk`/`bsimcmg`, and the non-ADMS branch adds `hicum2`. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no `DEVLIB`, no `dlopen`-based per-device shared-library loading, and no C preprocessor `#define`. The behavioral facts the macro encodes (which device classes exist in v41) are also irrelevant here: every new name added or moved by the hunk (`hisimhv2`, `hicum2`, `psp103`, `bsimbulk`, `bsimcmg`) is OUT per device-class-scope.md (frozen 2026-05-21) foundry-compact-model list.

Reorganizes the C preprocessor gating around the `relink()` helper: `#ifdef XSPICE` becomes `#if defined(XSPICE) || defined(OSDI)`, and the `#include "ngspice/mif.h"` / `"ngspice/cm.h"` / `"ngspice/cpextern.h"` are moved out of the `XSPICE`-gated block (with `"ngspice/fteext.h"` and `"ngspice/cktdefs.h"` left in the new OR-gated block). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no `#ifdef`, no `#include`, no XSPICE / OSDI shared-library extension points, and no `relink()` (the function that fixes up `ft_sim->devices` / `DEVmaxnum` after dynamic device addition — both ngspice globals digiTS does not carry). No behavioral content.

The `add_device` XSPICE runtime-registration function. The hunk: (1) closes the `#endif` for the OR-gated relink block + reopens `#ifdef XSPICE` with the moved includes; (2) factors a `dnum = DEVNUM + n` local; (3) adds `DEVices[DEVNUM+i]->DEVmodSize = &MIFmSize;` alongside the existing `MIFiSize` assignment (the XSPICE per-device model-size pointer fix-up to match the existing instance-size pointer fix-up); (4) drops the `/* added by SDB on 6.20.2003 */` migration-narrative comment. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no `add_device`, no XSPICE runtime device addition (all components are static-registered via `register-all.ts`), no `MIFiSize`/`MIFmSize` (XSPICE code-model instance/model size pointers — ngspice C-heap allocator metadata), and no `DEVices`/`DEVNUM` C heap globals. The XSPICE code-model plugin surface is wholly absent from digiTS.

items.rationale: The XSPICE `add_udn` (add user-defined-node-type) function: factors `int utypes = g_evt_num_udn_types + n;` as a local. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no XSPICE UDN (user-defined-node) infrastructure, no `g_evt_udn_info` global array, no `Evt_Udn_Info_t` type, and no `add_udn` runtime extension surface. ngspice's UDN system is the event-driven code-model type system (digital-node + custom XSPICE node types); digiTS has no analogue. No behavioral content.

Large rewrite covering: (1) `load_opus` reformatted with explicit error-message dispatch (printf + FREE_DLERR_MSG paths through each dlsym failure), and the function signature tightened to `const char *name`; (2) move of the `extern coreInfo` declaration out of `load_opus` (paired with h003); (3) new Windows `dlerror`-message-buffer infrastructure: `errstr_fmt` template, sized `errstr` buffer, `free_dlerr_msg` helper, and a `FormatMessage`-fail fallback path inside Windows `dlerror`; (4) a whole new `#ifdef OSDI` block adding `osdi_add_device` + `load_osdi` (the v41 OSDI plugin entry point, with `OsdiObjectFile` / `OsdiRegistryEntry` / `osdi_create_spicedev`). PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no XSPICE code-model loader, no Windows `LoadLibrary`/`dlopen` shared-library loader, no OSDI Verilog-A-derived device extension format (`ref/ngspice/src/osdi/`), and no `coreInfo_t` global. None of `load_opus`, `osdi_add_device`, `load_osdi`, `free_dlerr_msg`, or the new OSDI types map onto any digiTS function. No behavioral content for the static-build digiTS engine.

C extern-prototype header for `dev.c`. The hunk only (1) tightens `load_opus(char *)` to `load_opus(const char *)` (a C constness edit on a dynamic-library entry point), and (2) adds `int load_osdi(const char *)` for the new OSDI plugin loader. PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: digiTS has no prototype headers — function signatures are TypeScript class/method declarations. Neither `load_opus` nor `load_osdi` has a digiTS counterpart (both are XSPICE/OSDI shared-library entry points; digiTS is a static-build with no plugin surface). No surviving declaration carries new behavior.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/newton-raphson.ts — new `limitlog` (or `devLimitLog`) export sibling of the existing pnjlim/fetlim/limvds family (newton-raphson.ts:100/185/243). v41 adds `DEVlimitlog(deltemp, deltemp_old, LIM_TOL, *check)`, a logarithmic damping helper for per-iteration `deltemp` (junction/device temperature delta), used by self-heating IN-class load paths. The function follows the same shape as the existing limiters (returns the damped value, sets `*check`/`limited` when damping engaged, prints once on NaN). Per device-class-scope.md (frozen 2026-05-21) "Engine-side consequences": DEVlimitlog is PORT — generic NR limiting helper, callable by any device including IN classes. Q3 resolved in the simpler direction (option A): this overlay exports the v41-new helper into `newton-raphson.ts`; per-device wirings are owned by per-device passes (BJT / dio / vdmos / etc.), each of which will call this helper from its own load path as the per-device v41 hunks land.
verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/devsup.c:153-184 (DEVlimitlog), v41-NEW all-additions hunk (addedLines 33, removedLines 0, no v26 baseline). ngspice->digiTS correspondence re-derived independently (rename-maps NOT consulted); fresh v41 read of devsup.c:153-184 matches the device-dispatch.md slice (docLineRange 546-585) byte-for-byte. Applier ported into newton-raphson.ts as exported `limitlog` (sibling of pnjlim/fetlim/limvds), with `LimitlogResult{value,check}` + module-level `_limitlogResult` + `_limitlogShown` latch. Independent identifier map: deltemp_old->deltempOld, LIM_TOL->limTol, int *check->returned LimitlogResult.check, static bool shown->module `let _limitlogShown` (C function-static->module latch, sec4 surface form), FALSE/TRUE->false/true, isnan->Number.isNaN, fprintf(stderr)->console.error, log10->Math.log10. Body bijective line-for-line: `*check=0`->`let check=0`; NaN guard `if(!shown && (isnan(deltemp)||isnan(deltemp_old)))` condition+operand+&&/|| order identical, 3 stderr strings same+order, deltemp=0.0, *check=1, shown=TRUE->_limitlogShown=true; upper clamp `deltemp = deltemp_old + LIM_TOL + log10((deltemp-deltemp_old)/LIM_TOL)` operand-for-operand identical; lower clamp `deltemp = deltemp_old - LIM_TOL - log10((deltemp_old-deltemp)/LIM_TOL)` operand-for-operand identical incl. the asymmetric swapped numerator (deltemp_old-deltemp); both arms set *check=1; `return deltemp`->set .value/.check then return struct. Two-output contract (damped deltemp AND *check) and the once-only `static bool shown` latch semantics faithfully modeled. No forbidden shapes: no operand reorder, no factor-out/inline, no algebraic simplification, no merged/split statements, no nesting change. sec1a harness gate N/A: single all-additions hunk (not reconstruction, not device completion) and the helper is unwired into any solve path. The co-located shouldReorder/NISHOULDREORDER refactor in the same file diff is a separate ledger item, out of scope for devsup.c#h001.

items.rationale: Parameter-name correction inside `ACM_SourceDrainResistances` (`double *drainConductance`/`*sourceConductance` → `double *drainResistance`/`*sourceResistance`). Per device-class-scope.md (frozen 2026-05-21) "Engine-side consequences" section: the ACM helper family (`ACM_SourceDrainResistances`, `ACM_saturationCurrents`, `ACM_junctionCapacitances`, devsup.c:186-561, ~375 LOC) is NO-COUNTERPART by transitive consequence. Sole callers are BSIM3 (verified at bsim3/b3temp.c:820, bsim3/b3set.c:1002, bsim3/b3ld.c:402, bsim3/b3ld.c:2299) and BSIM3v32 (verified at bsim3v32/b3v32temp.c:890, bsim3v32/b3v32temp.c:953, bsim3v32/b3v32set.c:1010, bsim3v32/b3v32ld.c:397, bsim3v32/b3v32ld.c:2582, bsim3v32/b3v32ld.c:2612), and both BSIM3 and BSIM3v32 are OUT per the foundry-compact-model list. With no IN-class consumer, the helper has no place in digiTS — the parameter-rename has no body to apply onto.

Propagation of the h002 parameter rename through the `ACM_SourceDrainResistances` body — the four case-1/11/2/12/3/13 assignments `*drainConductance` → `*drainResistance` and `*sourceConductance` → `*sourceResistance`. Same transitive-consequence verdict as h002 per device-class-scope.md (frozen 2026-05-21) "Engine-side consequences" section: sole callers BSIM3 (b3temp.c:820, b3set.c:1002, b3ld.c:402, b3ld.c:2299) and BSIM3v32 (b3v32temp.c:890, b3v32temp.c:953, b3v32set.c:1010, b3v32ld.c:397, b3v32ld.c:2582, b3v32ld.c:2612) are OUT per the foundry-compact-model list; the helper has no IN-class consumer and is NO-COUNTERPART.

verifierNotes: Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/devsup.c:653-665 (DevCapVDMOS), v41-NEW all-additions hunk (device-dispatch.md:625-647). Bijective construct correspondence re-derived independently (rename-maps NOT consulted), fresh v41 read: double s=(cgdmax-cgdmin)/(1+M_PI/2) <-> const s=(cgdmax-cgdmin)/(1+Math.PI/2) (operands/grouping identical, M_PI->Math.PI); double y=cgdmax-s <-> const y=cgdmax-s; if(vgd>0) *capgd=0.5*(s*tanh(a*vgd)+y) else *capgd=0.5*(s*atan(a*vgd)+y) <-> ternary vgd>0 ? 0.5*(s*Math.tanh(a*vgd)+y) : 0.5*(s*Math.atan(a*vgd)+y) (condition, branch arms, tanh/atan split, operand order all identical); *capgs=0.5*cgs <-> const capgs=0.5*cgs; out-pointers *capgs,*capgd <-> returned {capgs,capgd} (C<->TS surface form, allowed). No construct dropped, no operand reorder, no algebraic simplification, no forbidden shape. PLACEMENT: the contract maps tsFile=vdmos.ts, but DevCapVDMOS lives in ngspice devsup.c (shared device-support, NOT a device file); digiTS counterpart is src/solver/analog/newton-raphson.ts:373 export function devCapVdmos (sibling of pnjlim/fetlim/limvds/limitlog) -- the IDENTICAL architectural choice already accepted for devsup.c#h001 (DEVlimitlog->newton-raphson.ts limitlog, APPLIED). It is CALLED from the VDMOS load path at src/components/semiconductors/vdmos.ts:1054 (devCapVdmos(vgd,cgdmin,cgdmax,a,cgs) -> s0 CAPGS/CAPGD), so the vdmos.ts integration the contract references is the call site; the helper definition is the shared file. Already committed at cc981161 (ancestor of recon 813f321f); no working-tree delta. sec1a harness gate N/A here (single all-additions helper hunk; whole-device harness parity already established null by recon vdmos#recon/wholeClass on vdmos-power-switch.dts). items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/ckt-context.ts (CKTepsmin: minimum argument floor for log() in junction saturation-current evaluation; default 1e-28 -> no default-behavior change per #58). DEVnameHash/MODnameHash are unchanged context (shell device/model symbol tables), not added lines.
verifierNotes: MATCH. functionGroup include-ngspice/cktdefs.h::struct CKTcircuit {; tsFile src/solver/analog/ckt-context.ts. Sub-item slice docLineRange[579-586] of split hunk h012 adds exactly `double CKTepsmin; /* minimum argument value for some log functions, e.g. diode saturation current*/` (diff line 583) + one blank line; the DEVnameHash/MODnameHash lines are unchanged context and the noise_input add is carved to sibling sub-item #h012b (NO-COUNTERPART, frozen). Verified against fresh ref/ngspice/src/include/ngspice/cktdefs.h:323 (CKTepsmin member) and cktinit.c:94 (sckt->CKTepsmin = 1e-28) by hand; rename-maps NOT consulted. TIER1 (zero-delta blocked-hunk): the construct `double CKTepsmin` <-> ckt-context.ts:346 `cktEpsmin: number = 1e-28` is PRESENT, placed by the blocked-by reconstruction item include-ngspice#recon/epsmin (APPLIED, hunkHash 909f8bd025236ffc, commit d082bac0). Per TASK.md sec6 blocked-hunk semantics the pre-image is case (a) once the recon is APPLIED; the field default 1e-28 matches cktinit.c:94 exactly, the cited cktdefs.h:323 declaration is verbatim. Allowed C<->TS differences only: `double`->`number`, identifier CKTepsmin->cktEpsmin, comment wording. The applier's incremental delta is zero because the field was landed by the recon, NOT because a real change was dropped -- the added construct is present and correct. PASS. TIER2 (bijective, struct-member add): the v41 struct member `double CKTepsmin` maps one-to-one to the class field cktEpsmin (same semantic = minimum log-argument floor for diode/VDMOS saturation current, same default 1e-28, correct type and citation); no unmapped v41 construct, no digiTS construct without a v41 counterpart. PASS. FILE-SCOPE: git diff src/solver/analog/ckt-context.ts empty (field committed via recon commit d082bac0); rename-maps/include-ngspice.md committed. The other working-tree-modified files (bjt.ts/mosfet.ts = pre-existing AC stampAc work per the gitStatus snapshot and the recon verifierNote; ledger/state files) are NOT edits of this group -- no over-application attributable to include-ngspice/#h012a. Source-only single-hunk per VERIFICATION.md sec1; the section1a harness gate was carried by the blocked-by reconstruction (null firstDivergence on resistive-divider.dts).

The N* functions are the noise iteration (NaskQuest/NsetParm) and the wrapper is C++ ABI plumbing; the changed lines belong to PSS/RFSPICE/noise/C++-linkage, none of which digiTS implements. No behavioral counterpart.

Prototype-only `extern void NIresetwarnmsg(void)`. The function (niiter.c) resets the file-static `msgcount` that throttles ngspice's 'singular matrix' warnings to 6 per run — a frontend warning-throttle plumbing layer. digiTS has no msgcount-style singular-matrix-warning throttle and no per-analysis warning-counter reset (it surfaces singular-matrix through its own diagnostics), so there is no ported function for this prototype to declare. NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5(a). (#62.)

The RFSPICE/C++/temper-expr changes all belong to subsystems digiTS does not implement. No behavioral counterpart.

complex.h is documented "Some defines used mainly in cmath.c" -- the FTE shell complex-expression evaluator: cph/cmag/radtodeg/degtorad reference the shell global cx_degrees, and rcheck writes to cp_err / win_x_fprintf (CLI error stream). The struct ngcomplex / cuComplex typedefs back the shell dvec store. digiTS computes AC small-signal via SparseSolver complex mode (per project memory: ComplexSparseSolver deleted, AC uses SparseSolver complex mode) and formats magnitude/phase in ac-analysis.ts -- it does not consume these CLI cmath macros. No behavioral counterpart in the engine.

items.rationale: Hunk adds IF_REDUNDANT variants of the IOP/IP/OP IFparm-table construction macros (IOPUR/IOPPR/IOPAR/.../IPR/OPUR). These macros build ngspice IFparmTable rows whose IF_REDUNDANT flag marks a parameter as an alias hidden from listings -- a property of the ngspice IFparm parameter-table / ask-set introspection surface. digiTS expresses model parameters via its model registry and property bag, not IFparm tables; the IF_REDUNDANT alias-flag macros have no behavioral counterpart.

ngspice command-interpreter / front-end (FTE+CP) artifact: device-generator iterator struct (shell "show" command). digiTS has no SPICE command shell, plot manager, dvec store, wordlist, or interactive parser; programmatic access is via DefaultSimulatorFacade + CircuitSpec (src/headless/default-facade.ts), not a deck/command loop. C-language plumbing with no behavioral existence in the DC/TRAN/AC engine (analysis-scope.md).

XSPICE event-driven / code-model (MIF) simulation artifact: XSPICE general-enhancements (rshunt/ramp/convlimit) structs. digiTS implements its digital domain natively (src/components + unified compiler), not via ngspice's XSPICE event queue / code-model interface. This whole subsystem (evt/cm/mif/enh/inertial) has no behavioral existence in the digiTS analog DC/TRAN/AC engine; guarded by #ifdef XSPICE in ngspice. C-language plumbing.

Drops the named GENnode1..GENnode7 fields from struct GENinstance and replaces them with a flexible trailing-array accessor `static inline int *GENnode(struct GENinstance *inst){ return (int*)(inst+1); }`. This is pure C-ABI memory-layout plumbing: it changes WHERE a C struct stores its node indices (named fields -> trailing int[] past the base struct, via pointer arithmetic), not WHICH external node each pin maps to. No behavioral content — node values are unchanged. digiTS represents device nodes via its pin/slot schema (element.ts) + compiler node-id assignment (compiler.ts), with no GENinstance base struct and no analogue to 'place node ints right after the base'. NO-COUNTERPART per PLANNING.md §5(a). (#64.)

iferrmsg.h defines ngspice's IFerror integer return codes (E_*); the hunk adds E_EXISTS_BAD for duplicate instance/model creation. digiTS does not use ngspice's IFerror integer protocol; it surfaces errors through its own diagnostic-code system (diagnostics.ts) and CircuitSpec validation. These integer constants have no behavioral existence in digiTS.

Hunk swaps the #ifdef XSPICE include from mifparse.h+mifcmdat.h to miftypes.h (forward-typedef split for the XSPICE MIF code-model structs referenced in IFdevice). XSPICE include-management plumbing; the XSPICE code-model interface is not implemented. No counterpart.

Hunk adds `#ifdef OSDI const void *registry_entry;` to struct IFdevice -- the per-device registry pointer for the OSDI loader (Verilog-A compiled models via libosdi). OSDI is a compiled-model loading subsystem digiTS does not implement (it has no OSDI/Verilog-A loader); the field is guarded by #ifdef OSDI. No behavioral counterpart.

sim.h's enum simulation_types tags FTE output-vector physical types. The single v41 hunk replaces the noise output labels SV_OUTPUT_N_DENS/SV_OUTPUT_NOISE/SV_INPUT_N_DENS/SV_INPUT_NOISE with SV_VOLTAGE_DENSITY/SV_CURRENT_DENSITY/SV_SQR_* -- all of which are noise-analysis spectral-density output types. Noise analysis is not implemented (analysis-scope.md); digiTS has no FTE output-vector type tagging. No behavioral counterpart.

trcvdefs.h defines the TRCV job struct and DCT_START1.. param-index ids consumed by ngspice's INP/IFparm parser for the .dc transfer-curve card. digiTS has no SPICE deck parser; DC sweeps are driven through DefaultSimulatorFacade + parameter-sweep.ts against a CircuitSpec, not via these card-parser param indices. The enum has no behavioral existence in the engine. (This concerns the deck-parser surface, not the DC analysis itself, which is implemented.)

items.rationale: C-header struct-member/macro/prototype; digiTS has no CKTcircuit/ngspice-header layer, no separable TS image — verified ABSENT.

New Doxygen \dir descriptor: 'all functions for dealing with vectors in the control language'. Pure documentation artifact (PLANNING.md s5 case a).

Automake fragment building libdense.la. C build-system plumbing (case-(a) C-language artifact) with no behavioral content. The library it produces is linked only by the S-parameter (RFSPICE) analysis, which digiTS does not implement (case-(b), analysis-scope.md positive verdict: DC-OP/TRAN/AC only).

Entire dense Mat/CMat matrix library (new file in v41, 1280 lines). Positive absence evidence: the only ngspice callers of these symbols (newcmat/cmultiply/cinverse/cdet/rowechelon/lu/qr/CMat structs) are span.c, cktspdum.c (CKTspCalcSMatrix), and nevalsrc.c, every include guarded by #ifdef RFSPICE - i.e. the S-parameter analysis introduced in v41 (span.c header: Alessio Cacciatori 2021; includes ngspice/spardefs.h). S-parameter is a whole SPICE analysis digiTS does not implement (analysis-scope.md implements DC-OP, transient, AC small-signal only). digiTS's DC/TRAN/AC linear-algebra is the sparse solver (src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts, Sparse 1.3a), which has no dependency on this dense library. Case-(b) per PLANNING.md §5.

Public header for dense.c: cplx/Mat/CMat/MatList typedefs and prototypes. Included only inside #ifdef RFSPICE (span.c:27). Same positive evidence as dense.c#h001: backs the S-parameter (RFSPICE) analysis, which digiTS does not implement (analysis-scope.md). Case-(b) per PLANNING.md §5.

Static-inline complex-scalar arithmetic helpers backing the dense matrix library. Included only inside #ifdef RFSPICE (span.c:28, nevalsrc.c:37). Same positive evidence as dense.c#h001: exclusive to the S-parameter (RFSPICE) analysis, not implemented by digiTS (analysis-scope.md). Case-(b) per PLANNING.md §5.

fftInit() (cos-table + bit-reversal-table allocator for the ngspice split-radix FFT). C-language artifact (case a): the hunk only changes `UtblArray[M] == 0`/`BRLowArray[...] == 0` pointer comparisons to `== NULL` -- pointer-style hygiene, zero behavioral content. The enclosing fftInit lives only in the frontend `fft` command and 1-f-code.c noise synthesizer call graphs, neither of which is in digiTS's DC-OP/transient/AC scope (analysis-scope.md). digiTS has no counterpart to this allocator (src/runtime/fft.ts is an unrelated radix-2 UI FFT that builds its twiddle factors inline, no malloc'd table cache).

fftInit() (real-FFT bit-reversal-table branch). C-language artifact (case a): only `BRLowArray[(M-1)/2] == 0` -> `== NULL` pointer-comparison hygiene, no behavioral content. Same scope basis as #h001: fftInit has no consumer in digiTS's three implemented analyses and no digiTS counterpart.

fftFree() (releases the malloc'd cos/bit-reversal tables). C-language artifact (case a): the hunk swaps `!= 0`->`!= NULL` comparisons and the C heap-free `free()`->`tfree()` (ngspice's NULL-safe txfree wrapper that also nulls the pointer), `= 0`->`= NULL`. Pure C heap-management plumbing -- per PLANNING.md §4, `<dev>del/dest/free` heap-free has no counterpart in GC'd JS. Its sole transient-path caller (vsrcacct.c/isrcacct.c) invokes it only to clean up the 1/f-noise synthesis tables, a TRNOISE-waveform feature digiTS does not implement (digiTS ports only the TRNOISE breakpoint *schedule*, not the FFT-based 1/f value synthesis).

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/timestep.ts::almostEqualUlps
items.rationale: Deletes the file-local C macro `#define int64_min (((int64_t) -1) << 63)`. This is C-language scaffolding (a typed integer constant); it carries no behavioral content of AlmostEqualUlps and has no digiTS form - timestep.ts uses BigInt and an early-return opposite-sign guard, never an int64 lexicographic reorder. NO-COUNTERPART case (a): C-macro/constant cleanup, no behavioral change (both forms equal 0x8000000000000000). Pre-existing algorithmic divergence in the opposite-sign path is raised as open question Q1, not buried here.

NO-COUNTERPART case (a): dead C fallback file removed, no IN-scope consumer.
Adds `#include "ngspice/randnumb.h"`. C prototype-header plumbing; digiTS has no randnumb module or header. NO-COUNTERPART case (a): C include/prototype-header plumbing.

verifierNotes: MATCH. functionGroup niaciter.c::Modified 1999 Emmanuel Rouat; tsFile src/solver/analog/ac-analysis.ts (AcAnalysis frequency-sweep loop, reorder/LUfac dispatch :429). Verified against fresh ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/niaciter.c:106-117 (NIacIter signature + local decls + retry: label), v41 diff spec/ngspice-v41-engine-diffs/maths-ni.md docLineRange 107-115. The only real -/+ line of #h001b is the removal of `double *temp;` (the C scratch pointer that backed the manual three-line temp-swap), which v41 replaces with the SWAP macro; digiTS has no manual-pointer-swap temp (it uses _swapAcRhs at ac-analysis.ts:458/524-526), so `double *temp` has no TS counterpart (allowed C->TS difference, sec4). `int ignore;`/`double startTime;`/`int error;` are reorder-stat/error scratch with no digiTS counterpart (digiTS does not track STATreorderTime/STATdecompTime; the dispatch surfaces the error code as `const err`). PASS. TIER2 (bijective, fresh v41 read): NIacIter's `ckt->CKTnoncon=0`+CKTacLoad maps to solver._resetForAssembly()+stampAc fill/accumulate (ac-analysis.ts:380-399); the NIACSHOULDREORDER reorder-vs-LUfac dispatch (niaciter.c:123-152) maps to `const err = fi === 0 ? solver.orderAndFactor() : solver.factor()` (:429) -- fi===0 selects SMPcReorder at the first frequency, reuse SMPcLUfac after; the `retry:`/goto E_SINGULAR loop is the spOrderAndFactor singular path inside orderAndFactor. No v41 construct without a digiTS counterpart over the niaciter signature region. FILE-SCOPE: working-tree git diff source = integration.ts (group nicomcof#h003) + bjt.ts/mosfet.ts (pre-existing AC-stampAc sibling work, present at session start per gitStatus snapshot, zero niaciter/AC-RHS-buffer content) -- not over-application by any maths-ni group. Every -/+ pair of #h002 is an allowed C->TS difference: the six `*ckt->CKTrhs = 0;`...`*ckt->CKTirhsOld = 0;` pointer-deref clears become `this._acRhsRe[0]=0;`...`this._acRhsOldIm[0]=0;` index clears (pointer-deref vs [0] indexing, sec4), in the IDENTICAL ngspice order rhs/rhsSpare/rhsOld/irhs/irhsSpare/irhsOld (ac-analysis.ts:449-454); the two manual three-line temp-swaps `temp=irhsOld; irhsOld=irhs; irhs=temp;` then the rhs swap become SWAP(double*, irhs, irhsOld) then SWAP(double*, rhs, rhsOld) which digiTS realizes as `_swapAcRhs()` swapping the imag pair first then the real pair (:524-526) -- same swap targets, same order (imag-then-real). PASS. TIER2 (bijective, fresh v41 read): SMPcSolve maps to solver.solve(_acRhsRe,_acRhsRe,_acRhsIm,_acRhsIm) in-place (:444); the six ground-sentinel clears map one-to-one and in order; the two SWAPs map to _swapAcRhs imag-then-real; post-swap the solution lives in _acRhsOld{Re,Im} (= CKTrhsOld/CKTirhsOld), read by the output-extraction loop (:462-475). items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/integration.ts solveGearVandermonde ag-zeroing (:169-175)
verifierNotes: MATCH. functionGroup nicomcof.c::NIcomCof(CKTcircuit *ckt); tsFile src/solver/analog/integration.ts (solveGearVandermonde ag-zeroing :165-171). NOTE the task wrapper labelled this group hunks=[nicomcof.c#h001], but #h001 (functionGroup `Author: 1985 Thomas L. Quarles`, the cpextern.h include + xmu #define removal) and #h002 (case-2 CKTxmu) are ALREADY APPLIED; the ag-zeroing edit the applier actually made is the NIcomCof functionGroup hunk #h003 (the task mapping `ag-zeroing :169-175` + functionGroup name `NIcomCof` identify it unambiguously). Verdict recorded against #h003 accordingly. Verified against fresh ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/nicomcof.c:65-66, v41 diff maths-ni.md docLineRange 174-182. Correspondence re-derived independently (rename-maps NOT consulted): ckt->CKTag <-> ag (Float64Array, the integration coefficient vector); ckt->CKTdelta <-> dt. TIER1: working-tree git diff of integration.ts is NON-empty and line-isomorphic to the hunk. The v41 hunk's real -/+ pair is `-bzero(ckt->CKTag,7*sizeof(double))` -> `+memset(ckt->CKTag, 0, 7*sizeof(double))` (both clear the full 7-wide CKTag array; bzero and memset(.,0,.) are the same byte-clear). The applier changed digiTS from `for (let i = 0; i <= order; i++) ag[i] = 0;` (a too-narrow v26 baseline that cleared only slots 0..order) to `ag.fill(0, 0, 7);` -- the byte-span isomorph of memset(CKTag,0,7*sizeof(double)) over the full 7-wide vector. The following `ag[1] = -1 / dt;` is unchanged and matches `ckt->CKTag[1] = -1/ckt->CKTdelta;` (nicomcof.c:66). This is a v26-baseline correction folded with the v41 hunk (the old narrow clear left slots [order+1..6] stale across calls; the full memset matches ngspice). PASS. TIER2 (bijective): solveGearVandermonde clears the whole 7-wide ag (matching memset), sets ag[1]=-1/dt, then builds the Vandermonde matrix and LU-solves for the Gear coefficients (nicomcof.c:67-115) -- the matrix-build / LU-decompose / forward-back substitution constructs were not touched by this hunk and remain construct-for-construct with nicomcof.c. No v41 construct unmapped. FILE-SCOPE: working-tree git diff source = integration.ts (this group) + rename-maps/maths-ni.md + bjt.ts/mosfet.ts (pre-existing AC-stampAc sibling, zero nicomcof/CKTag content). Source-only single-hunk per sec1.

items.rationale: The CKTagp `bzero`->`memset` change lives inside the `#ifdef PREDICTOR` block (nicomcof.c:128-206). PREDICTOR is `#undef`'d in ngspice's default build (ref/ngspice/visualc/include/ngspice/config.h:475 `/* #undef PREDICTOR */`); the agp predictor-coefficient machinery has no behavioral existence in the reference build digiTS targets, and digiTS deliberately mirrors that default (analog-engine.ts:384-390: 'no predictor, no agp, no CKTpred buffer'). Raised as open question Q1 for explicit ratification.

verifierNotes: MATCH. functionGroup niconv.c::NIconvTest(CKTcircuit *ckt); tsFile src/solver/analog/newton-raphson.ts (niConvTest isnan early-return :424-426). Verified against fresh ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/niconv.c:39-59, v41 diff maths-ni.md docLineRange 205-237. Correspondence re-derived independently (rename-maps NOT consulted): ckt->CKTrhs[i]/CKTrhsOld[i] <-> ctx.rhs[i]/ctx.rhsOld[i]; CKTreltol/CKTvoltTol/CKTabstol <-> ctx.reltol/ctx.voltTol/ctx.iabstol; CKTtroubleNode/CKTtroubleElt <-> ctx.troubleNode/ctx.troubleElt; new/old <-> newV/oldV. TIER1: the applier delta is the sibling nrRealign recon edit to newton-raphson.ts (NON-empty; git show 17b40e89 adds `if (Number.isNaN(newV)) return 1;` at niConvTest:424-426) -- not dropped. The v41 hunk's substantive +/- is the NEW isnan block: `+if (isnan(new)) { if (ft_ngdebug) fprintf(...); return 1; }` (niconv.c:43-47); digiTS realizes the numerical half `if (Number.isNaN(newV)) return 1;`. The `if (ft_ngdebug) fprintf(stderr, ...nan...)` is a frontend verbose-debug print; digiTS has no `set ngdebug` control -> NO-COUNTERPART (allowed, sec4), only the return-1 numerical effect is mirrored. The rest of #h002 is tab->space reindent of the STEPDEBUG printf + `ckt->CKTtroubleNode=i`/`CKTtroubleElt=NULL` (whitespace, allowed). PASS. TIER2 (bijective, fresh v41 read): niConvTest maps to NIconvTest construct-for-construct -- 1-based [1,size] loop tests the same row SET as digiTS [0,matrixSize) (row 0 = ground sentinel, rhs[0] forced 0 post-solve); the SP_VOLTAGE voltTol arm vs the branch abstol arm (niconv.c:48-50,60-62) is realized by `tol = i < nodeCount ? reltol*max(|old|,|new|)+voltTol : reltol*max(|old|,|new|)+iabstol` -- the if/else in v41 exists solely to pick the tolerance constant, and the digiTS ternary picks the SAME constant; the comparison `fabs(new-old) > tol` and the blame `troubleNode=i; troubleElt=null; return 1` are byte-identical in both v41 arms and run once in digiTS (same control flow: per row, pick tol by type, compare, blame+return). NEWCONV tail return(0) -> return 0 (niconv.c:81). No v41 construct unmapped. FILE-SCOPE: working-tree diff = integration.ts + bjt.ts/mosfet.ts (pre-existing sibling, zero niconv content); newton-raphson.ts changes are the nrRealign recon commit. Source-only single-hunk per sec1; the nrRealign recon carries the null-firstDivergence DC/tran gate.

items.rationale: C heap free of predictor arrays (CKTpred, CKTsols[]) inside `#ifdef PREDICTOR`. Two independent grounds for absence: (a) JS is garbage-collected, so a FREE() block has no behavioral counterpart (PLANNING.md §4 del/dest row); (b) PREDICTOR is `#undef`'d in ngspice's default build (config.h:475) and digiTS implements no predictor buffers (analog-engine.ts:384-390).

NIdIter is the distortion-analysis iteration solver; its sole callers in ngspice are distoan.c (verified ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/analysis/distoan.c:287-414). Distortion analysis is NOT implemented in digiTS (analysis-scope.md: no distortion engine file, AnalogEngine method, or MCP tool).

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts niIntegrate TRAP cases (:31-37)
verifierNotes: MISMATCH (Tier 2, whole-function bijection fail). Tier 1 PASSES: hunk maths-ni.md docLineRange 350-365 is whitespace-only (every +/- pair removes trailing whitespace / shifts a line break; TRAP order-1 `CKTstate0[ccap]=CKTag[0]*CKTstate0[qcap]+CKTag[1]*CKTstate1[qcap]` and order-2 `-CKTstate1[ccap]*CKTag[1]+CKTag[0]*(CKTstate0[qcap]-CKTstate1[qcap])` arithmetic unchanged), so a zero-line TS delta is line-isomorphic per VERIFICATION.md sec4 zero-delta rule; ni-integrate.ts:33 and :36 match the v41 TRAP arithmetic operand-for-operand. Tier 2 FAILS on the GEAR case: ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/niinteg.c:42-64 computes state0[ccap]=0 then accumulates HIGH->LOW via case fall-through (order 6: +=ag[6]*state6, ...,+=ag[2]*state2, +=ag[1]*state1, +=ag[0]*state0). src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts:44-46 accumulates LOW->HIGH: `ccap = ag[0]*q0 + ag[1]*q1` then `for k=2..order: ccap += ag[k]*qHistory[k-2]`. At the default GEAR order 2 (state-pool.ts:71 maxOrder=2) v41 evaluates ag[2]*s2 + ag[1]*s1 + ag[0]*s0 whereas ni-integrate.ts evaluates ag[0]*s0 + ag[1]*s1 + ag[2]*s2 -- a reordered floating-point summation (VERIFICATION.md sec4 forbidden: 'reordered operands in an arithmetic expression'; CLAUDE.md treats load() / integration accumulation order as bit-load-bearing). Remedy (within-file, in scope for ni-integrate.ts): rewrite the GEAR branch to accumulate from highest order down to ag[0], mirroring niinteg.c:42-64 fall-through order (initialise ccap=0; add ag[order]*q[order] first, ag[0]*q0 last). This is not the hunk's TRAP delta but it is part of the NIintegrate function that Tier 2 requires to be bijective.

APPLIER ROUND 2 RESPONSE (attempt 1): Fixed the named GEAR-accumulation-order divergence in src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts. The GEAR branch now mirrors niinteg.c:43-64 fall-through order: `ccap = 0` (niinteg.c:43), then `for (let k = order; k >= 2; k--) ccap += ag[k] * (qHistory[k - 2] ?? 0)` (the case <order>...case 2 fall-through chain, highest order first, niinteg.c:47-59), then `ccap += ag[1] * q1` (case 1 first add, niinteg.c:62) then `ccap += ag[0] * q0` (case 1 second add, niinteg.c:63). At default GEAR order 2 this now evaluates 0 + ag[2]*s2 + ag[1]*s1 + ag[0]*s0, operand-for-operand identical to v41. TRAP branch unchanged (already operand-for-operand identical, zero-line delta for hunk #h001 itself). qHistory[k-2] maps state2..state6 -> qHistory[0..4]. No other file touched (in-file, in functionGroup scope per TASK.md §2).

VERIFIER ROUND 3 (attempt 1, separate context). Tier 1 PASSES (unchanged): hunk maths-ni.md docLineRange 350-365 is whitespace-only; the TS delta touches only the GEAR branch, so the TRAP region delta is empty and line-isomorphic per VERIFICATION.md sec4 zero-delta rule (ni-integrate.ts:33 TRAP order-1 = ag[0]*q0+ag[1]*q1 == niinteg.c:28-29; ni-integrate.ts:36 TRAP order-2 = -ccapPrev*ag[1]+ag[0]*(q0-q1) == niinteg.c:32-34, operand-for-operand). GEAR-accumulation-order divergence from round 2 is now FIXED and confirmed bijective: ni-integrate.ts:46-53 (ccap=0; for k=order..2 ccap+=ag[k]*qHistory[k-2]; ccap+=ag[1]*q1; ccap+=ag[0]*q0) maps construct-for-construct onto niinteg.c:43-64 fall-through (high->low, ag[1]*state1 then ag[0]*state0 last); state2..state6->qHistory[0..4] via k-2; universal exit ni-integrate.ts:59-60 == niinteg.c:77-78. NEW Tier-2 MISMATCH (whole-function bijection, independently derived): two v41 error-guard constructs have NO digiTS counterpart. (1) TRAP `default: return(E_ORDER)` at ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/niinteg.c:36-39 has no counterpart in src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts: the `else` at :34-37 applies the order-2 formula for ANY order != 1 (e.g. order 3) instead of raising E_ORDER; v41 raises E_ORDER for TRAP order>=3. (2) GEAR `default: return(E_ORDER)` at niinteg.c:66-67 fires for order<1 AND order>6; ni-integrate.ts:42-43 guards only `order < 1`, so GEAR order>=7 falls through the loop (ag[7]*qHistory[5] = ag[7]*(undefined ?? 0)) with no E_ORDER. Per VERIFICATION.md sec2/sec4 a v41 construct with no digiTS counterpart is an automatic Tier-2 fail; this is NOT an architectural divergence (the applier already ported the analogous GEAR `order < 1` E_ORDER guard at ni-integrate.ts:42-43, proving these guards are in-file, in-scope portable work per TASK.md sec2). Remedy (within ni-integrate.ts): TRAP branch must raise E_ORDER for order != 1 && order != 2 (mirror niinteg.c:36-39: keep order===1 and order===2 explicit, throw E_ORDER otherwise); GEAR guard must raise E_ORDER for order < 1 OR order > 6 (mirror niinteg.c:66-67), not order<1 alone.

APPLIER ROUND 3 RESPONSE (attempt 2): Added the two missing E_ORDER guard constructs named by the round-3 verifier, both within src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts (in-file, in functionGroup scope; mirrors the existing GEAR order<1 guard the applier already ported in round 2). (1) TRAP: restructured the order branch from `if (order === 1) {...} else {...}` to `if (order === 1) {...} else if (order === 2) {...} else { throw E_ORDER }`. order===1 maps to niinteg.c:27-29 case 1; order===2 maps to niinteg.c:31-34 case 2 (the order-2 arithmetic -ccapPrev*ag[1]+ag[0]*(q0-q1) is byte-identical, merely moved from the bare `else` to `else if (order === 2)`, so it stays operand-for-operand identical to niinteg.c:32-34); the new trailing `else { throw new Error(...E_ORDER) }` maps construct-for-construct onto niinteg.c:36-39 `default: return(E_ORDER)` via the sanctioned C-error-return -> TS-throw surface form already used at the GEAR guard and the E_METHOD default. items.rationale: NInzIter is the noise-analysis adjoint-system solver; its sole callers in ngspice are noisesp.c:294 and noisean.c:412 (verified). Noise analysis is NOT implemented in digiTS (analysis-scope.md: no noise method on AnalogElement, no noise engine file, no noise MCP tool).

NIpzMuller is a pole-zero (PZ) Muller's-method root-finding routine operating on PZtrial structs. Pole-zero analysis is NOT implemented in digiTS (analysis-scope.md: no PZ method on AnalogEngine, no PZ engine file, no PZ MCP tool); grep confirms no PZ/NIpz/PZtrial counterpart anywhere in src.
NIpzSym2 is a pole-zero (PZ) symmetric-2 root-finder (PZtrial-typed). Pole-zero analysis NOT implemented (analysis-scope.md); no PZ counterpart in src.
Same NIpzSym2 pole-zero root-finder (the special-case b/b_mag fix). Pole-zero analysis NOT implemented (analysis-scope.md); no PZ counterpart in src.

Two independent positive-absence bases. (1) Subsystem absence: ft_interpolate has no behavioral existence in digiTS - its only ngspice consumers are the nutmeg/CP frontend (interp.c linearize, fourier.c, plotcurv.c/plotit.c, cmath4.c::cx_interpolate), none under spicelib/, and digiTS has no src/frontend, no linearize/fourier/plot command, and no polyfit/ft_interpolate/ft_peval symbol in src/. It is outside analysis-scope.md's DC-OP/transient/AC scope (frontend post-processing, not a solve). (2) PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: the hunk is bcopy(data,ydata,...)->memcpy(ydata,data,...) - a BSD->ISO stdlib idiom swap with identical copy semantics and zero behavioral/numerical change.

Two independent positive-absence bases. (1) Subsystem absence: ft_polyfit (least-squares poly fit via Gauss-Jordan) is the primitive beneath ft_interpolate; same frontend/CP-shell-only consumer set, no spicelib/ caller, no digiTS counterpart symbol, no CP shell - outside analysis-scope.md. (2) PLANNING.md §5 case (a) C-language artifact: the hunk replaces two inline 3-statement row/element swaps with the SWAP(type,a,b) macro (macros.h:38-43), which expands to the identical three statements - bit-identical, zero behavioral/numerical change.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts (no macro layer)
items.rationale: C-language artifact: removes the `#undef MALLOC` and `#undef REALLOC` preprocessor lines (memory-allocation macro plumbing for tmalloc/trealloc). digiTS is garbage-collected JS with no tmalloc/MALLOC/REALLOC macros; there is no behavioral content to port. Evidence: the hunk body is exclusively `#undef` directives for C memory macros.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts::_swapColumns / _spcRowExchange / _spcColExchange (inline swaps, no macro)
items.rationale: C-macro statement-safety artifact: rewrites the SWAP(type,a,b) macro from a brace-block `{...}` to a `do {...} while(0)` form so it is safe inside an unbraced `if`. digiTS has no SWAP macro - the swap operations it needs are written inline in _swapColumns (sparse-solver.ts:1167) and the SWAP sites in _spcRowExchange (2936-2940) / _spcColExchange (2994-2998). The macro hygiene rewrite has no behavioral content; the underlying swap semantics are already ported. Evidence: the hunk body is exclusively a C preprocessor `#define` reformatting.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts (no tmalloc/txfree/trealloc analogue)
items.rationale: C-language artifact: adds `const` qualifiers to the `txfree(const void *)` and `trealloc(const void *, size_t)` extern prototypes. digiTS is GC'd JS and ports neither tmalloc/txfree/trealloc nor the SP_MALLOC/SP_REALLOC macros that wrap them; there is no pointer-freeing API to const-qualify. Evidence: the hunk body is exclusively C `extern` prototype declarations for the heap allocator.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts (no HAS_WINGUI hook)
items.rationale: C/GUI artifact: defines the `INCRESP` macro under `#ifdef HAS_WINGUI` plus an `extern void SetAnalyse(...)` declaration - a Windows-GUI responsiveness hook that periodically calls SetAnalyse() to keep the GUI repainting during a long factorization. The remaining lines are include-ordering and blank-line whitespace shuffle. digiTS is a headless numeric layer with no Windows GUI, no SetAnalyse, and no progress-callback hook in the factor path. No behavioral content. Evidence: the entire add is gated on `#ifdef HAS_WINGUI` and the macro body only calls the GUI primitive SetAnalyse().

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/sparse-solver-instrumentation.ts (in-memory CSC read; no file dump)
items.rationale: C file-I/O debug artifact: changes spFileVector's fopen mode from append ("a") to write ("w") so a fresh debug dump overwrites rather than appends. spFileVector writes the RHS vector to a text file and is reached only via SMPprintRHS / SMPprint (spsmp.c:285,296) - a developer matrix-dump facility never on any solve path. digiTS is browser-deployed with no filesystem and ports no spFileVector/spFileMatrix/spPrint: its only white-box surface, sparse-solver-instrumentation.ts, reads the assembled matrix in memory as CSC and states it has no ngspice analogue (file header lines 19-23). No behavioral existence in digiTS. Evidence: the changed line is a literal fopen() call inside a File-write debug routine.

items.tsFunction: src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts::_initStructure (Size=0 path, sparse-solver.ts:1302; invoked once at ckt-context.ts:685)
items.rationale: The hunk adds an `int size` parameter to SMPnewMatrix and forwards it to spCreate(size,1,&Error) instead of the hard-coded spCreate(0,1,&Error). The new non-zero capability has a behavioral consumer only in sensitivity analysis: niinit.c:28 (the DC/TRAN/AC NR matrix init that digiTS implements) calls SMPnewMatrix(&CKTmatrix, 0) - identical to the v26 zero argument - while the sole non-zero caller is cktsens.c:145 (SMPnewMatrix(&delta_Y, size)). Sensitivity is NOT implemented per analysis-scope.md (no SENstruct/SENparms anywhere in src/; no sensitivity MCP tool). digiTS's _initStructure already reproduces the spCreate(Size=0) path digiTS uses (AllocatedSize = MAX(0,MINIMUM_ALLOCATED_SIZE), Size=0). The behavioral delta lives entirely in the non-implemented sensitivity analysis. Evidence: only non-zero call site is cktsens.c:145; the implemented-analysis call site niinit.c:28 passes 0.

src/solver/analog/sparse-solver.ts (no SMPconstMult/SMPmultiply analogue)
items.rationale: The hunk adds two new SMP-layer wrappers, SMPconstMult (-> spConstMult) and SMPmultiply (-> spMultiply). Their only callers in the entire ngspice tree are cktsens.c:426 (SMPconstMult(delta_Y,-1.0)) and cktsens.c:485 (SMPmultiply(delta_Y,...)) - the sensitivity analysis. Sensitivity is NOT implemented per analysis-scope.md (no SENstruct/SENparms in src/; no sensitivity engine method or MCP tool). Both functions exist solely to serve the non-implemented sensitivity analysis; digiTS has no spConstMult/spMultiply port and no caller that would need one. Evidence: the only two call sites of SMPconstMult/SMPmultiply are both in cktsens.c (sensitivity).

ngspiceBaseline: src/spicelib/analysis/tfanal.c (TFanal: small-signal transfer ratio + input/output resistance from the linearized DC system)
items.title: Build the .tf small-signal transfer-function analysis as a thin driver over the existing linearized DC solve: after the DC operating point, compute the small-signal transfer ratio d(output)/d(input source) plus the input resistance (at the input source) and output resistance (at the output port) from the factored DC Jacobian. New IN-scope analysis per #57 (analysis-scope.md amended 2026-05-29); no v26 baseline in digiTS (absent today). Reconstruct the driver so the v41 tfanal.c hunks apply as ordinary per-hunk deltas.
items.tsFunction: a new .tf driver over the factored DC Jacobian (transfer ratio + Zin/Zout) on the AnalogEngine, plus the facade/MCP surface (circuit_tf) to expose it
verifierNotes: APPLIED. runTransferFunction driver (dc-operating-point.ts) is line-isomorphic to ngspice tfanal.c:73-157 (re-derived independently from ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/analysis/tfanal.c): zero RHS over [0..size] (tfanal.c:74-76); input injection isource nodePos-=1/nodeNeg+=1 (tfanal.c:79-80) vs vsource branch+=1 (tfanal.c:83); re-solve + rhs[0]=0 (tfanal.c:87-88); transfer ratio node Pos-Neg / branch (tfanal.c:113-117); input R isource Neg-Pos / vsource -1/i with 1e-20|1e20 clamp (tfanal.c:122-129); same-source shortcut (tfanal.c:132-139); second zero+output injection (tfanal.c:140-149); second re-solve + rhs[0]=0 (tfanal.c:150-151); output R node Neg-Pos / branch 1/MAX(1e-20,...) (tfanal.c:153-156). R-1 reSolveFactored primitive (analog-engine.ts) maps SMPsolve(M,CKTrhs,CKTrhsSpare); rhs/rhsSpare length matrixSize+1 (ckt-context.ts:874-875) so inclusive i<=size indexing is safe. R-3 label->branch via _deviceMap (the findDevice backing map, analog-engine.ts:1646) + inEl.branchIndex (element.ts:65). Engine/coordinator/null-coordinator/facade/MCP surfaces (Parts D/E) declared+implemented per spec. File-scope extra src/core/__tests__/analog-engine-interface.test.ts is a compile-forced AnalogEngine-stub conformance edit (adds transferFunction to satisfy the new interface member), minimal, no logic. Harness regression gate after server_restart: firstDivergence null on ALL classes for resistive-divider.dts (DC/tran), rc-transient.dts (tran), inductor-rl-step.dts (tran), rc-lowpass-ac.dts (AC). Verified against tfanal.c:73-157.

items.title: Seed the previous-solution buffer at IC/nodeset setup: port CKTic's CKTrhsOld[n]=CKTrhs[n]=value dual-write (cktic.c:31,39) so iteration 0 of a .ic/.nodeset circuit starts from the constrained voltage. Complementary to the per-iteration maths-sparse#recon/nodesetIcRowZero matrix stamp; FIX-002 (compiler propagation + ctx.ics/nodesets seeded before _allocateNodesetIcHandles) is the shared precondition that is already landed. digiTS today reaches the matrix (matrix axis null) but never seeds rhsOld, so iter-0 voltage/rhs/state diverge.
items.tsFunction: the CKTic-counterpart IC/nodeset setup in MNAEngine: after the RHS buffers are zeroed and before the first NR solve, write each ctx.nodesets / ctx.ics value into BOTH rhs[node] and rhsOld[node] via a direct rhsOld[node]=rhs[node]=value assignment (not stampRHS's +=)
verifierNotes: APPLIED 2026-06-02 (kind-1, recorded from cktic implementer commit df50b69f and its separate-context verifier gate). The CKTic rhsOld dual-write seed is built in src/solver/analog/analog-engine.ts: at IC/nodeset setup, before the first NR solve, rhsOld[node]=rhs[node]=value is written for each (node,value) in ctx.nodesets and ctx.ics (direct assignment mirroring cktic.c:31,39 '='), gated as ngspice gates the nodeset/IC branches. The commit's ic-gate harness gate reports firstDivergence full-null, confirming iteration-0 of a .ic/.nodeset circuit starts from the constrained voltage bit-exact vs the ngspice DLL. Complements FIX-002 / maths-sparse#recon/nodesetIcRowZero (the per-iteration cktLoad stamp). No-IC circuits byte-identical (empty seed loop).

ngspiceBaseline: src/spicelib/analysis/optran.c (OPtran: pseudo-transient 0..opfinaltime reusing the transient kernel, no output capture)
src/spicelib/analysis/cktop.c (CKTop OPtran call site + outcome mapping, :101-108)
items.title: Port ngspice OPtran, the operating-point pseudo-transient last-resort convergence fallback (optran.c), plus its CKTop call site (cktop.c:101-108): an opt-in (nooptran default true) fallback invoked after direct + gmin + source stepping that reuses the existing transient kernel. Default-off => DC-OP byte-identical to today. The cosmetic/gmin-restructure portions of cktop.c#h001 are Decision-1 OUT; only the behavioral OPtran integration is reconstructed here.
items.tsFunction: an OPtran fallback in solveDcOperatingPoint after source stepping (gated on the optran option) reusing MNAEngine's transient stepping; plus the optran/opstepsize/opfinaltime/opramptime option surface
verifierNotes: Port COMPLETE (commit 248f5c75): OPtran pseudo-transient driver + opstepsize/opfinaltime/opramptime option plumbing (default-off) + cktop.c:101-108 post-source-step fall-through + harness optran NgspiceJobAnalysis variant. Acceptance #1-#3 PASS (default-off byte-identical; surfaces 1+2 green). Recorded ESCALATED not APPLIED because acceptance #4 is RED - see escalation.
items.escalation: FIX-006 / ESC-025: acceptance #4 (bit-exact OPtran convergence parity on the inductor-singular fixture v1 1 0 dc 3 / v2 2 0 dc 5 / l1 1 2 1m) is BLOCKED. digiTS dynamic_gmin spuriously converges the l1 branch-current singularity to a nonphysical ~2e12 A (gmin^-1 scale) solution that passes the voltage-based NIconvTest, so solveDcOperatingPoint reports the static ladder succeeded and never reaches the OPtran fallback - whereas the ngspice DLL fails gmin+source on the branch-current singularity and falls to OPtran (physical ~2mA). Same root as the standing elemConverged ours=true/ngspice=false divergence. Gate #4 cannot pass until FIX-006 (digiTS convergence test must reject gmin-leakage-scale branch currents). The OPtran port itself is correct and simply unreachable on this fixture until then. See ESCALATIONS.md ESC-025 and FIX-006.

items.title: Build the complete v26-baseline ASRC arbitrary/behavioural source (the B-element: V=expr / I=expr) as a new digiTS analog element. Reconstructs the parse-tree-driven controlled source covering all four controlled-source topologies (VCVS/VCCS/CCVS/CCCS) selected by the controlling-variable types, with per-iteration parse-tree evaluation returning rhs + partial derivatives (built on src/solver/analog/expression.ts), the linearized Jacobian/RHS companion stamp, branch-equation allocation for the voltage form, temperature coefficients (tc1/tc2/reciproctc), small-signal (ASRCacValues) precompute, and the convergence test. Faithful to the v26 baseline; the v41 deltas (m/reciprocm multiplier, CKTsrcFact MODETRANOP ramp, ASRCvars[] index cache, TREALLOC scratch, restructured TSTALLOC walk) then apply as the blocked PENDING hunks. Component shape (single B / BV+BI split, modelRegistry) is OQ-1.
items.tsFunction: BSourceAnalogElement (setup/load/stampAc/computeTemperature/setParam/getPinCurrents) built on src/solver/analog/expression.ts (evaluate + differentiate as the IFparseTree/IFeval counterpart) + src/components/register-all.ts registration

items.title: Rebuild CAPacLoad as AnalogCapacitorElement.stampAc (jωC AC small-signal stamp)
items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/capacitor.ts — AnalogCapacitorElement.stampAc (no allocation in stampAc; reuses the four _handles[0..3] returned by allocNortonStamp in setup(), mirroring ngspice's CAPsetup/CAPacLoad function boundary where CAPsetup TSTALLOCs once at capsetup.c:114-117 and CAPacLoad performs pure imaginary-half stamps on the same pointers)
verifierNotes: REOPENED — prior MATCH retracted by user-directed audit against ngspice CAPacLoad as the canonical reference (not inductor.ts). Two structural divergences remain at capacitor.ts:458-482 vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/cap/capacld.c:16-40: (1) Function-boundary divergence (Tier-2 bijective): ngspice splits the work between CAPsetup (capsetup.c:114-117 TSTALLOC x4 — allocates the four (posPos, negNeg, posNeg, negPos) matrix cells exactly once) and CAPacLoad (capacld.c:16-40 — zero allocation, pure stamp on the four pre-allocated double* matrix pointers via *(...Ptr+1) ±= m*val). digiTS currently allocates the cells in setup() via allocNortonStamp (returning _handles[0..3]) AND has a 6-line `if (this._hAcPosPos === -1) { ...4x allocElement... }` lazy-alloc block in stampAc (capacitor.ts:469-474) that has no counterpart in CAPacLoad. VERIFICATION.md sec2 Tier-2: a digiTS construct with no v41 counterpart is MISMATCH. (2) Insertion-order divergence (harness instrumentation): sparse-solver.ts:472-474 pushes one _insertionOrder entry per allocElement call regardless of whether the (row,col) already exists; the lazy-alloc block produces 4 extra entries (8 total cap entries vs ngspice's 4), which harness_topology_diff would see as a structural divergence in the per-element-pool order. The matrix shape itself matches ngspice (allocElement on duplicate row,col returns the same handle via the diag fast-path at :451-454 or _spcFindElementInCol find-or-create at :456), so the AC harness paired tests do not fail — but the function-boundary and insertion-order both differ. Required fix: drop the four _hAcPosPos/_hAcNegNeg/_hAcPosNeg/_hAcNegPos fields and the lazy-alloc block; stamp through the existing _handles[0..3] tuple already returned by allocNortonStamp in setup(). This restores ngspice's one-allocation-in-setup, pure-stamp-in-CAPacLoad shape line-for-line. The cap-stampac.md spec needs the parallel update.

Tier1+Tier2 MATCH vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/cap/capacld.c:16-40 and ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/cap/capsetup.c:114-117. Re-derived ngspice->digiTS correspondence independently (rename-maps/cap.md NOT consulted). Function-boundary now matches line-for-line: cells allocated exactly once in setup() (capacitor.ts:377) via allocNortonStamp(ctx.solver, posNode, negNode) (stamp-helpers.ts:40-50), which issues allocElement(posNode,posNode) / allocElement(negNode,negNode) / allocElement(posNode,negNode) / allocElement(negNode,posNode) in the capsetup.c:114-117 TSTALLOC order and returns [hPP, hNN, hPN, hNP]. stampAc (capacitor.ts:462-478) performs zero allocation, destructures _handles, and stamps the imaginary half via solver.stampElementImag (sparse-solver.ts:670-672 writes _elValImag[handle]), which is the +1 imaginary pointer pair to the real half _elVal[handle] written by load(). Acceptance criterion 5 satisfied: Grep for _hAcPosPos|_hAcNegNeg|_hAcPosNeg|_hAcNegPos returns zero matches; stampAc body contains no allocElement call. Tier-2 bijective check: capacld.c:28 m=CAPm <-> capacitor.ts:466 const m=this._M; capacld.c:30 val=CKTomega*CAPcapac <-> capacitor.ts:464 const val=omega*this.C (operands/operator/order identical); capacld.c:32-35 *(...Ptr+1) ±= m*val <-> capacitor.ts:474-477 four solver.stampElementImag(_handles[i], ±m*val) calls with posPos->_handles[0], negNeg->_handles[1], posNeg->_handles[2], negPos->_handles[3] mapping bijectively to TSTALLOC order. Model/instance linked-list walk -> per-element invocation under defaultStampAcHandler (default-loaders.ts:61-68) is the VERIFICATION.md sec4 sanctioned iteration plumbing collapse. Cross-check: inductor.ts:491-505 was updated in parallel to the same shape and mirrors INDsetup/INDacLoad's CAPsetup/CAPacLoad-isomorphic function boundary — supporting evidence that the canonical pattern is correctly applied to both passives.

items.title: Add CKTepsmin floor + apply it to diode/vdmos setup
items.tsFunction: CKTCircuitContext epsmin field + diode/vdmos setup consumers
verifierNotes: MATCH (reconstruction). VERIFICATION.md sec1a both legs satisfied. SPEC-PRESENCE GATE: spec/v41-port/reconstruction/engine-epsmin.md present on disk, RATIFIED 2026-05-30. (a) Source isomorphism re-derived INDEPENDENTLY against fresh ref/ngspice (rename-maps NOT consulted). Citations verified by hand: cktdefs.h:323 declares "double CKTepsmin; /* minimum argument value for some log functions, e.g. diode saturation current*/"; cktinit.c:94 "sckt->CKTepsmin = 1e-28;"; diosetup.c:92-103 knee-current disable gated on model->DIOforwardKneeCurrentGiven / model->DIOreverseKneeCurrentGiven each testing "< ckt->CKTepsmin" and clearing the *Given flag; diosetup.c:189-191 "if (model->DIOsatCur < ckt->CKTepsmin) model->DIOsatCur = ckt->CKTepsmin;"; vdmosset.c:250-252 "if (model->VDIOjctSatCur < ckt->CKTepsmin) model->VDIOjctSatCur = ckt->CKTepsmin;". PART A (engine field): ckt-context.ts adds cktEpsmin:number=1e-28 adjacent to cktIndVerbosity, cited cktdefs.h:323 + cktinit.c:94; constructor + refreshTolerances override cktEpsmin from params.epsmin when defined (mirrors the cktIndVerbosity hot-reload pattern, cited cktdojob.c:110 CKTepsmin=task->TSKepsmin). analog-engine-interface.ts adds SimulationParams.epsmin?:number + DEFAULT_SIMULATION_PARAMS.epsmin=1e-28. PART B (SetupContext surface): setup-context.ts adds readonly epsmin:number after nomTemp; analog-engine.ts _buildSetupContext populates epsmin: this._ctx?.cktEpsmin ?? (params.epsmin ?? 1e-28), the same wiring shape as temp/nomTemp (the spec-sanctioned fallback when the context is not reachable). PART C (diode setup, diode.ts): setup() floors params.IS to ctx.epsmin when params.IS < ctx.epsmin and re-runs applyDioTempResult(dioTemp(...)) so tIS (DIOtSatCur) derives from the floored value, matching diosetup.c:190-191 applied before DIOtemp. Knee disable forces IKF/IKR to Infinity when isFinite(params.IK*) && params.IK* < ctx.epsmin. Verified the diode load() knee gates at diode.ts:772 / diode.ts:786 use isFinite(params.IKF) / isFinite(params.IKR), so forcing Infinity skips the high-injection Norton arm = the structural analogue of ngspice clearing DIOforwardKneeCurrentGiven / DIOreverseKneeCurrentGiven. The leading isFinite guard leaves an ungiven (default-Infinity) knee untouched, disables only a given finite sub-floor knee. PART D (vdmos setup, vdmos.ts): setup() floors p.IS to ctx.epsmin when p.IS < ctx.epsmin. Verified _tSatCur is computed in computeTemperature() at vdmos.ts:709 (reading p.IS) which runs AFTER setup(), so the floor precedes the temperature pass that consumes it = VDMOSsetup-before-VDMOStemp order; the spec re-run clause is not needed because computeTemperature runs post-setup. VDMOS has no IKF/IKR; only the body-diode saturation current is floored. The 1e-28 constant appears ONLY at the cktEpsmin field default + DEFAULT_SIMULATION_PARAMS; every device floor reads ctx.epsmin, no device site inlines 1e-28. Bijective over reconstructed scope. (b) HARNESS GATE: server_restart -> harness_start(dllPath ref/ngspice/visualc/sharedspice/Release.x64/ngspice.dll) on src/solver/analog/__tests__/ngspice-parity/fixtures/resistive-divider.dts (the contract gate fixture) -> harness_run -> harness_first_divergence: firstDivergence NULL across all 8 classes (voltage/rhs/matrix/state/integration/limiting/convergence/shape), earliest null; 107/107 tran steps both engines, 0 failed, avgIterations 2.028 both, maxIterations 5 both, stepCount delta 0. Bit-exact. FILE-SCOPE: git diff --name-only shows the 6 recon tsFiles (ckt-context.ts, setup-context.ts, analog-engine.ts, analog-engine-interface.ts, diode.ts, vdmos.ts) plus bjt.ts/mosfet.ts which carry pre-existing unrelated AC stampAc sibling work (present at session start per the gitStatus snapshot; verified zero epsmin/CKTepsmin content in those diffs) -- NOT over-application by this recon, which touched only its 6 tsFiles. Commit scoped to the 6 recon tsFiles + progress/ledger.

items.title: Build the CKTindverbosity-gated inductive-system Cholesky positive-definite verification subsystem (INDsystem linked-list bookkeeping, cholesky() helper, missing/duplicate K diagnostics) — v41-new with no v26 baseline
items.tsFunction: src/components/passives/mutual-inductor.ts — add the inductive-system Cholesky positive-definite verification subsystem invoked from MutualInductorElement.computeTemperature when the engine-side verbosity flag is set. Components: (a) a `cktIndVerbosity` integer config carried on TempContext (engine plumbing the digiTS counterpart of ngspice's `CKTindverbosity`); (b) INDsystem-shaped linked-list bookkeeping on AnalogInductorElement (`system`, `systemNextInd`, `systemIdx`) and MutualInductorElement (`systemNextMut`) — the digiTS counterpart of the v41 inddefs.h fields; (c) a module-private `cholesky(matrix, n)` helper performing in-place Cholesky factorisation, the counterpart of ngspice's file-static cholesky(); (d) verification driver in computeTemperature that traverses the inductive-system graph, assembles each system's mutual-inductance matrix, calls cholesky(), and emits stderr diagnostics for non-positive-definite systems / duplicate K instances / incomplete K coupling sets / negative inductances / |K|>1. The v41 baseline is itself v41-new (no v26 functionality to reconstruct against); the reconstruction targets the v41 state directly per PLANNING.md §5b intent (subsystem digiTS lacks entirely). Once APPLIED, the v41 hunks in `blocks` apply as ordinary per-stamp deltas onto the rebuilt subsystem.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Reconstruction item (v41-new, no v26 baseline). Tier-1+Tier-2 re-derived independently from fresh v41 reads of ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/ind/{muttemp.c,inddefs.h,indsetup.c}, ref/ngspice/src/include/ngspice/cktdefs.h, ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/cktinit.c, ref/ngspice/src/frontend/com_option.c; rename-maps NOT consumed. Every cited line verified by hand: cktdefs.h:111 'int CKTindverbosity;', cktinit.c:65 'sckt->CKTindverbosity = 2;', com_option.c:50 'indverbosity = %d', indsetup.c:103-104 'here->system = NULL; here->system_next_ind = NULL;' positioned after the CKTmkCur branch (97-101) and before the TSTALLOC block (112-116). CHOLESKY (mutual-inductor.ts:66-83 vs muttemp.c:13-32) is construct-bijective: outer for i<n, inner for j<=i, Summe=A(i,j) init, inner dot-product for k<j with Summe -= A(i,k)*A(j,k) (operand order preserved), i>j -> A(i,j)=Summe/A(j,j), else if Summe>0 -> A(i,i)=sqrt(Summe), else return false, trailing return true. Flat row-major a[n*r+c] (no 2D array, no n*i/n*j hoist, explicit inner loop not reduce, Math.sqrt not **0.5, early return not flag-and-continue). MERGE-CHAIN (ind-family-temperature.ts:132-205 vs muttemp.c:45-118): 5-way if/else-if chain in exact v41 order -- (!s1&&!s2) fresh system size=2 (62-73); (s1&&!s2) ind2 joins, size++ (74-81); (!s1&&s2) ind1 joins mirror (82-89); (s1===s2) thread mut only (90-93); else merge s1+=s2.size, s2.size=0, IND-chain tail-walk with break + splice (102-109), MUT-chain tail-walk with break + splice + prepend here (110-116). Each branch's assignment order matches line-for-line. MATRIX ASSEMBLY (208-302 vs 121-205): if(firstSystem) gate, szMax scan, pop=Uint8Array+INDmatrix=Float64Array, per-system !size continue, pop.fill/INDmatrix.fill, diagonal loop INDmatrix[i*sz+i]=_effectiveLForVerify + system_idx=i++ (143-147), off-diagonal loop with SWAP-when-j<k (155-156), pop/repetitions/expect bookkeeping (157-162), chained INDmatrix[j*sz+k]=INDmatrix[k*sz+j]=MUTfactor (163). WAIVER (276-292 vs 168-181): positive=cholesky; if(!positive){positive=true; MUT-walk |K|!=1 break; IND-walk L<0 break}. EMIT GATE (296 vs 183): !positive||repetitions||(expect&&verbosity>1). Diagnostic text verbatim per condition (muttemp.c:184-203) routed through ctx.diagnostics.emit with involvedElements in IND-then-MUT traversal order. Two separate MUT-walk/verify-walk loops and two separate diagonal/off-diagonal loops -- no loop fusion. No forbidden shapes (no operand reorder, factor-out, statement merge/split, algebraic simplification, branch/loop-nesting change). Parts A-D all present: ckt-context.ts cktIndVerbosity=2 + tempCtx populate + resetTempCtx + ctor read of params.indVerbosity + refreshTolerances re-read+resetTempCtx; temp-context.ts _indVerbosity+diagnostics; SimulationParams.indVerbosity default 2 + DEFAULT_SIMULATION_PARAMS:2; AnalogInductorElement _system/_systemNextInd/_systemIdx (null/null/-1) + setup() init at the v41 position + package-private accessors; MutualInductorElement _systemNextMut + accessors + IndSystem interface; three DiagnosticCode union entries. Pass ordering (ind-family-temperature.ts:53-82): Pass1 IND.computeTemperature, Pass2 MUT.computeTemperature, Pass3 verify gated on _indVerbosity>0. Decision (ii) re-run-on-setCircuitTemp confirmed: analog-engine.ts setCircuitTemp (1383-1391) and configure({indVerbosity}) (1256-1259) both re-run cktTemp() -> IND family temp handler Pass 3, not cached. SEC1a HARNESS GATE (ngspice.dll Release.x64, instrumented build with ni_ac_register; the default visualc-shared/spice.dll is stale and yields a vacuous ngspice-side 0-step run -- this is also the root cause of the in-tree inductor/diode/mosfet parity test failures): firstDivergence NULL across all 8 classes (voltage/rhs/matrix/state/integration/limiting/convergence/shape, earliest null) on transformer-canon-rl-pulse (coupled-inductor+MUT, 112/112 bit-exact), tapped-transformer-canon-dc-grounded-ct (3-IND/3-MUT system exercising the merge chain, 107/107), inductor-canon-rl-step (107/107), rlc-oscillator (111/111). Verify subsystem does not perturb the solve. DEVIATION (a) pass-entry reset loop (ind-family-temperature.ts:116-123, re-nulls _systemPtr/_systemNextIndPtr/_systemIdxPtr/_systemNextMutPtr): no direct v41 line in MUTtemp, but a faithful reproduction of the clean INDsetup state (indsetup.c:103-104 + field defaults) that v41's single-MUTtemp-per-setup flow assumes at each pass entry. v41 tfrees the INDsystem structs (muttemp.c:210-214) WITHOUT re-nulling ind->system (latent use-after-free across a temp sweep with no intervening INDsetup). digiTS re-runs the verify per Decision (ii) without re-running setup(), and GC keeps the prior pass's IndSystem live on the instance fields, so without the reset the merge chain reads stale systems. This is the CLAUDE.md fold-in-latent-bugs requirement; harness gate confirms no solve perturbation -> MATCH-with-note, acceptable. DEVIATION (b) cholesky/verifyInductiveSystems exported instead of module-private: visibility-only (VERIFICATION.md sec4 surface-form), mandated by the CLAUDE.md three-surface rule which requires Surface-1 headless reachability without forbidden private-field tunnelling / SetupContext hand-building; no arithmetic/structural impact -> acceptable allowed-difference. Three-surface tests all present and the two unit files pass (7/7): Surface 1 ind-inductive-system-verify.test.ts (criteria 10-13 + waiver + engine gate via DefaultSimulatorFacade.configure, 6 tests), Surface 2 circuit-dc-op-indverbosity.test.ts (MCP circuit_dc_op indVerbosity forwarding, 1 test); Surface 3 ind-verbosity-configure.spec.ts (Playwright sim-configure->sim-configured echo) verified by source inspection of postmessage-adapter _handleConfigure + SimulatorHarness postToSim/waitForMessage/loadDigXml (Playwright not run here). PRE-EXISTING FAILURES confirmed unrelated: 8 inductor.test.ts + 6 diode/mosfet temp-sweep failures all share the identical comparison-session-asserts.ts signature (ngspice=NaN / 'shape step 0/iter -1 step_shape[0] ngspice=0') caused by the in-tree comparison-session.ts/netlist-generator.ts harness migration (modified in working tree) defaulting to the stale spice.dll; diode/mosfet have no inductors and never invoke Pass 3, so the verify subsystem cannot be the cause. All 14 acceptance criteria satisfied.

items.title: Build the complete Parker-Skellern (PS) JFET2 short-channel field-effect transistor device, faithful to the v26 ngspice baseline: the full PS-model param set (psmodel.c/.h), drain-current + trapping (vtrap/vgstrap) + gate-charge model, depletion-cap temperature corrections, prime-node series resistances (rd/rs), DC/transient load, AC small-signal load, temperature pass, LTE/trunc, instance/model param setters, and .ic/UIC initial conditions. JFET2 has a v26 baseline (PS model, A.E. Parker 1994) but was never ported to digiTS; reconstruct to v26 so the v41 diff hunks apply as ordinary per-hunk deltas. Component shape Jfet2N/Jfet2P per OQ-JFET2-SHAPE.

MATCH (reconstruction). VERIFICATION.md sec1a both legs satisfied. SPEC-PRESENCE GATE: spec/v41-port/reconstruction/maths-misc-randnumb.md present in main checkout (RATIFIED 2026-05-30, RNG-1=SEED-INJECTION). (a) Source isomorphism re-derived INDEPENDENTLY (rename-maps NOT consulted) against fresh ref/ngspice/src/maths/misc/randnumb.c. tsFile src/solver/analog/monte-carlo.ts. SeededRng holds eight uint32 state words _s1.._s8 mapped 1:1 to CombState1..8 (randnumb.c:54-59); Park-Miller _state removed. tauS (monte-carlo.ts:308-311) bijective with TauS (randnumb.c:125-129): b=(((state<<c1)^state)>>>c2), return (((state&m)<<c3)^b), every shift/xor/mask forced uint32 via >>>0 (C logical >> on unsigned matches JS >>>). lgcs (:316-318)=LGCS (:131-134) A1*state+A2 via Math.imul(a1,state)+a2 >>>0 (low-32 wrap of C unsigned multiply). combLCGTaus (:323-330)=randnumb.c:140-148: tauS(_s1,13,19,12,0xFFFFFFFE)^tauS(_s2,2,25,4,0xFFFFFFF8)^tauS(_s3,3,11,17,0xFFFFFFF0)^lgcs(_s4,1664525,1013904223), scaled 2.3283064365387e-10 with >>>0 before the double multiply. combLCGTausInt (:334-340)=randnumb.c:154-162 over _s5.._s8, no scaling. drand (:343-345)=2*x-1 (randnumb.c:95-98); next()/uniform() re-rooted onto combLCGTaus/drand. gauss0 (:356-372) bijective with randnumb.c:195-215: polar loop r=v1*v1+v2*v2 while r>=1, fac=sqrt(-2*log(r)/r), glgset=v1*fac, gliset=false, return v2*fac; off-call returns cached _glgset (latch is instance state). gauss1 (:376-385)=randnumb.c:220-231; rgauss (:389-398)=randnumb.c:240-254; poisson (:413-427)=randnumb.c:260-274 (P=exp(-lambda), P*=lambda/k, cap max_k=1000); exprand (:430-432)=randnumb.c:278-283 (-log(u)*mean). setState (:285-303) injects CombState1..8 via >>>0 per RNG-1=SEED-INJECTION (Acceptance #6/#9). Cosine-form Box-Muller deleted. Frontend hunks h001-h003 (checkseed/com_sseed/cp_getvar shell) correctly NOT ported (NO-COUNTERPART); CombLCGTaus2/CombLCGTausInt2 inlined test-duplicates correctly NOT ported (arithmetically identical). Bijective over reconstructed scope. (b) GATE: no ngspice divergence circuit applies (RNG bit stream is not a solver matrix); the test IS the gate per contract. Ran node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/solver/analog/__tests__/monte-carlo.test.ts -t SeededRng in the worktree: 10 passed, 0 failed, 13 skipped. Covers seeded-stream reproducibility, setState bit-identity (combLCGTaus + combLCGTausInt 200 draws each across two streams), gauss0 cached-second-value latch reconstructed from raw uniforms (first==v2*fac, second==v1*fac), polar Box-Muller moments (|mean|<0.1, 0.9<std<1.1), drand [-1,1), combLCGTausInt uint32, exprand reproducible/non-negative, poisson integer in [0,1000]. FILE-SCOPE: git diff --name-only = monte-carlo.ts + monte-carlo.test.ts (recon tsFiles) + .vitest-failures.json (test-runner artifact, not committed). No over-application.

items.title: Reconstruct ngspice's nodeset/.ic matrix-stamping mechanism (ZeroNoncurRow) so .nodeset/.ic circuits are bit-exact per-iteration to ngspice. ngspice (cktload.c:167-186 ZeroNoncurRow + the apply at cktload.c:105-158) ZEROES the non-current row and stamps an exact 1*v=value for a pure-voltage nodeset/ic node (the currents=0 branch), using the 1e10 soft-pin only in the currents=1 branch. digiTS currently always uses a 1e10 large-conductance pin (ckt-load.ts:30,121-150) with no row-zeroing, producing different matrix entries at every applied iteration on .nodeset/.ic circuits (#68: a real per-iteration parity divergence, not an accepted one — this is the stamping mechanism, not matrix ordering/pivoting, so the 'Sparse Solver is Settled' directive does not shield it). Build the read-only column lookup + the ZeroNoncurRow row-zero/exact-constraint path into the nodeset/IC stamping; after it, spsmp.c#h002's SMPfindElt Col==-1 guard applies as an ordinary delta.
items.tsFunction: ckt-load.ts nodeset/IC application (replace the unconditional CKTNS_PIN=1e10 stamp with ngspice's ZeroNoncurRow: zero the non-current row + exact 1*v=value for pure-voltage rows, 1e10 soft-pin only in the currents=1 case) + sparse-solver.ts::_spcFindElementInCol (the read-only column lookup the row-zero walk requires)
verifierNotes: MISMATCH (recon not applied; left PENDING). VERIFICATION.md sec1a requires for APPLIED both (a) source isomorphic to the spec's ngspice baseline and (b) a clean harness gate. Neither can be evaluated because the recon was never built. (1) SPEC ABSENT: spec/v41-port/reconstruction/maths-sparse-nodeset-ic-rowzero.md (the `spec` field of this recon in planning/maths-sparse-decisions.json:98) does not exist on disk -- the reconstruction/ directory holds 9 files (cap-computetemperature, cap-stampac, ind-modelParams, jfet-initialConditions, jfet-tempModelParams, mut-inductiveSystemVerify, res-computeconductance, vccs-multiplierAndIc, vdmos-wholeClass) and none is the nodeset-ic-rowzero spec. With no spec there is no authored ngspice baseline to re-derive isomorphism against. (2) NO SOURCE CHANGE: `git diff --name-only` on branch v41-port does NOT include src/solver/analog/ckt-load.ts (the recon's sole tsFile); the only diffed paths are orchestration/env state (.claude/worktrees/*, .omc/state/mission-state.json, ref/ngspice submodule pointer, spec/v41-port/{ledger.json,ledger.md,progress.json}). ckt-load.ts is unmodified and still applies the unconditional 1e10 soft-pin for BOTH nodeset (ckt-load.ts:130-135: stampElement(nodesetHandles.get(node), CKTNS_PIN=1e10) + stampRHS(node, 1e10*value*srcFact)) and IC (ckt-load.ts:145-150: same shape via icHandles) with NO row-zeroing pass. This is exactly the pre-recon state the item targets to replace (decisions.json title: ngspice cktload.c:167-186 ZeroNoncurRow zeroes the non-current row and stamps exact 1*v=value for pure-voltage nodeset/ic rows, 1e10 only in the currents=1 branch; #68 per-iteration parity divergence). The ZeroNoncurRow row-zero/exact-constraint path and the read-only column lookup (_spcFindElementInCol consumer) named in the recon are absent. (3) blocks-target spsmp.c#h002 (SMPfindElt Col==-1 guard) remains PENDING in decisions.json, consistent with the recon being unapplied. HARNESS GATE not run: it is moot -- the sec1a gate is necessary ON TOP OF source isomorphism, which already fails (no spec, no edit), so APPLIED is impossible regardless of harness output; and running it against the unconditional-1e10 baseline would, per the recon's own #68 description, surface a real per-iteration nodeset/IC matrix divergence that is itself non-waveable (banned closing verdicts: settled-solver/pre-existing/tolerance). FILE-SCOPE CHECK: `git diff --name-only` contains no tsFile of this recon (ckt-load.ts unmodified) and no out-of-scope ts source file -- no over-application, but also no application. Remedy for the applier: author spec/v41-port/reconstruction/maths-sparse-nodeset-ic-rowzero.md from the ngspiceBaseline (cktload.c:105-158 + ZeroNoncurRow :167-186 + spsmp.c SMPfindElt read-only lookup), then implement in ckt-load.ts: replace the unconditional CKTNS_PIN stamp with ngspice's ZeroNoncurRow (zero the non-current row + exact 1*v=value for pure-voltage rows; 1e10 soft-pin only in the currents=1 case) plus the _spcFindElementInCol read-only column lookup the row-zero walk requires. Until built, this stays PENDING.
