items.title: Build the SW (voltage-controlled switch) AC small-signal load — propagate the saved switch state and stamp the on/off conductance into the AC matrix — as a stampAc method on the digiTS switch element classes
items.tsFunction: src/components/active/analog-switch.ts — add a stampAc method to AnalogSwitchSPSTElement and AnalogSwitchSPDTElement (the optional AnalogElement.stampAc hook, element.ts:123-129). Mirror SWacLoad (swacload.c): read current_state = CKTstate0[SWswitchstate] (the state saved during the DC-OP/transient load via this._pool.states[0][base + SLOT_STATE]); pick g_now = current_state ? SWonConduct : SWoffConduct (1/Ron : 1/Roff); stamp the four real-conductance cells (+g_now at posPos, -g_now at posNeg/negPos, +g_now at negNeg) via the AC solver's real-part stamps using the same matrix handles allocated in setup(). The SPDT element stamps both complementary paths. SW conductance is purely resistive, so there is no imaginary-part contribution. AC is implemented engine-wide; the SW element simply never declared stampAc, so it is invisible to AC. This is the v26 SWacLoad behavior; the v41 hunks (accessor/Ptr renames, SWstate→SWswitchstate macro) apply onto it as ordinary deltas once APPLIED.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Reconstruction item. VERIFICATION.md sec1a satisfied on both legs: (a) source isomorphic to the ngspice baseline (re-derived independently from fresh reads of ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/sw/{swacload.c,swdefs.h}; rename-maps NOT consulted). The named spec spec/v41-port/reconstruction/sw-acLoad.md does NOT exist (ledger item records specExists:false); the ledger item itself supplies ngspiceBaseline=[swacload.c,swdefs.h] and the tsFunction prescription (add a stampAc method mirroring SWacLoad: read current_state=CKTstate0[SWswitchstate], pick g_now=current_state?SWonConduct:SWoffConduct, stamp four real-conductance cells), exactly as the APPLIED sibling cccs#recon/multiplier (also specExists:false) was handled. AND (b) harness gate clean. ngspice->digiTS correspondence independently re-derived: SWswitchstate (=SWstate+0, swdefs.h:60) <-> SLOT_STATE=0 read via this._pool.states[0][base+SLOT_STATE]; SWonConduct/SWoffConduct (swdefs.h:77-78) <-> 1/rOnNow : 1/rOffNow; SWposPosPtr/SWposNegPtr/SWnegPosPtr/SWnegNegPtr (swdefs.h:35-42) <-> the four setup() handles _hPP/_hPN/_hNP/_hNN. SPST stampAc (analog-switch.ts:327-345): current_state=(states[0][base+SLOT_STATE])|0 -- the |0 reproduces swacload.c:26 (int) truncation; g_now=current_state?1/rOnNow:1/rOffNow (line 339) is bijective with swacload.c:28 g_now=current_state?SWonConduct:SWoffConduct (truthiness, any non-zero state -> on, exactly the C ternary -- NOT the REALLY_ON/HYST_ON test); four stamps +g_now/-g_now/-g_now/+g_now at posPos/posNeg/negPos/negNeg (lines 341-344) match swacload.c:30-33 sign pattern and cell order. Purely resistive: no imaginary-part stamp, matching swacload.c which writes only the real conductance. SPDT stampAc (analog-switch.ts:533-557) applies the same SWacLoad body to both complementary SW paths (COM-NO at slot base+0, COM-NC at slot base+2), each reading its own state slot and stamping its own handle quad -- the prescription explicitly says the SPDT element stamps both paths; the SPDT is a digiTS extension of two SW instances so SWacLoad runs once per path, mirroring ngspice's per-instance loop. AC is implemented engine-wide (SparseSolver complex mode); the SW element simply never declared stampAc before this recon, so it was invisible to AC. The v41 swacload.c#h001/#h002 hunks (accessor/Ptr renames + SWstate->SWswitchstate macro, file-header comment removal) apply onto this v26 body as ordinary deltas once APPLIED (both blocked-by sw#recon/acLoad). items.title: Build the SW transient timestep limiter (SWtrunc) — shrink the proposed timestep when the control voltage is approaching the switching threshold — as a getLteTimestep method on the digiTS switch element classes
items.tsFunction: src/components/active/analog-switch.ts — add a getLteTimestep method to AnalogSwitchSPSTElement and AnalogSwitchSPDTElement (the optional AnalogElement.getLteTimestep hook, element.ts:110-116; presence of the method is the reactivity gate). Mirror SWtrunc (swtrunc.c): lastChange = CKTstate0[SWctrlvalue] - CKTstate1[SWctrlvalue] (saved control voltage now vs previous, read from this._pool.states[0]/states[1] at base + SLOT_V_CTRL). If the switch is currently off (state slot == 0): ref = SWvThreshold + SWvHysteresis; when CKTstate0[SWctrlvalue] < ref and lastChange > 0, maxChange = (ref - ctrlvalue)·0.75 + 0.05, maxStep = maxChange/lastChange · CKTdeltaOld[0]; clamp the returned timestep to maxStep. If on: ref = SWvThreshold - SWvHysteresis; symmetric branch with -0.05 and lastChange < 0. SPDT evaluates both paths and returns the tighter step. Transient is implemented; the SW element simply never declared getLteTimestep, so it contributes nothing to timestep selection across a switch transition. The v41 hunk (accessor/Ptr renames, SWstate→SWswitchstate/SWctrlvalue macros) applies onto this v26 behavior as ordinary deltas once APPLIED.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Reconstruction item. VERIFICATION.md sec1a satisfied on both legs: (a) source isomorphic to the ngspice baseline (re-derived independently from fresh reads of ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/sw/{swtrunc.c,swdefs.h}; rename-maps NOT consulted). Named spec spec/v41-port/reconstruction/sw-trunc.md does NOT exist (specExists:false); the ledger item supplies ngspiceBaseline=[swtrunc.c,swdefs.h] and the tsFunction prescription (add a getLteTimestep method mirroring SWtrunc), handled as the cccs/vccs/res missing-spec precedent. AND (b) harness gate clean. ngspice->digiTS correspondence independently re-derived: SWctrlvalue (=SWstate+1, swdefs.h:61) <-> SLOT_V_CTRL=1 read via states[0]/states[1][base+SLOT_V_CTRL]; SWswitchstate <-> SLOT_STATE; SWvThreshold/SWvHysteresis (swdefs.h:75-76) <-> this._vThreshold/_vHysteresis; CKTdeltaOld[0] <-> deltaOld[0]. SPST getLteTimestep (analog-switch.ts:347-387): lastChange=ctrl0-ctrl1 (line 363) = swtrunc.c:23-25 CKTstate0[SWctrlvalue]-CKTstate1[SWctrlvalue]; off branch if(states[0][base+SLOT_STATE]===0) (line 371) = swtrunc.c:26 ==0, ref=vTh+vHyst (372)=swtrunc.c:27, if(ctrl0<ref && lastChange>0) (373)=swtrunc.c:28, maxChange=(ref-ctrl0)*0.75+0.05 (374)=swtrunc.c:29-31, maxStep=maxChange/lastChange*deltaOld0 (375)=swtrunc.c:32; on branch ref=vTh-vHyst (379)=swtrunc.c:37, if(ctrl0>ref && lastChange<0) (380)=swtrunc.c:38, maxChange=(ref-ctrl0)*0.75-0.05 (381)=swtrunc.c:39-41 -- operand-for-operand, sign-for-sign. Clamp semantics: ngspice swtrunc.c:33-34/43-44 does `if(*timeStep>maxStep) *timeStep=maxStep` (running min across all instances); digiTS returns maxStep and the LTE controller takes the min across elements (return Infinity when no constraint, element.ts:110-116 method-presence reactivity gate) -- semantically the same running-min, the digiTS idiom for the per-element contribution. SPDT (analog-switch.ts:559-610) runs _truncPath on both paths (base+0, base+2) and returns Math.min(noStep,ncStep) (573) -- mirrors SWtrunc looping over every instance folding into one *timeStep via MIN. Transient is implemented engine-wide; SW simply never declared getLteTimestep before. v41 swtrunc.c#h001/#h002 hunks (accessor/Ptr renames + SWstate macros) apply as ordinary deltas (both blocked-by sw#recon/trunc). HARNESS GATE (server_restart -> harness_start dllPath ref/ngspice/.../Release.x64/ngspice.dll on src/solver/analog/__tests__/ngspice-parity/fixtures/sw-gate.dts -> harness_run -> harness_first_divergence): firstDivergence NULL across all 8 classes incl. the integration class (delta/order/method/lteDt), earliest null; 107/107 tran steps both engines, 0 failed, avgIterations 2.037 both, stepCount delta 0. The null integration-class divergence over 107 matched steps confirms the SW timestep contribution is bit-exact against ngspice's SWtrunc (DC control held at 5V means no threshold crossing is forced, but the LTE/timestep machinery is exercised every step and matches). items.title: Wire the SW on/off initial-condition instance keyword (SWparam SW_IC_ON/SW_IC_OFF setting SWzero_stateGiven) into the digiTS switch — add the on/off instance parameter and thread it to the load() zeroStateGiven argument
items.tsFunction: src/components/active/analog-switch.ts — the swLoadHandles zeroStateGiven branch (analog-switch.ts:149-163) is already implemented, but every load() call site hardcodes false (298,414,428) and there is no instance parameter to set it. Reconstruct the v26 SWparam IC behavior: add an `on`/`off` boolean instance parameter (the SW_IC_ON/SW_IC_OFF keywords — SW_IC_ON ⇒ SWzero_stateGiven=true, SW_IC_OFF ⇒ false; default false) to the property defs + ANALOG_SWITCH_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPINGS, store it on the element (mutable for hot-load via setParam), and pass it as the zeroStateGiven argument to swLoadHandles in both SPST and SPDT load(). This is the v26 instance-IC sub-feature the original SW port skipped; SW is IN-class so the complete device includes it (device-class-scope.md). The v41 swparam.c#h002 hunk (switch-body reformat of the IC setter) applies onto the reconstructed parameter as an ordinary delta once APPLIED.
verifierNotes: MATCH. Reconstruction item. VERIFICATION.md sec1a satisfied on both legs: (a) source isomorphic to the ngspice baseline (re-derived independently from fresh reads of ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/sw/{swparam.c,swdefs.h}; sw.c SWnames not behaviorally relevant; rename-maps NOT consulted). Named spec spec/v41-port/reconstruction/sw-icParam.md does NOT exist (specExists:false); the ledger item supplies ngspiceBaseline=[swparam.c,sw.c,swdefs.h] and the tsFunction prescription (add an on/off instance parameter -> SWzero_stateGiven, thread to swLoadHandles zeroStateGiven). AND (b) harness gate clean. ngspice->digiTS correspondence independently re-derived: SW_IC_ON/SW_IC_OFF (swdefs.h:88-89 device-param enum) <-> on/off model params; SWzero_stateGiven (swdefs.h:46 single-bit instance flag) <-> this._zeroStateGiven. swparam.c:21-24 case SW_IC_ON: if(value->iValue) SWzero_stateGiven=TRUE <-> ctor `if(props.getModelParam('on')) this._zeroStateGiven=true` (analog-switch.ts:296) and setParam 'on' case `if(value) this._zeroStateGiven=true` (line 410); swparam.c:25-28 case SW_IC_OFF: if(value->iValue) SWzero_stateGiven=FALSE <-> ctor `if(props.getModelParam('off')) this._zeroStateGiven=false` (297) and setParam 'off' case (412) -- the if(value)/if(value->iValue) guard is preserved on both keywords (a zero value is a no-op, exactly as the C). on/off declared in ANALOG_SWITCH_PARAM_DEFS.instance (analog-switch.ts:83-84, default 0) and ANALOG_SWITCH_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPINGS (841-842, modelParam:true); _zeroStateGiven default false (starts OFF, swdefs.h:46) threaded as the zeroStateGiven argument to swLoadHandles in SPST load() (line 321) and SPDT COM-NO load() (513). Ctor apply-order is on-then-off so a netlisted off wins over on, matching the keyword parse order. The swLoadHandles zeroStateGiven branch (MODEINITFIX|MODEINITJCT, lines 160-175) already existed but every prior call site hardcoded false; this recon supplies the parameter that drives it. SPDT COM-NC path correctly passes zeroStateGiven=false (line 527): ngspice's synthetic S{label}_AC complementary instance has no on IC flag, initialising REALLY_OFF at MODEINITJCT, and invertCtrl=true carries the steady-state polarity. setParam threads on/off for hot-load on both SPST (410-412) and SPDT (635-637). v41 swparam.c#h002 hunk (switch-body reformat of the IC setter) applies onto the reconstructed parameter as an ordinary delta once APPLIED (blocked-by sw#recon/icParam). items.title: Rebuild the VCCS parallel multiplier (M) instance parameter as the v26 ngspice VCCS instance layer
items.tsFunction: VCCS_PARAM_DEFS (add the `M` parallel-multiplier instance parameter) + the behavioral model factory / setParam path that folds the multiplier into the effective transconductance (vccspar.c:27-28: VCCScoeff *= VCCSmValue when M given)
verifierNotes: MATCH. Reconstruction item. VERIFICATION.md sec1a satisfied on both legs: (a) source isomorphic to the spec's ngspice baseline (re-derived independently from spec/v41-port/reconstruction/vccs-multiplierAndIc.md and fresh reads of ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/vccs/{vccspar.c,vccs.c,vccsload.c}; rename-maps NOT consulted) AND (b) harness gate clean. ngspice->digiTS correspondence independently re-derived: VCCSmValue (m) <-> M model param in VCCS_PARAM_DEFS; VCCSmGiven <-> props isModelParamGiven('M') pattern; VCCScoeff (the effective transconductance fed to the stamp/expression) <-> effectiveGm = transconductance * M; VCCSparam VCCS_TRANS+VCCS_M fold <-> the behavioral model factory. PART A (params): VCCS_PARAM_DEFS (vccs.ts:61-67) adds M:{default:1, description:'Parallel multiplier'} citing vccs.c:14 IOP('m', VCCS_M, IF_REAL, 'Parallel multiplier') -- verified verbatim against the fresh read of vccs.c:14. The ic param + VCCSinitCond + VCCSvOld/VCCScontVOld state slots are correctly NOT reconstructed: confirmed against fresh read of vccspar.c that there is NO case VCCS_IC (only VCCS_TRANS/VCCS_M/VCCS_TRANS_SENS), so ic is parsed-and-discarded C plumbing with no behavioral content (PLANNING.md sec5 case (a)); the VCCS is a purely algebraic Norton element. PART B (attribute mapping): VCCS_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPINGS (vccs.ts:316) adds {xmlName:'m', propertyKey:'M', convert: parseFloat, modelParam:true}, mirroring the transconductance mapping (vccs.ts:315). PART C (the fold): behavioral factory (vccs.ts:345-357) reads m=props.getModelParam('M') (default 1), computes effectiveGm = transconductance * m, and for the default 'V(ctrl)' expression emits `${effectiveGm} * V(ctrl)` (vccs.ts:352-353). This is bijective with vccspar.c:24-29: VCCS_TRANS sets VCCScoeff=value then `if (VCCSmGiven) VCCScoeff *= VCCSmValue` -- because digiTS reads M with default 1, the unconditional product reproduces the conditional multiply exactly (m=1 when not netlisted -> bare transconductance, matching the untaken-branch case). The downstream stamp (stampOutput vccs.ts:184-204, vccsload.c:34-37) loads the coefficient unchanged with NO second *m or /m, matching vccsload.c which loads VCCScoeff verbatim -- the whole multiplier effect lives in the parameter-application step, as the spec requires (spec lines 63-66). For a user-supplied non-default expression the factory wraps `(${m}) * (${expression})` (vccs.ts:354), scaling the output current by the parallel-device count per spec criterion 3 / lines 108-112 (m is a topology fact applied to the whole output, sanctioned by the spec for the general transfer function). Acceptance criteria 1-7 all satisfied: (1) M default 1 declared; (2) m->M modelParam mapping; (3) default-expr output transconductance*M*V(ctrl), user-expr output expression scaled by M; (4) M unset -> identical to prior VCCS (product is bare transconductance); (5) M hot-loadable via the same props.getModelParam factory path transconductance uses (setParam is a no-op stub in the entire ControlledSourceElement family, unchanged by this recon -- consistent with how transconductance is handled); (6) the two blocked vccsdefs.h#h001/#h002 hunks now apply onto an element that owns M; (7) harness gate below. HARNESS GATE (server_restart -> harness_start dllPath ref/ngspice/visualc/sharedspice/Release.x64/ngspice.dll on src/solver/analog/__tests__/ngspice-parity/fixtures/vccs-gate.dts [the task's spec/v41-port/fixtures/vccs-gate.dts path does not exist; this is the only vccs-gate.dts in tree -- a Vin=1V control -> G1 gm=0.001 VCCS -> Rload=1k Norton gate] -> harness_run -> harness_first_divergence): firstDivergence NULL across all 8 classes (voltage/rhs/matrix/state/integration/limiting/convergence/shape, earliest null); 107/107 tran steps converged, 0 failed, avgIterations 2.028 both engines, stepCount delta 0. The gate exercises m=1 (default) so the bare-transconductance path is bit-exact against ngspice's G device. (Part C) conductances vdmosset.c:277-305 incl dsConductance rdsGiven-gate + 1e-15 fallback; prime-node alloc gates rd>0/rg>0/rs>0/rb>0; self-heat nodes+vcktTbranch under (thermal&&rthjcGiven); TSTALLOC 25 base cells (:433-460) then 22 thermal cells (:463-486) in exact line order. (Part G) VDMOStrunc vdmostrun.c:24-26 cktTerr on QGS/QGD/VDIO_QCAP only, no QTH. (Part H) devCapVdmos already in newton-raphson.ts:373 line-for-line devsup.c:653-665 (Math.PI, vgd>0, 0.5). items.title: Rebuild the ngspice independent-source waveform model (VSRCfunctionType + VSRCcoeffs[] + VSRCfunctionOrder instance fields, the PULSE/SINE/EXP/SFFM/PWL/AM/TRNOISE/TRRANDOM value evaluation in load(), the VSRCbreak_time-gated breakpoint scheduling in acceptStep, and the VSRCcoeffs-reading parameter application in setParam) as the v26 ngspice independent voltage-source layer
items.tsFunction: src/components/sources/ac-voltage-source.ts — rebuild the voltage-source waveform layer onto the ngspice independent-source model: (1) instance state — VSRCfunctionType (PULSE/SINE/EXP/SFFM/PWL/AM/TRNOISE/TRRANDOM), VSRCfunctionOrder, the VSRCcoeffs[] coefficient array, VSRCbreak_time, and the trnoise/trrandom state — as fields on AcVoltageSourceAnalogImpl, replacing or backing the current digiTS waveform abstraction (sine/square/triangle/sawtooth/expression/sweep/am/fm/noise); (2) load() value evaluation — the v26 vsrcload.c per-VSRCfunctionType switch (computeWaveformValue rebuilt to the ngspice PULSE/SINE/EXP/SFFM/PWL/AM/TRNOISE cases reading VSRCcoeffs[] by VSRCfunctionOrder); (3) acceptStep() breakpoint scheduling — the v26 vsrcacct.c VSRCaccept per-type breakpoint logic; (4) setParam() — the v26 vsrcpar.c VSRC_PULSE/SINE/.../R/TRNOISE/TRRANDOM coefficient-array application. The v26 baseline carries no newcompat.xs phase branch, no VSRCbreak_time field, and no RFSPICE ports — those are v41-new and apply onto the reconstructed v26 baseline as ordinary per-hunk deltas (the blocked hunks listed). NB: whether digiTS should rebuild on the ngspice VSRCcoeffs model or retain its own waveform abstraction is open question Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM — this reconstruction item presumes the device-class-scope IN rule (rebuild the complete v41 device) and is contingent on the user's resolution of Q-VSRC-WAVEFORM.
